1.1: Blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked, and in the path of sinners does not stand, and in the seat of those mocking does not sit.
1.2: But rather his delight is in the law of YHWH, and in His law he meditates day and night.
1.3: And he is like a tree planted by streams of water, which gives its fruit in its time; and its leaf shall not wither, and all that he does, he makes prosper.
1.4: Not so the wicked! But rather they are like the chaff, which the wind drives away.
1.5: Upon thus the wicked shall not stand in the judgment, nor the sinful ones in the assembly of the righteous.
1.6: For YHWH knows the way of the righteous, and the way of the wicked shall perish.

2.1: Why do the nations rage, and the peoples plot in vain?
2.2: The kings of the earth take their stand, and the rulers establish themselves together against YHWH and against His Anointed:
2.3: “Let us tear off their bonds, and cast away from us their cords.”
2.4: The One sitting in the heavens laughs; the Lord scoffs at them.
2.5: Then He speaks to them in His anger, and in his fury He terrifies them:
2.6: “And I have installed My King on Zion, the mountain of My holiness.”
2.7: I will recount the decree of YHWH; He has said to Me: “You are My Son; today I have begotten You.
2.8: Ask from Me and I will give the nations as Your inheritance, and the ends of the earth for Your possession.
2.9: You will break them with a rod of iron; You will shatter them like a vessel of a potter.”
2.10: And now, O kings, be prudent; be admonished, O judges of the earth.
2.11: Serve YHWH with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
2.12: Kiss the Son, lest He be angry, and you? perish in the way, when His wrath is kindled in an instant. Blessed are all those taking refuge in Him.

3.1: A Psalm of David, in his fleeing from the face of Absalom his son. O YHWH, how many are my adversaries! Many are rising up against me!
3.2: Many are saying of my soul, “There is no salvation for him in God.” Selah
3.3: and You, O YHWH, are a shield behind me, my glory, and One lifting high my head.
3.4: With my voice I cry to YHWH, and He answers me from the mountain of His holiness. Selah
3.5: I lie down and sleep; I awake, for YHWH sustains me.
3.6: I shall not fear the multitudes of people, who have set themselves all around against me.
3.7: Arise, O YHWH! Save me, O my God! O that You would strike all my enemies on the jaw; may you break the teeth of the wicked!
3.8: Unto YHWH is the salvation; may Your blessing be upon Your people. Selah

4.1: For the one directing. With stringed instruments. A Psalm of David. In my calling out, may You answer me, O God of my righteousness! In my distress, You widened it for me; show me favor and hear my prayer.
4.2: O sons of men, until when will my glory be shame? Will you? love vanity, and seek after falsehood? Selah
4.3: and know that YHWH has marked out the faithful one for Himself; YHWH hears in my calling to Him.
4.4: Tremble and do not sin; speak in your? heart on your? bed, and be silent. Selah
4.5: Sacrifice the sacrifices of righteousness, and trust in YHWH.
4.6: Many are saying, “Who will show us good?” Lift upon us the light of Your face, O YHWH.
4.7: You have put joy in my heart, more than the time when their grain and their new wine have increased.
4.8: In peace I will both lie down and will sleep, for You alone, O YHWH, cause me to dwell in safety.

5.1: For the one directing. With the Nehiloth. A Psalm of David. Give ear to my words, O YHWH; have regard for my murmuring.
5.2: Be attentive to the voice of my cry, O my King and my God, for to You I pray.
5.3: O YHWH, in the morning You hear my voice; in the morning I direct it to You, and I keep watch.
5.4: For You are not a God delighting in wickedness; evil shall not sojourn with You.
5.5: The one boasting shall not stand in Your sight; You hate all workers of iniquity.
5.6: You will destroy those speaking falsehood; a man of blood and deceit YHWH abhors.
5.7: But I, in the abundance of Your loving devotion, will come into Your house; I will bow down toward the temple of Your Holiness in fear of You.
5.8: O YHWH, guide me in Your righteousness, on account of my enemies; make straight Your path before my face.
5.9: For there is no firmness in his mouth; within them is destruction; their throat is a grave being wide open; they make their tongue smooth.
5.10: Declare them guilty, O God; let them fall by their own devices; in the abundance of their transgressions drive them away, for they have rebelled against You.
5.11: And let all those taking refuge in You rejoice forever; let them shout for joy, and may You cover them over; and let those loving Your name exult in You.
5.12: For You will bless the righteous one, O YHWH; as a shield You will surround him with favor.

6.1: For the one directing. With stringed instruments according to Sheminith. A Psalm of David. O YHWH, in Your anger do not rebuke me, and do not discipline me in Your rage.
6.2: Be gracious to me, O YHWH, for I am weak; heal me, O YHWH, for my bones are disturbed.
6.3: And my soul is disturbed greatly; and You, O YHWH, until when?
6.4: Return, O YHWH, draw out my soul; save me for the sake of Your loving devotion.
6.5: For in death is no remembrance of You; in Sheol, who can give thanks to You?
6.6: I have grown weary in my sighing; I cause my bed to swim through all the night; with my tears I melt my couch.
6.7: My eye has wasted away from grief; it grows old because of all afflicting me.
6.8: Turn away from me, all workers of iniquity, for YHWH has heard the voice of my weeping.
6.9: YHWH has heard my supplication; YHWH has received my prayer.
6.10: Let all my enemies be ashamed and disturbed greatly; let them turn back and be ashamed in an instant.

7.1: A Shiggaion of David, which he sang to YHWH, concerning the words of Cush, the Benjamite. O YHWH my God, in You I have taken refuge; keep me safe from all those pursuing me, and deliver me,
7.2: lest they tear my soul like a lion, tearing apart, and there is no one snatching me up.
7.3: O YHWH my God, if I have done this, if there is injustice in my palms,
7.4: if I have dealt with the one at peace with me with evil, or have drawn out my enemy without cause,
7.5: may the enemy pursue my soul and overtake it, and let him trample my life to the ground, and let him set down my honor in the dust. Selah
7.6: arise, O YHWH, in Your anger; lift up against the fury of my adversaries, and rouse Yourself for me; You have appointed a judgment.
7.7: And let the congregation of peoples surround You; and over it return in the heights.
7.8: YHWH shall judge the peoples; Judge me, O YHWH, according to my righteousness, and according to my integrity within me.
7.9: Please, let the evil of the wicked cease, and may you establish the righteous—even the One testing hearts and kidneys, O Righteous God.
7.10: My shield is on God, the One saving the upright of heart.
7.11: God is a righteous judge, and a God expressing wrath on every day.
7.12: If one shall not turn back, then He sharpens His sword; He has bent His bow and prepared it .
7.13: And for Himself He has prepared instruments of death; His arrows He makes into fiery shafts.
7.14: Behold, he travails with evil, and he conceives trouble, and brings forth falsehood.
7.15: He has dug a pit and hollowed it out, and he has fallen into a pit he has made.
7.16: His trouble shall return upon his head, and on the top of his head, his violence shall come down.
7.17: I will confess YHWH according to His righteousness, and I will sing praise to the name of YHWH-Elyon.

8.1: For the one directing. According to Gittith. A Psalm of David. O YHWH our Lord, how majestic is Your name in all the earth, who have set Your splendor above the heavens!
8.2: From the mouth of infants and sucklings You have established strength, on account of Your adversaries, to still the enemy and avenger.
8.3: When I see Your heavens, the works of Your fingers, the moon and the stars that You have established—
8.4: what is man that You should remember him, and the son of man, that You would attend to him?
8.5: And You made him lacking a little from the angels, and with glory and honor You crowned him.
8.6: You made him to rule over the works of Your hands; You have placed everything under his feet:
8.7: All sheep and cattle, and also the beasts of the field,
8.8: the birds of the heavens and the fish of the sea, those passing on the paths of the seas.
8.9: O YHWH, our Lord, how majestic is Your name in all the earth!

9.1: For the one directing. According to Muth-Labben. A Psalm of David. I will confess YHWH with all of my heart; I will recount all Your wonders.
9.2: I will be glad and rejoice in You; I will sing praise to Your name, O Most High.
9.3: In turning back my enemies they shall stumble and shall perish from Your face.
9.4: For You have done my justice and my claim; You have sat on the throne, judging with righteousness.
9.5: You have rebuked the nations; You have destroyed the wicked; their name You have wiped out forever and ever.
9.6: May the enemy be finished in ruins forever, and You have uprooted cities; let their remembrance perish with them!
9.7: And YHWH shall sit forever; He has established His throne for the judgment.
9.8: And He will judge the world in righteousness; He shall make judgment for the peoples in uprightness.
9.9: And YHWH will be a secure height for the oppressed, a secure height in times of distress.
9.10: And they will put their trust in You—those knowing Your name—for You have not forsaken those seeking You, O YHWH.
9.11: Sing praise to YHWH, the One sitting in Zion; declare His deeds among the peoples.
9.12: For the One seeking out the blood remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
9.13: Show favor to me, O YHWH; see my affliction from those hating me; raise me high from the gates of death,
9.14: so that I may recount all Your praises in the gates of the daughter of Zion. I will rejoice in Your salvation.
9.15: The nations have sunk down into the pit they have made; in the net that they hid, their foot is caught.
9.16: YHWH is known by the justice He has done; by the work of his own hands the wicked one is snared. Higgaion Selah
9.17: the wicked shall return to Sheol—all the nations forgetting God.
9.18: For the needy will not be forgotten forever, nor the hope of the poor perish to the end .
9.19: Arise, O YHWH! Do not let man prevail; let the nations be judged before Your face.
9.20: Put fear on them, O YHWH; let the nations know they are mortal. Selah

10.1: Why, O YHWH, do You stand far off? Do You hide in times of distress?
10.2: In his arrogance, the wicked hotly pursue the afflicted; may they be caught in the schemes that they have devised!
10.3: For the wicked one boasts over the desire of his heart, and he blesses a dishonest gainer; he has despised YHWH.
10.4: The wicked one, in the height of his face, does not inquire; there is no God in any of his thoughts.
10.5: His paths are firm at every time; Your judgments are on high before him, and all his adversaries, he sneers at them.
10.6: He has said in his heart, “I shall not be moved—from generation to generation—to whom will be no adversity.”
10.7: His mouth is full of cursing and deceit and oppression; under his tongue are trouble and iniquity.
10.8: He sits in an ambush of the villages; in the hidden places he kills the innocent one; his eyes look stealthily on the helpless.
10.9: He lies in wait in the hidden place, like a lion in his lair; he lies in wait to catch the afflicted one; he catches the afflicted one by drawing him into his net.
10.10: He is crushed; he bows down, and the hapless one falls by his strength.
10.11: He has said in his heart, “God has forgotten; He hides His face; He will not see it forever.”
10.12: Arise, O YHWH! O God, lift up Your hand! You must not forget the afflicted ones.
10.13: Upon what does the wicked spurn God? He has said in his heart, “You will not call me to account.”
10.14: Indeed you You have seen; You regard trouble and grief, to give it into Your hand. The hapless one leaves himself to You; for You are the One helping the fatherless.
10.15: Break the arm of the wicked and evildoer! Seek out his wickedness, until none is found.
10.16: YHWH is King forever and ever; the nations have perished from His land.
10.17: The desire of the afflicted ones You have heard, O YHWH; You will make firm their hearts; You will incline Your ear,
10.18: to do justice for the fatherless and oppressed; he shall not continue anymore to terrorize—the man from the earth,

11.1: For the one directing. Of David. In YHWH I have taken refuge. How can you? say to my soul, “Flee to your? mountain a bird!
11.2: For, behold, the wicked tread the bow; they make ready their arrow upon the string, to shoot in the darkness at those upright in heart.
11.3: If the foundations are thrown down, the righteous—what can he do?”
11.4: YHWH is is in His holy temple; YHWH—in the heavens is His throne. His eyes are watching, His eyelids examine the sons of man.
11.5: YHWH tests the righteous, and His soul hates the wicked one and the lover of violence.
11.6: May He rain down coals upon the wicked, fire and brimstone; and a burning wind is the portion of their cup.
11.7: For YHWH is righteous; He loves righteousness; the upright one will see His face.

12.1: For the one directing. According to Sheminith. A Psalm of David. Help, O YHWH! For the godly one ceases to be ; for the faithful ones disappear from the sons of man.
12.2: Vanity they speak, each man with his neighbor; with lips of flattery with a double heart they speak.
12.3: May YHWH cut off all lips of flattery, a tongue speaking great things —
12.4: those who have said, “By our tongues we will prevail; our lips are with us; who is lord over us?”
12.5: “From the oppression of the poor, from the groaning of the needy, now I shall arise,” says YHWH. “I will set in safety him who breathes for it.”
12.6: The words of YHWH are pure words, silver refined in a furnace of earth, purified sevenfold.
12.7: You, O YHWH, shall keep them; You will guard them from this generation forever.
12.8: On every side the wicked walk, as vileness is exalted by the sons of man.

13.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. Until when, O YHWH? Will You forget me forever? Until when will You hide Your face from me?
13.2: Until when shall I set counsel in my soul, and sorrow in my heart daily? Until when will my enemy be raised high over me?
13.3: Look attentively; answer me, O YHWH my God! Give light to my eyes, lest I sleep in death,
13.4: lest my enemy say, “I have prevailed against him.” My adversaries rejoice because I am shaken.
13.5: Yet I have trusted in Your loving devotion; My heart will rejoice in Your salvation.
13.6: I will sing to YHWH, because He has dealt bountifully with me.

14.1: For the one directing. Of David. The fool says in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt; they have done abominable deeds; there is no one doing good.
14.2: YHWH looks down from the heavens upon the sons of man, to see, is there one understanding, seeking God?
14.3: All have turned aside, together they have become corrupt; there is no one doing good, not even one.
14.4: All those working iniquity, have they not know? They devour my people they have eaten bread; on YHWH they do not call.
14.5: There they tremble with dread, for God is in the generation of the righteous.
14.6: The counsel of the poor you? have shamed, for YHWH is his place of refuge.
14.7: Who gives from Zion the salvation of Israel? In YHWH turning back the captivity of His people, let Jacob rejoice; let Israel be glad!

15.1: A Psalm of David. O YHWH, who may sojourn in Your tent? Who may dwell in the mountain of Your Holiness?
15.2: The one walking blamelessly, and doing righteousness, and speaking truth in his heart.
15.3: He does not have slander upon his tongue, and he has not done evil to his neighbor, and a reproach he has not taken up against his neighbor.
15.4: A rejected one is despised in his eyes, and those fearful of YHWH he honors—one who swears to endure evil, and not changing.
15.5: His silver he has not given in usury, and he does not take a bribe against the innocent. The one doing these things is not shaken forever.

16.1: A Miktam of David. Preserve me, O God, for I take refuge in You.
16.2: I said to YHWH, “You are my Lord. My goodness is nothing apart from You.”
16.3: As for the holy ones who are in the land, they and the excellent ones, all my delight is in them.
16.4: May their sorrows be multiplied, those who hurry after another! I will not pour out their drink offerings of blood, and I will not lift up their names upon my lips.
16.5: YHWH, is the portion of my share, and my cup; You are the One upholding my lot.
16.6: The lines have fallen to me in the pleasant places ; indeed, a pleasing inheritance is unto me.
16.7: I will bless YHWH, who counsels me; even in the nights, my kidneys instruct me.
16.8: I have set YHWH before me continually; because He is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.
16.9: Therefore my heart is glad and my glory rejoices; my flesh also will dwell securely.
16.10: For You will not abandon my soul to Sheol, and You will not give Your Holy one to see corruption.
16.11: You make known to me the path of life; fullness of joys is with Your face, pleasant things are at Your right hand forever.

17.1: A Prayer of David. Hear, O YHWH, a just cause; attend to my cry; give ear to my prayer without lips of deceit.
17.2: From before Your face, let my judgment come forth; may Your eyes see uprightness.
17.3: You have tested my heart; You have inspected in the night. You have refined me without Your finding, for I have purposed that my mouth shall not transgress.
17.4: As for the works of men, by the word of Your lips I have kept myself from the ways of a violent one.
17.5: To uphold my steps on Your paths, my footsteps are not shaken.
17.6: I call on You, for You will answer me, O God! Incline Your ear to me; hear my word.
17.7: Set apart Your loving devotion, You who save those taking refuge from the assailants—with Your right hand.
17.8: May you guard me as the pupil, the daughter of Your eye; hide me in the shadow of Your wings,
17.9: from wicked ones who would ruin me, my enemies in soul go around against me.
17.10: Their fat they have closed up; their mouths they speak with pride.
17.11: In our steps now they have surrounded us; they have set their eyes to cast us to the ground.
17.12: His likeness is as a lion longing to tear, and like a young lion lurking in hidden places.
17.13: Arise, O YHWH! Go before his face; cast him down, and rescue my soul from the wicked by your sword,
17.14: from men by Your hand, O YHWH, from men of the world—their portion is in this life. And Your treasured ones, You fill their belly; they are satisfied with sons, and they leave their remainder to their children.
17.15: I, in righteousness, will see Your face; in my waking, I will be satisfied with Your form.

18.1: For the one directing. Of the servant of YHWH, David, who spoke to YHWH the words of this song on the day that YHWH snatched him from the hand of all his enemies, from the hand of Saul. And he said: I love You, O YHWH, my strength.
18.2: YHWH is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer. My God is my rock—I take refuge in Him—my protection, and the horn of my salvation, my high place.
18.3: I will call upon YHWH, to be praised, and I shall be saved from my enemies.
18.4: The cords of Death surrounded me, and the torrents of Belial terrified me.
18.5: The cords of Sheol surrounded me; the snares of Death confronted me.
18.6: In the distress unto me, I called to YHWH, and to my God I cried for help. From His temple He heard my voice, and my cry for help came before His face into His ears.
18.7: And the earth shook and quaked, and the foundations of the hills trembled; and they were shaken because of the burning from Him.
18.8: Smoke went up from His nostril, and a devouring fire from His mouth; coals were kindled from it.
18.9: And He stretched apart the heavens and came down, and a dark cloud was under His feet.
18.10: And He rode upon a cherub and flew, and He took flight upon the wings of the wind.
18.11: He made darkness His covering, His canopy around Him—darkness of waters and thick clouds of vapors.
18.12: From the brightness before Him His thick clouds passed on, hail and coals of fire.
18.13: And YHWH thundered in the heavens, and the Most High gave forth His voice—hail and coals of fire.
18.14: And He sent out His arrows and scattered them, and He hurled lightning and routed them.
18.15: And the channels of the sea were seen. And the foundations of the world were uncovered, from Your rebuke, O YHWH, from the blast of the breath of Your nostril.
18.16: He sent from above, he took hold of me; He drew me out from the many waters.
18.17: He snatched me away from my strong enemy, and from those hating me, for they were stronger than I.
18.18: They confronted me in the day of my calamity, and YHWH was as a support for me.
18.19: And He brought me out into a broad place; He pulled me out, because He took delight in me.
18.20: YHWH rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands, He returned it to me.
18.21: For I have kept the ways of YHWH, and I have not acted wickedly against my God.
18.22: For all His ordinances are before me, and I did not turn aside His statutes from me.
18.23: And I was blameless before Him, and I guarded myself from my iniquity.
18.24: And YHWH has returned to me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands before His eyes.
18.25: With the faithful one, You show Yourself faithful; with a blameless man, You show Yourself blameless;
18.26: with the purified one, You show Yourself pure; and with the crooked one, You show Yourself shrewd.
18.27: For You will save a lowly people, but the eyes of those rising high You bring low.
18.28: For You light my lamp; YHWH my God makes my darkness shine bright.
18.29: For with You I can run against a troop, and with my God I can leap a wall.
18.30: God—His way is perfect—the word of YHWH is refined; He is a shield for all those taking refuge in Him.
18.31: For who is God, apart from YHWH? And who is the Rock, except our God?—
18.32: The God girding me with strength, and making perfect my way.
18.33: Setting my feet like does, even on the heights He makes me stand.
18.34: He trains my hands for the battle, and my arms can bend down a bow of bronze.
18.35: And You gave to me the shield of Your salvation; and Your right hand supports me, and Your gentleness makes me great.
18.36: You made wide my path under me, and my ankles have not slipped.
18.37: I pursued my enemies and overtook them; and I did not turn back until they were consumed.
18.38: I wounded them, and they were not able to rise; they have fallen under my feet.
18.39: And You girded me with strength for the battle; those rising against me You bent down underneath me.
18.40: And my enemies, You have given to me the neck; and those hating me, I cut them off!
18.41: They cried for help, and no one saved them, upon YHWH, and He did not answer them.
18.42: And I beat them as dust upon the face of the wind; like mud in the streets I trampled them.
18.43: You rescued me from the disputes of people; You set me as the head of nations; a people I have not known shall serve me.
18.44: At the hearing of the ear, they listen to me; the sons of a foreigner feign obedience before me.
18.45: The sons of a foreigner fade away, and they come trembling from their strongholds.
18.46: YHWH lives! And blessed be my Rock, and the God of my salvation be exalted—
18.47: the God giving vengeance to me, and subduing the peoples under me,
18.48: causing me to escape from my enemies, even above, those who rise against me—You lift me up. From the man of violence, You have snatched me.
18.49: Upon thus, I will confess You among the nations, O YHWH, and to Your name I will sing praise.
18.50: He is making great the salvation of His king and showing loving devotion to His anointed, of David and to his seed, even to forever.

19.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. The heavens are recounting the glory of God, and the firmament is declaring the work of and His hands.
19.2: Day after day it pours forth speech, and night after night it shows forth knowledge.
19.3: Without speech and without words, their voice not being heard,
19.4: their voice has gone out into all the earth, and their words to the end of the world. He has placed a tent in them for the sun,
19.5: and it is like a bridegroom going forth from his chamber, rejoicing like a strong man to run the path.
19.6: From one end of the heavens is his going forth, and his circuit is upon their ends, and there is nothing being hidden from his heat.
19.7: The law of YHWH is perfect, refreshing the soul; the testimony of YHWH is sure, making wise the simple.
19.8: The statutes of YHWH are straight, making glad the heart; the commandment of YHWH is pure, bringing light to the eyes.
19.9: The fear of YHWH is pure, standing forever; the judgments of YHWH are true, being righteous altogether,
19.10: being desired above gold, and much above pure gold, and sweeter than honey and flowing honey from the honeycomb.
19.11: Indeed, Your servant is warned by them; in keeping them is great reward.
19.12: Who can discern his errors? From the hidden cleanse me.
19.13: Also, hold back Your servant from proud things ; may they not rule over me. Then I shall be complete, and cleansed of great transgression.
19.14: The words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart, let them be for favor before Your face, O YHWH, my Rock and my Redeemer.

20.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. May YHWH answer you in the day of distress; may the name of the God of Jacob set you on high.
20.2: May He send help for you from the Holy Place, and from Zion may He support you.
20.3: May He remember all your gifts, and anoint your burnt offering. Selah
20.4: may He give to you according to your heart, and fulfill all your counsel.
20.5: We will rejoice in your salvation, and we will raise a banner in the name of our God; may YHWH fulfill all your petitions.
20.6: Now I know that YHWH brings salvation for His anointed; He will answer him from His holy heaven, with the saving might of His right hand.
20.7: These in the chariots, and these in the horses, but we, in the name of YHWH our God, will remember.
20.8: They have bowed down and fallen, and we have risen and stand upright.
20.9: O YHWH, save the king! Answer us on the day we call.

21.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. O YHWH, in Your strength the king rejoices! And in Your salvation, how greatly he exults!
21.2: The desire of his heart You have given to him, and the request of his lips you have not withheld. Selah
21.3: for You put before him the blessings of goodness; You set upon his head a crown of pure gold.
21.4: Life he has asked from You, and You gave it to him—length of days, forever and ever.
21.5: Great is his glory in Your salvation; splendor and majesty You have placed upon him.
21.6: For You set for him blessings forever; You make him rejoice with gladness before Your face.
21.7: For the king trusts in YHWH, and in the loving devotion of the Most High he shall not be shaken.
21.8: Your hand will find all Your enemies; Your right hand will find those hating You.
21.9: You will place them as in a furnace of fire at the time of Your presence; YHWH in His anger will swallow them up, and the fire will devour them.
21.10: Their fruit You will destroy from the earth, and their seed from among the sons of men.
21.11: For they stretched out against you for evil. They devised a plot; they will not prevail.
21.12: For You set them to the shoulder; You prepare Your bowstrings against their faces.
21.13: Be exalted, O YHWH, in Your strength; we will sing and praise Your might.

22.1: For the one directing. According to “The Doe of the Dawn.” A Psalm of David. My God, my God, why have You forsaken me, far away from my salvation, from the words of my roaring?
22.2: O my God, I cry out by day, and You do not answer, and by night, and there is no quiet for me.
22.3: And You are holy, sitting amid the praises of Israel.
22.4: In You our fathers trusted; they trusted, and You delivered them.
22.5: To You they cried, and they were given escape; in You they trusted, and they were not put to shame.
22.6: And I am a worm, and not a man, the scorn of men, and despised by the people.
22.7: All seeing me mock at me; they gape with the lip; they shake the head:
22.8: “Roll to YHWH; let Him deliver him and snatch him away, if indeed He delights in him.”
22.9: Yet You brought me forth from the womb; You made me trust upon the breasts of my mother.
22.10: Upon You I was cast forth from the womb; from the belly of my mother, You are my God.
22.11: You should not be far from me, for distress is near, and because there is no one to help.
22.12: Many strong bulls have surrounded me, mighty ones of Bashan have encircled Me.
22.13: They open against me their mouths, a lion tearing and roaring.
22.14: I am poured out like water, and all of my bones are separated; my heart has become like wax, and has melted in the midst of my bowels.
22.15: My strength is dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue clings to my jaws; and in the dust of death You lay me.
22.16: For dogs have surrounded me; the swarm of the wicked has encircled me, piercing my hands and my feet.
22.17: I can count all my bones; they glare; they look at me.
22.18: They divide out my garments for themselves, and over my clothing they cast lots.
22.19: But You, O YHWH, do not be far away! O my strength, may You hurry to help me.
22.20: Snatch my soul from the sword, my only one from the hand of the dog!
22.21: Save me from the mouth of the lion, and from the horns of the wild oxen answer me.
22.22: I will declare Your name to my brothers; in the midst of the assembly I will praise You.
22.23: Those fearing YHWH, praise Him! All the seed of Jacob, honor Him! And fear Him, all the seed of Israel!
22.24: For He has not despised and has not detested the affliction of the afflicted, and He has not hidden His face from him, and in his crying out to Him, He has heard.
22.25: From You is my praise in the great assembly; my vows I will complete in front of those who fear Him.
22.26: The afflicted ones shall eat and be satisfied; those seeking Him shall praise YHWH. May your? hearts live forever!
22.27: They will remember and will turn to YHWH, all the ends of the earth; and before Your face they will bow down, all the clans of the nations.
22.28: For unto YHWH is the kingship, and He rules over the nations.
22.29: All the fat ones of the earth shall eat and bow low; before His face will bow all those going down to the dust, even the one unable able to keep alive his soul.
22.30: A seed shall serve Him; it will be recounted of the Lord to the generation.
22.31: They shall come and declare His righteousness to a people to be born, that which He has done!

23.1: A Psalm of David. YHWH is my shepherd; I shall not lack.
23.2: In pastures green He makes me to lie down; beside waters of resting He guides me.
23.3: He restores my soul; He guides me in the paths of righteousness, for the sake of His name.
23.4: Even though I may walk in the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for You are with me; Your rod and Your staff, they comfort me.
23.5: You arrange a table before me in front of my enemies; You anoint my head with the oil; my cup overflows.
23.6: Surely goodness and mercy will pursue me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of YHWH for the length of days.

24.1: A Psalm of David. Unto YHWH is the earth and its fullness, the world and those dwelling in it.
24.2: For He has founded it upon the seas, and upon the rivers He established it.
24.3: Who will ascend onto the hill of YHWH, or who will stand in His holy place?
24.4: The one clean of hands and pure of heart, who has not lifted up his soul to vanity, and has not sworn by deceit.
24.5: He will lift up a blessing from YHWH, and righteousness from the God of his salvation.
24.6: This is the generation of those seeking Him, those seeking Your face, O God of Jacob. Selah
24.7: lift up, O gates, your? heads, and be lifted up, O ancient doors, and the King of Glory shall come in!
24.8: Who is this King of Glory? YHWH strong and mighty, YHWH mighty in battle!
24.9: Lift up, O gates, your? heads, and be lifted up, O ancient doors, and the King of glory shall come in !
24.10: Who is this King of glory? YHWH of Hosts, He is the King of Glory! Selah

25.1: of David. Unto You, O YHWH, I lift up my soul.
25.2: My God, in You I trust. Do not let me be put to shame; do not let my enemies rejoice over me.
25.3: Indeed, all those waiting on You will not be put to shame; those who act treacherously without cause will be ashamed.
25.4: May You show me Your ways, O YHWH; may You teach me Your paths.
25.5: Make me to walk in Your truth and teach me, for You are the God of my salvation; on You I wait all the day.
25.6: Remember Your mercies, O YHWH, and Your loving devotion, for they are from of old.
25.7: May You not remember the sins of my youth, and my transgressions; according to Your loving devotion, may You remember me, for the sake of Your goodness, O YHWH.
25.8: Good and upright is YHWH; upon thus, He directs sinful ones in the way.
25.9: He guides the humble ones in justice, and He teaches the humble ones His way.
25.10: All the paths of YHWH are loving devotion and truth, to those keeping His covenant and His decrees.
25.11: And for the sake of Your name, O YHWH, may You pardon my iniquity, though it is great.
25.12: Who is this man, the one fearing YHWH? He will direct him in the way he must choose.
25.13: His soul shall dwell in goodness, and his seed shall possess the land.
25.14: The counsel of YHWH is with those fearing Him, and He makes known to them His covenant.
25.15: My eyes are continually toward YHWH, for He brings out my feet from a net.
25.16: May You turn to me and show favor to me, for I am lonely and afflicted.
25.17: The troubles of my heart have enlarged; may You bring me out from my distress.
25.18: Look on my affliction and my trouble, and lift all my sins.
25.19: See my enemies!—For they are many, and with violent hatred they hate me.
25.20: Guard my soul and deliver me; let me not be put to shame, for I take refuge in You.
25.21: May integrity and uprightness keep me, for I wait for You.
25.22: May You redeem Israel, O God, from all his distress.

26.1: Of David. Judge me, O YHWH! For I have walked in my integrity, and I have trusted in YHWH without slipping.
26.2: Examine me, O YHWH, and test me; refine my kidneys and my heart.
26.3: For Your loving devotion is before my eyes, and I have walked in Your truth.
26.4: I do not sit with men of vanity, and with dissemblers I will not go in.
26.5: I hate the assembly of evildoers, and with the wicked I do not sit.
26.6: I wash my hands in innocence, and I go about Your altar, O YHWH,
26.7: to sound with a voice of thanksgiving, and to recount all Your wonders.
26.8: O YHWH, I love the habitation of Your house, and the place of the dwelling of Your glory.
26.9: Do not gather my soul with sinners, and my life with men of blood,
26.10: in whose hands is a scheme, and whose right hand is filled with a bribe.
26.11: And I, in my integrity, walk; redeem me and be merciful to me.
26.12: My foot stands on level ground; in the assemblies I will bless YHWH.

27.1: Of David. YHWH is my light and my salvation—of whom shall I be afraid? YHWH is the strength of my life—of whom shall I be in dread?
27.2: In the coming near of the wicked against me, for the eating of my flesh, my adversaries and my enemies to me, they stumble and fall.
27.3: Though a host may encamp against me, my heart will not fear; though war should rise up against me, in this I will keep my trust.
27.4: One thing I have asked from YHWH—it do I seek—that I may dwell in the house of YHWH all the days of my life, to look on the beauty of YHWH, and to inquire in His temple.
27.5: For He will hide me in His shelter in the day of evil; He will conceal me under the cover of His tent; upon a rock He will set me high.
27.6: And now my head will be held high over my enemies all around me, and I will offer in His tent sacrifices of shouting; I will sing and make music to YHWH.
27.7: Hear, O YHWH, my voice when I call, and be merciful to me and answer me.
27.8: You have said to my heart “Seek My face.” Your face, O YHWH, I will seek.
27.9: Do not hide Your face from me; do not stretch Your servant away in anger! You have been my help—do not leave me and do not forsake me, O God of my salvation.
27.10: Though my father and my mother have forsaken me, yet YHWH will gather me in .
27.11: Teach me Your way, O YHWH, and lead me on a level path, on account of my enemies.
27.12: Do not give me up to the desires of my adversaries, for false witnesses have risen against me, and they breathe out violence.
27.13: Still I believed to look on the goodness of YHWH in the land of the living.
27.14: Wait for YHWH; be strong and let your heart be courageous. And wait for YHWH!

28.1: Of David. To You, O YHWH, I call out. O my Rock, do not be deaf to me, lest You be silent to me and I be likened with those descending to the Pit.
28.2: May You hear the voice of my supplications, in my crying out to You, in my lifting up my hands toward Your holy sanctuary.
28.3: Do not drag me away with the wicked, and with the workers of iniquity, those speaking peace with their neighbors, and evil is in their heart.
28.4: Give to them according to their doing, and according to the evil of their deeds! According to the work of their hands, give to them; return their dealing to them!
28.5: For they do not have regard toward the works of YHWH, and toward the deed of His hands; He shall overthrow them, and not rebuild them.
28.6: Blessed be YHWH, for He has heard the voice of my supplications.
28.7: YHWH is my strength and my shield; in Him my heart trusts, and I am helped; so my heart rejoices, and with my song I will give thanks to Him.
28.8: YHWH is the strength for them; and He is a stronghold of salvation for His anointed.
28.9: May You save Your people and bless Your inheritance, and shepherd them and carry them, even to forever.

29.1: A Psalm of David. Ascribe to YHWH, O sons of the mighty ones. Ascribe to YHWH glory and strength.
29.2: Ascribe to YHWH the glory of His name; bow down before YHWH in the splendor of holiness.
29.3: The voice of YHWH is upon the waters; the God of glory thunders—YHWH is over many waters.
29.4: The voice of YHWH is with power, the voice of YHWH is with majesty.
29.5: The voice of YHWH breaks the cedars, and YHWH shatters the cedars of Lebanon.
29.6: He makes Lebanon skip like a calf, and Sirion like the son of a wild ox.
29.7: The voice of YHWH is hewing flames of fire.
29.8: The voice of YHWH causes the wilderness to writhe; YHWH causes the wilderness of Kadesh to writhe.
29.9: The voice of YHWH twists the oaks, and strips bare the forests—and in His temple all of it is saying, “Glory!”
29.10: YHWH has sat over the flood, and YHWH sits as King forever.
29.11: YHWH gives strength to His people; YHWH blesses His people with peace.

30.1: A Psalm. A song for the dedication of the house of David. I will exalt You, O YHWH, for You have drawn me up, and You did not let my enemies rejoice over me.
30.2: O YHWH my God, I cried for help to You, and You healed me.
30.3: O YHWH, You have brought up my soul from Sheol; from my going down to the Pit, You have kept me alive.
30.4: Make music to YHWH, His faithful ones, and praise His holy name.
30.5: For a moment is His anger; for a lifetime is His favor. In the night lodges weeping, and in the morning, joy.
30.6: And I said in my prosperity, “I will not be shaken forever.”
30.7: O YHWH, by Your favor You made my mountain stand strong; You have hidden Your face—I was dismayed.
30.8: To You, O YHWH, I called out, and to my Lord I made supplication.
30.9: “What profit is in my blood, in my going down to the Pit? Will the dust confess You? Will it declare Your truth?
30.10: Hear, O YHWH, and have mercy on me; O YHWH, be the One giving help to me.”
30.11: You have turned my mourning to dancing for me; You have opened my sackcloth and clothed me with gladness,
30.12: so that my glory will sing praise to you, and not be silent. O YHWH, my God, I will give thanks to You forever.

31.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. In You, O YHWH, I have taken refuge; do not let me be ashamed forever; in Your righteousness, deliver me!
31.2: Incline Your ear to me, and quickly deliver me. Be for me as a rock of refuge, as a house of strongholds to save me.
31.3: For You are my rock and my stronghold; and for the sake of Your name, You will lead me and guide me.
31.4: You will bring me out from the net that they have hidden for me, because You are my refuge.
31.5: Into Your hand I commit my spirit; You have redeemed me, O YHWH, God of truth.
31.6: I have hated those serving worthless idols; and I toward YHWH place my trust.
31.7: I will rejoice and be glad in Your loving devotion—in that You have seen my affliction, and you knew of the distress of my soul.
31.8: And You did not shut me up in the hand of the enemy, You set my feet on a broad place.
31.9: Show favor to me, O YHWH, for there is distress for me! My eye, my soul, and my belly have wasted away with grief.
31.10: For my life has been consumed with grief, and my years with groaning; my strength fails in my iniquity, and my bones are wasting away.
31.11: Among all my enemies I am an object of scorn, even to my neighbors, and a source of dread, exceedingly to those who know me; those seeing me in the street flee from me!
31.12: I have been forgotten like a dead man from the heart; I am perishing like a vessel.
31.13: For I have heard the slander of many, terror from round about, in their taking counsel together against me; they have plotted to take my life.
31.14: Yet I have trusted in You, O YHWH; I say, “You are My God.”
31.15: In Your hand are my times; deliver me from the hand of my enemies, and from those pursuing me.
31.16: Cause Your face to shine upon Your servant; save me in Your loving devotion.
31.17: O YHWH, do not let me be ashamed, for I have called to You. Let the wicked be ashamed; let them be silent in Sheol.
31.18: Let the lips of falsehood become dumb, those speaking against the righteous with arrogance, with pride and contempt.
31.19: How great is Your goodness, which You have laid up for those fearing You, which you have prepared for those taking refuge in You before the sons of man!
31.20: You shall hide them in the hidden place of Your presence, from the plots of man; you shall store them in a shelter, from the strife of tongues.
31.21: Blessed be YHWH, for He has made marvelous His loving devotion to me, in a city under siege.
31.22: For I said in my haste, “I am cut off from before Your eyes!” Surely You heard the voice of my supplications, in my crying out to You.
31.23: Love YHWH, all you His holy ones! YHWH is watching the faithful and recompensing the worker of pride in excess.
31.24: Hold firm, and He shall strengthen your? heart, all those waiting for YHWH.

32.1: Of David. A Maskil. Blessed is he with transgression lifted, with sin is covered.
32.2: Blessed is the man for whom YHWH does not reckon iniquity, and in his spirit is no deceit.
32.3: When I remained silent, my bones were worn out by my groaning all the day.
32.4: For day and night Your hand was heavy upon me; my juice was overturned, as in the drought of summer. Selah
32.5: I made known to You my sin, and my iniquity I did not hide; I said, “I will make confession over my transgressions to YHWH,” and You forgave the guilt of my sin. Selah
32.6: upon thus, let every godly one pray to You, in a time to find; even with a flood of great waters toward him, they shall not touch him.
32.7: You are a hiding place unto me; from distress you keep me; with shouts of deliverance, You will surround me. Selah
32.8: I will instruct you and direct you in the way that you should go; with My eye I will give counsel concerning you.
32.9: You? should not be like a horse, like a mule without understanding, with bit and bridle to curb its ornaments, that there is no coming near to you.
32.10: Many are the pains of the wicked, and the one trusting in YHWH, loving devotion shall surround him.
32.11: Be glad in YHWH and rejoice, O righteous ones, and shout for joy, all upright of heart!

33.1: Give a ringing cry, O righteous ones, in YHWH! Beautiful is the praise for the upright.
33.2: Give thanks to YHWH with the harp; with an instrument of ten strings , make music to Him.
33.3: Sing to Him a new song; play skillfully with a shout of joy.
33.4: For straight is the word of YHWH, and all His work is trustworthy.
33.5: He loves righteousness and justice; the earth is full of the loving devotion of YHWH.
33.6: By the word of YHWH the heavens were made, and by the breath of His mouth all their host.
33.7: He gathers together the waters of the sea as a heap; He lays up the depths in storehouses.
33.8: Let them be afraid of YHWH, all the earth; let all those dwelling in the world stand in awe of Him!
33.9: For He has spoken, and it came to pass; He has commanded, and it stood firm.
33.10: YHWH breaks apart the counsel of nations; He has diasllowed the plans of the peoples.
33.11: The counsel of YHWH stands forever, the plans of His heart from generation and to generation.
33.12: Blessed is the nation for whom YHWH is its God, the people He has chosen as the inheritance for Him.
33.13: From the heavens, YHWH looks; He sees all the sons of man.
33.14: From the fixed place of His sitting, He gazes toward all those dwelling on the earth.
33.15: He fashions their hearts together; He attends to all their works.
33.16: No king is saved by the abundance of his strength; a mighty man is not delivered by the abundance of his power.
33.17: His horse is a false thing for salvation, and in the abundance of its strength it cannot provide escape.
33.18: Behold, the eye of YHWH is toward those who fear Him, to those waiting on His loving devotion,
33.19: to snatch their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.
33.20: Our soul waits for YHWH—He is our help and our shield.
33.21: For in Him our heart shall rejoice, because we have trusted in His holy Name.
33.22: May Your loving devotion, O YHWH, be upon us, as we do wait for You.

34.1: Of David, in his changing his behavior before Abimelech, and he drove him off, and he went. I will bless YHWH at every time; His praise is continually in my mouth.
34.2: In YHWH my soul boasts; let the humble hear and be glad.
34.3: Magnify YHWH with me, and let us exalt His name together.
34.4: I sought YHWH, and He answered me, and He snatched me up from all my fears.
34.5: They looked toward Him, and they shone, and their faces were not ashamed.
34.6: This poor one called out, and YHWH heard, and from all his troubles He saved him.
34.7: The angel of YHWH encamps all around those who fear Him, and he rescues them.
34.8: Taste and see that YHWH is good; blessed is the man who takes refuge in Him.
34.9: Fear YHWH, His holy ones, for There is no lack for those fearing Him.
34.10: The young lions are in want and are hungry, and those seeking YHWH shall not lack any good thing .
34.11: Come, my sons, listen to me; the fear of YHWH I will teach you?.
34.12: Who is the man desiring life, loving days, that he may see good?
34.13: Keep your tongue from evil and your lips from speaking deceit.
34.14: Turn away from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.
34.15: The eyes of YHWH are toward the righteous, and His ears are inclined to their cry.
34.16: The face of YHWH is against those doing evil, to cut off from the earth their memory.
34.17: They cry out, and YHWH hears, and He delivers them from all their troubles.
34.18: Near is YHWH to the broken of heart, and He saves those crushed in spirit.
34.19: Many are are the afflictions of a righteous one, but from them all YHWH delivers him.
34.20: He guards all his bones; not one of them is broken.
34.21: Evil will kill the wicked, and those hating a righteous one will be held guilty.
34.22: YHWH redeems the soul of His servants, and not any of those taking refuge in Him will be condemned.

35.1: Of David. Contend, O YHWH, with my opponents; fight with those fighting me!
35.2: Take hold of shield and buckler, and rise in help of me,
35.3: and draw out the spear and javelin against those pursuing me! Say to my soul, “I am your salvation.”
35.4: Let them be shamed and dishonored, those seeking my soul; let them be turned back and confounded, those planning evil for me!
35.5: Let them be like chaff before the wind, and the angel of YHWH driving them away.
35.6: Let their path be darkness and slickness—and let the angel of YHWH pursue them!
35.7: For without cause, they have hidden a pit for me—their net; they have dug one for my soul without cause.
35.8: Let destruction come upon him that he shall not know, and the net that he has hidden, let it catch him; the destruction, let him fall into it!
35.9: And my soul will rejoice in YHWH; it will exult in His salvation.
35.10: All my bones will say, “O YHWH, who is like You?—Snatching the afflicted from the one stronger than he, and the afflicted one and the needy one from the one plundering him.”
35.11: Violent witnesses rise up; those whom I do not know; they interrogate me.
35.12: They repay me evil in place of good, to the bereavement of my soul.
35.13: And I—in their sickness my clothing is sackcloth; I humbled my soul with fasting, and my prayer returned upon my bosom.
35.14: As for a friend or brother to me—I went about as one mourning; in grief as for my mother, I bowed down.
35.15: And in my stumbling, they were glad and gathered themselves together; attackers they gathered together against me, and ones I did not know, they tore and did not cease.,
35.16: Like godless mockers at a feast, they gnashed at me with their teeth.
35.17: O Lord, how long will you look on? Turn back my soul from their ravages, my only one from the young lions!
35.18: I will give You thanks in the great assembly; among a mighty people I will praise You.
35.19: Do not let my enemies rejoice over me in deception; those hating me for no reason wink the eye.
35.20: For they do not speak peace, and against the quiet of the land, they devise words of deceit.
35.21: And they opened wide against me their mouths; they said, “Aha, aha! Our eyes have seen it !”
35.22: You have seen it, O YHWH; do not be silent! O Lord, do not be far from me!
35.23: Rouse Yourself, and awaken to my judgment, my God and my Lord, to my cause!
35.24: Judge for me according to Your righteousness, O YHWH my God, and do not let them rejoice over me.
35.25: Do not let them say in their heart, “Aha, our desire!” Do not let them say, “We have swallowed him up!”
35.26: Let them be put to shame and brought to confusion together, those being glad at my distress; may they be clothed with shame and dishonor, those magnifying themselves over me!
35.27: Let them shout for joy and be glad, those desiring my vindication, and let them say continually, “Let YHWH be magnified, the One delighting in the welfare of His servant!”
35.28: And my tongue shall utter Your righteousness—Your praise all the day.

36.1: For the one directing. Of the servant of YHWH, of David. An utterance Of the transgression of the wicked is within my heart; there is no dread of God before his eyes.
36.2: For he is flattering to himself in his own eyes, having found his iniquity to be hated.
36.3: The words of his mouth are wickedness and deceit; he has ceased to be prudent and do good.
36.4: He devises iniquity upon his bed; he stands himself on a path that is not good; he does not reject evil.
36.5: O YHWH, Your loving devotion is into the heavens, Your faithfulness as far as the clouds.
36.6: Your righteousness is like the mountains of Might, Your ordinances the great deep; You keep safe man and beast, O YHWH.
36.7: How precious is Your loving devotion, O God! Even the sons of man take refuge under the shadow of Your wings.
36.8: They are filled from the fatness of Your house, and You let them drink from the stream of Your delights.
36.9: For with You is the fountain of life; in Your light we see light.
36.10: Draw out Your loving devotion to those knowing You, and Your righteousness to the upright of heart.
36.11: Do not let the foot of pride come against me, and the hand of the wicked, do not let it drive me away.
36.12: There the workers of iniquity do fall! They have been cast down, and are not able to rise.

37.1: Of David. You should not fret ove those doing evil, and do not be jealous of the workers of iniquity.
37.2: For like the grass, soon they are cut off, and like the green of the herb they shall wither.
37.3: Trust in YHWH and do good; dwell in the land and graze in faithfulness.
37.4: And delight yourself in YHWH, and He will give to you the desires of your heart.
37.5: Roll upon YHWH your way; trust in Him, and He will act.
37.6: And He will bring forth your righteousness like the dawn, and your justice like the noonday sun.
37.7: Be still before YHWH, and wait patiently for Him; do not fret over the one succeeding in his path, over the man making schemes.
37.8: Cease from anger, and leave behind wrath; you should not burn— it only causes harm.
37.9: For those doing evil will be cut off, and those waiting on YHWH, they will inherit the land.
37.10: And in yet a little while, the wicked man is no more; and you will look for his place, and it is no more.
37.11: And the meek will inherit the land, and they will delight in the abundance of peace.
37.12: The wicked man is plotting against the righteous one and is gnashing his teeth at him.
37.13: The Lord laughs at him, for He sees that his day is coming.
37.14: The wicked have drawn out the sword and have bent their bows, to cast down the poor and needy, and to slay those upright in the path.
37.15: Their sword will come into their own heart, and their bows will be broken.
37.16: Better is the little of the righteous, than the riches of many wicked.
37.17: For the arms of the wicked will be broken, and YHWH upholds the righteous.
37.18: YHWH Knows the days of the blameless, and their inheritance will be forever.
37.19: They will not be ashamed in a time of evil, and in days of famine they will be satisfied.
37.20: But the wicked will perish, and the enemies of YHWH; like the splendor of the meadows they will be consumed, and with the smoke they will be consumed.
37.21: The wicked borrows and does not repay, and the righteous one is gracious and giving.
37.22: For those being blessed by Him will inherit the land, and those being cursed by Him will be cut off.
37.23: By YHWH the steps of a man are established, and in his way He delights.
37.24: Though he falls, he will not be cast down, for YHWH is upholding his hand.
37.25: I have been young, and I am old; and I have not seen the righteous forsaken, and his seed seeking bread.
37.26: All the day, he is gracious and lending, and his seed are a blessing.
37.27: Turn away from evil and do good, and you will dwell forever.
37.28: For YHWH loves justice, and He will not forsake His holy ones; forever they are preserved, and the seed of the wicked will be cut off.
37.29: The righteous will inherit the land, and they will dwell forever in it.
37.30: The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, and his tongue speaks justice.
37.31: The law of his God is in his heart; his steps do not slip.
37.32: The wicked is keeping watch on the righteous, and he is seeking to kill him.
37.33: YHWH will not leave him in his hand, and will not declare him wicked in his being judged.
37.34: Wait on YHWH and keep His way, and He will exalt you so as to possess the land; in the cutting off of the wicked, you will see it .
37.35: I have seen a wicked, ruthless one spread out like a luxuriant native tree.
37.36: And he passed away, and behold, he was no more; and indeed I sought him, and he could not be found.
37.37: Observe a blameless one, and see an upright one, for the end of the man is well-being.
37.38: But those transgressing will be destroyed together; the end for the wicked man is cut off.
37.39: And the salvation of the righteous is from YHWH; He is their stronghold in time of distress.
37.40: And YHWH helps them and rescues them; He shall rescue them from the wicked and save them, because they have taken refuge in Him.

38.1: A Psalm of David, for remembrance. O YHWH, do not rebuke me in Your wrath and chastise me in Your fury.
38.2: For Your arrows descend into me, and Your hand comes down on me.
38.3: No soundness is in my flesh before the face of Your anger; no peace is in my bones before the face of my sin.
38.4: For my iniquities have passed over my head; like a heavy burden, they are heavy for me.
38.5: My wounds are foul and festering, before the face of my foolishness.
38.6: I am bent, I am bowed down even to excess; all the day I go about mourning.
38.7: For my loins are full of burning, and there is no soundness in my flesh.
38.8: I am numb and crushed, even to excess; I roar from the groaning of my heart.
38.9: O Lord, all my desire is before You, and my groaning is not hidden from You.
38.10: My heart pounds, my strength has forsaken me, and the light of my eyes—even they are not with me.
38.11: My beloved and my friends stand away from my plague; my neighbors stand far off.
38.12: And those seeking my soul lay snares, and those searching out my harm speak; they ponder destruction and treachery all the day.
38.13: And I am like a deaf one who does not hear and like a mute one who does not open his mouth.
38.14: And I am like a man who is not hearing, and in his mouth are no rebukes,
38.15: because for You, O YHWH, I have waited; You will answer, O Lord my God.
38.16: For I said, “Lest they rejoice over me; in the slipping of my foot they magnify themselves against me.”
38.17: For I am ready to fall, and my sorrow is before me continually.
38.18: For I declare my iniquity; I am anxious over my sin.
38.19: And my enemies without cause are many, and those hating me without cause have been multiplied.
38.20: And those repaying evil for good, they oppose me, because of my pursuing good.
38.21: Do not forsake me, O YHWH; O my God, do not be far from me!
38.22: Hurry to help me, O Lord, my salvation.

39.1: To the one directing. For Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. I said, “I will guard my ways from sinning with my tongue; I will guard my mouth with a muzzle, as long as a wicked one is before me.”
39.2: I was mute in silence, I kept silent from good, and my sorrow was stirred up.
39.3: My heart became hot within me; in my musing, a fire was kindled; I have spoken with my tongue:
39.4: “Cause me to know, O YHWH, my end, and the measure of my days—what is it?— That I may know how fleeting I am .
39.5: Behold, as handbreadths have made my days, and my lifetime as nothing before You. Truly each one is a vapor, each man is stationary. Selah
39.6: surely like a shadow every man goes about; surely in vain they are in commotion; he heaps up, and does not know who will gather them.
39.7: And now, what am I waiting, O Lord? My hope, it is in You.
39.8: May You deliver me from all my transgressions; do not make me the reproach of the fool!
39.9: I have become mute, and cannot open my mouth—O that You would act!
39.10: Turn Your plague away from me! By the blow of Your hand I am consumed.
39.11: With rebukes over iniquity, You discipline a man, the things he desires you melt away like a moth; surely every man is a vapor. Selah
39.12: hear my prayer, O YHWH, and turn your ear to my cry; at my tears may You not be silent! For I am a sojourner with You, a sojourner like all my fathers.
39.13: Turn Your gaze from me, that I might be made glad, before I go on and am no more.”

40.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. I have waited patiently for , YHWH! And He inclined to me and heard my cry.
40.2: And He brought me up from the pit of desolation, from the mud of the mire; and He raised up my feet upon a rock, and has made firm my steps.
40.3: And He put in my mouth a new song—praise to our God. Many will see it and will fear, and they will trust in YHWH.
40.4: Blessed is the man who makes YHWH his refuge, and does not turn toward the proud, and those turning aside to falsehood!
40.5: Much You have done, O YHWH my God, and wonderful are your thoughts toward us—none can compare to You! Were I to declare and speak, they are vast beyond numbering.
40.6: In sacrifice and offering You did not take delight, You have opened the ears for me; burnt offering and sin offering You did not require.
40.7: Then I said, “Behold, I have come—in the roll of the book it is written of me.
40.8: I have delighted to do Your will, O my God, and Your law is in the midst of my heart.”
40.9: I have proclaimed tidings of righteousness in the great assembly; behold, I do not restrain my lips, O YHWH, You have known.
40.10: Your righteousness I have not kept hidden in the midst of my heart; Your faithfulness and Your salvation I have declared; I have not concealed Your loving devotion and Your truth from the great assembly.
40.11: You, O YHWH, do not withhold Your mercies from me; may Your loving devotion and Your truth continually guard me.
40.12: For evils have surrounded against me, until there is no numbering them ; my iniquities have overtaken me, and I am not able to see; they are vast beyond the hairs of my head, and my heart has left me.
40.13: Take pleasure, O YHWH, to deliver me! O YHWH, may You hurry to help me!
40.14: Let them be put to shame and confounded together, those seeking my soul, to snatch it away! Let them be driven back and humiliated, those delighting in my harm!
40.15: Let them be desolate for a reward of their shame, those saying to me, “Aha, aha!”
40.16: All those seeking You, may they rejoice and be glad in You; let those loving Your salvation say continually, “YHWH be magnified!”
40.17: And I am poor and needy; may the Lord think of me; You are my help and my deliverer—O my God, do not delay!

41.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. Blessed is he who acts wisely toward the poor; in a day of evil, YHWH will rescue him.
41.2: YHWH will guard him and keep him alive; he will be blessed in the land, and You will not deliver him to the will of his enemies.
41.3: YHWH will support him on his couch of illness; You turn over all his bed in his sickness.
41.4: I said, “O YHWH, be gracious to me; heal my soul, for I have sinned against You.”
41.5: My enemies speak evil against me: “When will he die and his name perish?”
41.6: And if one comes to see me , he speaks emptiness; his heart gathers iniquity for himself; he goes out—at the street he speaks.
41.7: All those hating me, they whisper together against me; against me they devise evil to me:
41.8: “Let a thing of destruction be poured into him, and he who lies down shall not rise up again.”
41.9: Even a man of my welfare, he in whom I trusted, who ate my bread, has lifted up the heel against me.
41.10: But You, O YHWH, show favor to me and raise me up, that I may repay them!
41.11: By this I know that You have delighted in me: That my enemy will not shout over me.
41.12: And I, in my integrity, may You take hold of me and set me before Your face forever.
41.13: Blessed be YHWH, God of Israel, from forever and even to forever! Amen and Amen.

42.1: For the one directing. A Maskil of the sons of Korah. As the deer pants for streams of water, so my soul pants for You, O God.
42.2: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When shall I come and appear before the face of God?
42.3: My tears have been to me as bread, day and night, in the saying to me all the day, “Where is your God?”
42.4: These things I remember, and I pour out my soul within myself: How I crossed over with the throng; I moved with them up to the house of God, with the voice of shouting and praise, with a multitude keeping the feast.
42.5: Why are you cast down, O my soul, and in commotion within me? Wait for God, for still I will praise Him, for the salvation of His face.
42.6: O My God, my soul is downcast within me; upon thus I remember You, from the land of the Jordan and Hermon, from the mount of Mizar.
42.7: Deep is calling to deep, at the voice of Your waterfalls, and all Your breakers and waves have passed over me.
42.8: By day, YHWH commands His loving devotion, and in the night His song is with me, a prayer to the God of my life.
42.9: I will say to God my rock, “Why have You forgotten me? Why must I walk in mourning in the oppression of the enemy?”
42.10: With a shattering in my bones, my enemies taunt me, in their saying to me all the day, “Where is your God?”
42.11: Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why are you in commotion within me? Wait for God, for still I will praise Him, the salvation of my face, and my God.

43.1: Judge for me, O God, and plead my cause! From a nation unfaithful, from a man of deceit and injustice, may You deliver me.
43.2: For You are the God of my strength. Why have You rejected me? Why must I walk in mourning, in the oppression of the enemy?
43.3: Send forth Your light and Your truth; they shall guide me; let them bring me to Your holy mountain, and to Your dwelling place!
43.4: And I will come to the altar of God, to God, the joy of my rejoicing; and I will praise you with the harp, O God, my God.
43.5: Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why are you disquieted within me? Wait for God, for still I will praise Him, the salvation of my face, and my God.

44.1: For the one directing. Of the sons of Korah. A Maskil. O God, with our ears we have heard; our fathers have recounted to us the work You did in their days, in the days of old.
44.2: You, with Your hand, dispossessed the nations, and You planted them; You afflicted the peoples and sent them forth.
44.3: For they did not gain possession of the land by their sword, and their arm did not bring salvation to them; but it was Your right hand, and Your arm, and the light of Your face, because You delighted in them.
44.4: You are my King, O God; Command deliverances for Jacob.
44.5: Through You we push back our adversaries; by Your name we trample those rising against us.
44.6: For not in my bow do I trust, and my sword will not save me.
44.7: For You have saved us from our adversaries, and those hating us, You have put to shame.
44.8: In God we have boasted all the day, and we will praise Your name forever. Selah
44.9: yet You have rejected us and put us to shame, and You do not go forth with our armies.
44.10: You make us turn back from the adversary, and those hating us have taken plunder for themselves.
44.11: You have appoint us for food like sheep, and among the nations You have scattered us.
44.12: You sell Your people with no profit, and You do not gain increase by their price.
44.13: You make us a reproach to our neighbors, a mockery and derision to those around us.
44.14: You make us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.
44.15: All the day, my disgrace is before me, and the shame of my face has covered me,
44.16: at the voice of a reproacher and reviler, from the face of the enemy and avenger.
44.17: All this has come upon us, and we have not forgotten You, and we have not dealt falsely with Your covenant.
44.18: Our heart has not turned back, nor our steps turned aside from Your path.
44.19: Yet You have crushed us in the place of jackals, and You have covered over us with the shadow of death.
44.20: If we had forgotten the name of our God or spread out our hands to a strange god,
44.21: would not God search this out? For He knows the secrets of the heart.
44.22: Yet for Your sake we are killed all the day; we are reckoned as sheep for the slaughter.
44.23: Rouse Yourself! Why do You sleep, O Lord? Awake! Do not reject us forever.
44.24: Why do You hide Your face? You forget our affliction and our oppression.
44.25: For our soul is bowed down to the dust; our belly has clung to the ground.
44.26: Rise up as helper to us, and redeem us for the sake of Your loving devotion!

45.1: For the one directing. According to “The Lilies.” Of the sons of Korah. A Maskil. A song of love. My heart is stirred by a good word; I am telling my works, O king; my tongue is the pen of a swift scribe.
45.2: You are beautiful above the sons of man; favor is poured out from your lips; upon thus God has blessed you forever.
45.3: Gird your sword upon the thigh, O mighty one, with your majesty and splendor!
45.4: And in your splendor prosper! Ride forth upon a word of truth and gentleness and justice; and your right hand will teach you fearful things .
45.5: Your arrows are sharp—the peoples fall under you—in the heart of the enemies of the king.
45.6: Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; a scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your kingdom.
45.7: You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; upon thus, God, your God has anointed you with the oil of joy above your companions.
45.8: All your garments are with myrrh and aloes and cassia; from the palaces of ivory, the harps have made you glad.
45.9: Daughters of kings are among your precious ones; the queen stands at your right hand, in gold of Ophir.
45.10: Hear, O daughter, and see; and incline your ear, and forget your people and the house of your father.
45.11: For the king himself desires your beauty, because he is your lord—and bow to him—
45.12: and the daughter of Tyre, with a gift; the rich among the people, will seek your face.
45.13: All glorious is the daughter of the king inside; her clothing is with weavings of gold.
45.14: In various colors she is brought to the king; the virgins following her, companions of hers, shall be brought to you.
45.15: They are brought along with joy and rejoicing; they shall come into the palace of the king.
45.16: In place of your fathers shall be your sons, whom you shall make to be princes in all the earth.
45.17: I will cause your name to be remembered through all generations; upon thus the people will praise you forever and ever.

46.1: For the one directing. Of the sons of Korah. According to Alamoth. A Song. God is unto us a place of refuge and strength, a help in distress surely to be found.
46.2: Upon thus we will not fear, with the changing of the earth, and with the shaking of the mountains into the heart of the seas—
46.3: its waters roar and foam, and the mountains quake in its rising. Selah
46.4: there is a river whose streams make glad the city of God, the Holy Place of the dwelling of the Most High.
46.5: God is within her; she will not be shaken; God will help her at the turn of the morning.
46.6: The nations raged, the kingdoms were shaken; He has given forth with His voice; the earth melts.
46.7: YHWH of Hosts is with us; a secure height for us is the God of Jacob. Selah
46.8: Come, see the works of YHWH, who has set devastation upon the earth.
46.9: He makes wars cease to the end of the earth; the bow He breaks, and the spear He cuts apart; the shields He burns in the fire.
46.10: “Be still and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted over the earth.”
46.11: YHWH of Hosts is with us; a secure height for us is the God of Jacob. Selah

47.1: For the one directing. Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm. All you peoples, clap your hands; shout unto God, with a voice of a ringing cry!
47.2: For YHWH-Elyon is to be feared; He is the great King over all the earth.
47.3: He turns back the peoples under us, and the nations under our feet.
47.4: He chose for us our inheritance, the excellence of Jacob, whom He loves. Selah
47.5: God has gone up with a shout, YHWH with the voice of a shofar.
47.6: Sing praises to God, sing praises! Sing praises to our King, sing praises!
47.7: For the King of all the earth is God—sing praises with skill!
47.8: God reigns over the nations; God sits upon the throne of His holiness.
47.9: The princes of the peoples have gathered together, the people of the God of Abraham; for the shields of the earth are unto God— He is greatly to be exalted.

48.1: A song. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Great is YHWH, and greatly to be praised, in the city of our God, the mountain of His holiness.
48.2: Beautiful in elevation, the joy of all the earth, like the peaks of Zaphon is Mount Zion, the city of the great King.
48.3: God is in her citadels, known as a secure height.
48.4: For behold, the kings assembled; they passed over together.
48.5: They saw, so they were astonished; they were in dismay, and they hurried away.
48.6: Trembling took hold of them there, and writhing like a woman in labor.
48.7: With a wind of the east you broke apart the ships of Tarshish.
48.8: According to what we have heard, so we have seen, in the city of YHWH of Hosts, in the city of our God; God will establish her even to forever! Selah
48.9: we have reflected, O God, on Your loving devotion, within Your temple.
48.10: According to Your name, O God, so Your praise is unto the ends of the earth; Your right hand is full of righteousness.
48.11: Let Mount Zion be glad; let the daughters of Judah rejoice, on account of Your judgments!
48.12: Go around Zion and encircle her, and count her towers.
48.13: Set your? heart toward her bulwark, pass between her citadels, so that you? may recount it to the following generation.
48.14: For this is God, Our God, forever and ever! He will lead us over death.

49.1: For the one directing. Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm. Hear this, all you peoples; give ear, all you dwellers of the world,
49.2: both sons of a man and sons of a great man, the rich and the poor together!
49.3: My mouth will speak wisdom, and a declaration of my heart, understanding.
49.4: I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will open up my riddle with the harp.
49.5: Why should I fear in the days of evil, the perversion that surrounds me at my heels,
49.6: those trusting in their wealth, and who boast in the abundance of their riches?
49.7: A brother surely cannot ransom a man, and he cannot give to God his redemption price.
49.8: For costly is the ransom of their soul, and it shall cease forever,
49.9: that he might live on forever, and not see the Pit.
49.10: For he sees wise men die; the fool and the senseless one perish together, and they leave their wealth for others.
49.11: Their inward thoughts are their homes forever, their dwelling places from generation to generation, they proclaimed over the lands their own names.
49.12: And man in his wealth does not remain; he becomes like the beasts that perish.
49.13: This is their path of folly for them, and after them in their sayings they took delight. Selah
49.14: like sheep they are appointed for Sheol, and death will be their shepherd; the upright will rule over them in the morning, and their form shall be consumed in Sheol—the habitation for him.
49.15: Surely God will redeem my soul from the hand of Sheol, for He shall take me. Selah
49.16: do not be afraid when a man becomes rich, when the glory of his house increases.
49.17: For in his death he will take of it all nothing; his glory will not go down after him,
49.18: though his soul, in his living, he blesses, and they praise you when you do well for yourself.
49.19: He will come to the generation of his fathers; even to forever they shall not see light.
49.20: A man in his splendor does not understand; he is like the beasts that perish!

50.1: A Psalm of Asaph. God of gods, YHWH, speaks and calls the earth, from the rising of the sun to the going down.
50.2: From Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shines forth.
50.3: Our God comes and will not be silent; before His face a fire devours, and all around Him it will storm exceedingly.
50.4: He calls to the heavens from above, and to the earth, for the judging of His people:
50.5: “Gather to Me My holy ones, those having cut a covenant with Me by sacrifice.”
50.6: And let the heavens declare His righteousness—for God Himself is Judge. Selah
50.7: “Hear, O My people, and I will speak; O Israel, and I will testify against you: I am God, your God.
50.8: It is not over your sacrifices that I rebuke you, or your burnt offerings before me continually.
50.9: I will not take a bull from your house or male goats from your folds.
50.10: For every living thing of the forest is unto Me, and the cattle on a thousand hills.
50.11: I know every bird of the mountains, and the wild beast of the field is unto Me.
50.12: If I were hungry, I would not say it to you, for the world and its fullness is to Me.
50.13: Do I eat the flesh of bulls, and do I drink the blood of goats?
50.14: Make a sacrifice of thanksgiving to God, and fulfill to the Most High your vows.
50.15: And call on Me in the day of distress, and I will rescue you, and you will glorify Me.”
50.16: And to the wicked God says, “What to you is to recount My statutes, and to lift up My covenant upon your mouth?
50.17: And you have hated instruction, and cast My words behind you!
50.18: If you see a thief, then you are pleased with him, and your portion is with those committing adultery.
50.19: With your mouth you send forth evil, and your tongue weaves deceit.
50.20: You sit against your brother and speak; against the son of your mother you place fault.
50.21: These things you have done, and I kept silent; you thought I was just like you. I will rebuke you and set in array before your eyes.
50.22: Understand this, please, you forgetting God, lest I tear you in pieces, and there is no one to deliver!
50.23: The one making a sacrifice of thanksgiving honors Me, and he who sets his path, I will make to look on the salvation of God.”

51.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David, in the coming of Nathan the prophet to him after that he had gone in to Bathsheba. Have mercy on me O God, according to Your loving devotion; according to the abundance of Your compassion, wipe out my transgressions.
51.2: Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.
51.3: For I know my transgressions, and my sin is before me continually.
51.4: Against You, You alone, have I sinned, and have done the evil in your eyes, so that You are right when You speak, and You are pure when You judge.
51.5: Behold, in iniquity I was brought forth, and in sin my mother conceived me.
51.6: Behold, You take delight in truth in the inner parts; in the inmost place You teach me wisdom.
51.7: Cleanse my sin with hyssop, and I will be clean; wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.
51.8: Make me hear joy and gladness; let the bones You have crushed rejoice.
51.9: May You hide Your face from my sins, and wipe out all my iniquities.
51.10: Create a clean heart in me, O God, and renew a right spirit within me.
51.11: Do not cast me away from before Your face, and do not take away from me Your Holy Spirit.
51.12: Restore to me the joy of Your salvation, and uphold me by a willing spirit.
51.13: I will teach transgressors Your ways, and sinners will return to You.
51.14: Deliver me from bloodguilt, O God, the God of my salvation; my tongue will sing of Your righteousness.
51.15: O Lord, open my lips, and my mouth will declare Your praise.
51.16: For You do not take delight in sacrifice, or I would give it ; with a burnt offering You are not pleased.
51.17: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit. A heart broken and crushed, O God, You will not despise.
