51.18: By Your pleasure, do good to Zion; may You build up the walls of Jerusalem.
51.19: Then You will delight in the sacrifices of righteousness, a burnt offering and whole sacrifice ; then they will offer bulls upon Your altar.

52.1: For the one directing. A Maskil of David, in the coming of Doeg the Edomite, when he announced to Saul and said to him, “David has gone to the house of Ahimelech.” Why do you boast in evil, O mighty man? The loving devotion of God is all the day.
52.2: Your tongue devises destruction, like a sharp razor working deceit.
52.3: You love evil more than good, lying more than speaking righteousness. Selah
52.4: You love all devouring words, O tongue of deceit!
52.5: Even God will tear you down forever; He will take you up and pluck you out of the tent and uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
52.6: and the righteous will see and fear, and they shall laugh at him:
52.7: “Behold the man who did not set God as his refuge, and who trusted in the abundance of his wealth, and took strength by destruction.”
52.8: And I am green, like an olive tree in the house of God; I trust in the loving devotion of God forever and ever.
52.9: I will confess You forever, because You have done it . And I will call on Your name, for it is good before of Your holy ones.

53.1: For the one directing. According to Mahalath. A Maskil of David. The fool says in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt and have done abominable injustice; there is no one doing good.
53.2: God looks down from the heavens upon the sons of man, to see: Is there one acting wisely, seeking God?
53.3: All have turned back, together they have become corrupt; there is no one doing good, not even one.
53.4: Will they not learn, the workers of iniquity, those devouring my people they have devoured bread; they do not call upon God.
53.5: There they feared a fear—there was no fear. For God scattered the bones of the one setting camp against you; you have put them to shame, for God has rejected them.
53.6: Who will give from Zion the salvation of Israel? God, in turning back the captivity of His people, let Jacob rejoice; let Israel be glad!

54.1: For the one directing. With stringed instruments. A Maskil of David, at the coming of the Ziphites, when they said to Saul, “Is not David hiding himself with us?” O God, by Your name save me, and by Your might defend me!
54.2: O God, hear my prayer, give ear to the words of my mouth.
54.3: For strangers have risen up against me, and terrible ones have sought my soul; they have not put God before them. Selah
54.4: behold, God is the One giving help to me; the Lord is with those upholding my soul.
54.5: He will return the evil to my enemies—in Your faithfulness put an end to them!
54.6: With a freewill offering I will sacrifice to You; I will give thanks to Your name, O YHWH, for it is good.
54.7: For from all distress He has delivered me, and on my enemies my eye has looked.

55.1: For the one directing. With stringed instruments. A Maskil of David. Turn your ear, O God, to my prayer, and do not hide Yourself from my supplication.
55.2: Be attentive to me, and answer me; for I am restless in my complaint, and make a noise,
55.3: from the voice of the enemy, from the face of the oppression of the wicked. For they bring down trouble upon me, and in anger they hate me.
55.4: My heart is writhing within me, and the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
55.5: Fear and trembling have come upon me, and shuddering has covered me.
55.6: And I said, “Who shall give to me a wing like a dove, that I might fly away and be at rest—
55.7: behold, I would go far off wandering and would lodge in the wilderness. Selah
55.8: I would hurry to shelter for myself, from the rushing wind, from the tempest.”
55.9: Swallow up, O Lord, divide their tongues! For I have seen violence and strife in the city.
55.10: Day and night they go around it, on its walls, and iniquity and trouble are within it.
55.11: Disasters are within it, and oppression and deceit do not depart from its streets.
55.12: For it is not an enemy who scorns me, or I could bear it; and not one hating me who wielded power against me, or I could hide myself from him.
55.13: And it was you, a man like me in rank, my companion, and one known by me,
55.14: with whom we would take sweet counsel together; in the house of God we walked with the throng.
55.15: Devastation is upon them! May they go down alive to Sheol! For evils are in their dwellings, within them.
55.16: I will call to God, and YHWH will save me.
55.17: Evening and morning and noon I meditate and cry aloud, and He hears my voice.
55.18: In peace He has redeemed my soul from the battle against me, though there were many against me.
55.19: God will hear and afflict them—even He who sits of old—Selah with whom there are no changes for Him. And they have not fear God.
55.20: He has stretched out his hands against those united with him; he has profaned his covenant.
55.21: His mouth has been smoother than butter, and war is in his heart; his words have been softer than oil, and they are drawn swords.
55.22: Cast your burden upon YHWH, and He will sustain you; He will not give a righteous one to be shaken forever.
55.23: And You, O God, will bring them down to the Pit of destruction, men of blood and deceit! They will not live even half their days. And I will trust in You.

56.1: For the one directing according to “A Dove of Distant Oaks.” Of David. A Miktam in the seizing of him by the Philistines in Gath. Show favor to me, O God, for man would swallow me up; all the day fighting, he oppresses me.
56.2: My enemies swallow me up all the day, for many are fighting against me proudly.
56.3: On the day I am afraid, I will trust in You.
56.4: In God—His word I praise—in God do I trust. I will not fear; what can flesh do to me?
56.5: All the day they wrest my words; their thoughts are all for evil against me.
56.6: They gather together, they hide, they mark my steps when they lie in wait for my soul.
56.7: Over iniquity bring no escape for them! In your anger, may You cast down the nations, O God!
56.8: My wanderings You have counted; put my tears in Your bottle—are they not in Your book?
56.9: Then my enemies will turn back on the day I cry out. This I have known, that God is for me.
56.10: In God whose word I do praise, in YHWH whose word I praise—
56.11: in God I have put my trust! I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?
56.12: Your vows, O God, are upon me; I will give thank offerings to You.
56.13: For You have delivered my soul from death, Do You not keep my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living!

57.1: For the one directing. To “Do not Destroy.” Of David. A Miktam in his fleeing from the face of Saul in the cave. Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy on me! For in You my soul takes refuge, and in the shadow of Your wings I will make my refuge, until the calamities have passed by.
57.2: I will call out to El-Elyon, to God, who fulfills for me.
57.3: He sends forth from the heavens, and saves me; He rebukes the one panting after me. Selah God sends forth His loving devotion and His faithfulness.
57.4: My soul is in the midst of lions; I lie down among flames—the sons of men, their teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue is a sharp sword.
57.5: Be exalted above the heavens, O God, Your glory above all the earth!
57.6: They have prepared a net for my steps; my soul is bent down; they have dug before me a pit—may they fall into its midst! Selah
57.7: my heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast; I will sing and make music.
57.8: Awake, my glory! Awake, O harp and lyre—I will awaken the dawn!
57.9: I will confess You among the peoples, O Lord; I will make music to You among the nations.
57.10: For Your loving devotion reaches to the heavens, and Your faithfulness to the clouds.
57.11: Be lifted high above the heavens, O God, and above all the earth Your glory!

58.1: For the one directing. “Do not Destroy.” Of David. a Miktam. O silent ones, Is it truly righteousness you? speak? Do you? judge uprightly, O sons of man?
58.2: Even in the heart you? work iniquities; in the earth you? weigh out the violence of your? hands.
58.3: The wicked are estranged from the womb; they have gone astray from the belly, speaking lies.
58.4: Their venom is like the venom of a snake, like the deaf cobra that stops up its ear,
58.5: which will not listen to the voice of those whispering—charmers most skillfully charming.
58.6: O God, may You break their teeth in their mouths; tear out the great teeth of the young lions, O YHWH!
58.7: Let them flow away like waters, running continually to themselves; he treads the bow —may his arrows be as though cut in pieces.
58.8: Like a snail melting as it goes, the stillborn child of a woman, may they not see the sun!
58.9: Before your? pots can discern the thorn, as those living, as burning wrath He sweeps them away.
58.10: The righteous one will rejoice when he has seen vengeance; he will wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.
58.11: Then man will say, “Surely fruit is for the righteous; surely there is a God judging the earth!”

59.1: For the one directing. “ To Do not Destroy.” Of David. a Miktam at the sending of men by Saul, when they watched the house in order to kill him. Snatch me from my enemies, O my God; from those who rise against me, set me on a place up high.
59.2: Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from men of blood!
59.3: For behold, they lie in wait for my life; the mighty ones gather against me, not for my transgression and not for my sin, O YHWH.
59.4: Without my fault, they run and prepare themselves. Awake to help me, and see!
59.5: And You, O YHWH, God of Hosts, the God of Israel, may You awaken to attend to all the nations! You must not be merciful to any of those acting treacherously with wickedness. Selah
59.6: they return at the evening; they growl like a dog, and go all around the city.
59.7: Behold, they belch out with their mouths. Swords are in their lips: “For who hears?”
59.8: But You, O YHWH, laugh at them! You scoff at all the nations.
59.9: O my strength, I will keep watch for You, for God is my secure height.
59.10: The God of loving devotion will meet me; God will let me look on my enemies.
59.11: Do not kill them, lest my people forget. Send them staggering by Your strength, and bring them down! O Lord, our shield.
59.12: The sin of their mouth is a word of their lips; let them be captured in their pride and in the cursing and lies they recount.
59.13: Consume them in fury, consume them , and they are no more! And let them know that God is ruling in Jacob to the ends of the earth. Selah
59.14: and they return in the evening; they growl like a dog, and go round about the city.
59.15: They wander up and down for food; and if they are not satisfied, then they grumble.
59.16: But I will sing of Your power, and I will sing out Your loving devotion in the morning. For You have been a high place for me, and a refuge for me in the day of distress.
59.17: O My strength, to You I will sing praises! For God is my secure height, the God of loving devotion to me.

60.1: For the one directing. According to “The Lily of the Covenant.” A Miktam of David for teaching, in his struggling with Aram-naharaim and with Aram-zobah, when Joab returned and struck Edom in the Valley of Salt—twelve thousand men . O God, You have rejected us; You have broken us; You have been angry; may You return to us!
60.2: The earth You have made quake; You have torn it open; may You heal its fractures, for it is tottering.
60.3: You have made Your people see hardship; You have made us drink the wine of staggering.
60.4: You have given a banner to those who fear You, to be set prominently before the face of the bow. Selah
60.5: That Your beloved may be rescued, bring salvation with Your right hand and answer us.
60.6: God has spoken in His holiness: “I will rejoice; I will divide Shechem, and I will measure out the Valley of Succoth.
60.7: Gilead is unto Me, and Manasseh is unto Me; and Ephraim is the helmet for My head; Judah is My scepter;
60.8: Moab is the pot for my washing, and upon Edom I will cast My sandal; I will give a shout over Philistia.”
60.9: Who will bring me to the city of fortification? Who will lead me to Edom?
60.10: Have You not, O God, rejected us, and do You not go forth, O God, with our armies?
60.11: Give to us help against the enemy—for empty is the help of man!
60.12: In God we will perform with strength, and He will trample our adversaries.

61.1: For the one directing. On a stringed instrument. Of David. Hear, O God, my cry; attend to my prayer.
61.2: From the end of the earth I call out to You in the feebleness of my heart; may You lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
61.3: For You have been a place of refuge for me, a tower of strength against the face of the enemy.
61.4: Let me sojourn in Your Tent forever; I will take refuge in the shelter of Your wings. Selah
61.5: for You, O God, have listend to my vows; You have appointed the inheritance of those fearing Your name.
61.6: May You add days to the days of the king; may his years be as generation and generation.
61.7: May he dwell forever before God; may You appoint loving devotion and faithfulness to guard him.
61.8: So I will sing praise to your name forever, that I may perform my vows, day by day.

62.1: For the one directing. According to Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. Only toward God is my soul silent; from Him is my salvation.
62.2: Only He is my Rock and my salvation, My tower; not much will I be shaken.
62.3: Until when will you? plot against a man? You? are destroyed, all of you?, like a wall leaning, a fence being pushed down.
62.4: Surely from his high position they take counsel to thrust him down; they delight in lies—with their mouth they bless, and within them they curse. Selah
62.5: Only for God be silent, O my soul, for from Him is my hope.
62.6: Only He is my Rock and my salvation, my secure height; I will not be shaken.
62.7: In God is my salvation and my glory, the Rock of my strength; my refuge is in God.
62.8: Trust in Him at all times, O people; pour out your? heart before Him; God is is a refuge for us. Selah
62.9: Only a vapor are the sons of a man, a lie the sons of a man—in the scales they go up as a vapor together.
62.10: You should? not trust in oppression, and do not be vain in robbery; on wealth when it increases you? should not set the heart.
62.11: Once God has spoken; twice I have heard this, that strength is unto God,
62.12: and unto You, O Lord, is loving devotion. For You do recompense to each man according to his work.

63.1: A Psalm of David, in his being in the wilderness of Judah. O God, You are my God; I seek after You—my soul thirsts for You, my flesh longs for You—in a land dry and weary, without waters.
63.2: So in the sanctuary I have seen You, to behold Your power and Your glory.
63.3: Because Your loving devotion is better than life, my lips will praise You.
63.4: So I will bless You in my life; in Your name I will lift up my hands.
63.5: As with milk and fatness, my soul is satisfied, and with lips of joyful shouts, my mouth will praise You,
63.6: when I remember You on my bed, through the watches of the night I meditate on You.
63.7: For You have been a help to me, and in the shadow of Your wings I will shout for joy.
63.8: My soul clings to You; Your right hand has taken hold of me.
63.9: And those who seek my soul for desolation, they will go into the depths of the earth.
63.10: They will fall by the hand of the sword; they will become a portion for foxes.
63.11: And the king will rejoice in God; everyone swearing by Him will exult, but the mouth of those speaking lies will be stopped up.

64.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. Hear, O God, my voice in my complaint; from the dread of the enemy, may You preserve my life.
64.2: Hide me from the counsel of the wicked, from the mob of workers of iniquity,
64.3: who sharpen their tongue like a sword, and aim a bitter word as their arrow,
64.4: to shoot in secret at the blameless; suddenly they shoot at him, and do not fear.
64.5: They strengthen themselves in a word of evil; they recount the hiding of snares—they say, “Who is looking at them?”
64.6: They search out injustice: “We have perfected a searching plot.” And the inner man and the heart is deep!
64.7: And God will shoot at them an arrow—suddenly they will be wounded.
64.8: They will be made to stumble, their own tongue being against them; all those looking on them will flee.
64.9: And all mankind will fear, and they will declare the work of God, and His deed they will wisely consider.
64.10: A righteous one will be glad in YHWH, and he will take refuge in Him; and let all the upright in heart exult!

65.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. A Song. To You praise is waiting, O God, in Zion, and to You the vow will be fulfilled.
65.2: O Hearer of prayer, to You all flesh will come.
65.3: Matters of iniquities prevail against me—our transgressions, You wipe them away!
65.4: Blessed is the one You choose and bring near, that he may dwell in Your courts; we will be satisfied with the goodness of Your house, the holiness of Your temple.
65.5: With fearsome things , may You answer us in righteousness, O God of our salvation, the confidence for all the farthest ends of the earth and the sea,
65.6: the One establishing the mountains by His strength, being girded with might,
65.7: stilling the roaring of the seas, the roar of their waves, and the uproar of the peoples.
65.8: And those dwelling at the ends were afraid of Your signs; the goings forth of morning and evening You cause to sing.
65.9: You attend to the earth and give it abundance; greatly You enrich it; the stream of God is full of water; You provide their grain when thus You prepare it—
65.10: soaking its furrows, descending on its ridges; with showers You soften it; its springing up You bless.
65.11: You crown the year with Your goodness, and Your tracks drip with fatness.
65.12: They drip on the pastures of the wilderness, and the hills are surrounded with joy.
65.13: The meadows are clothed with the flock, and the valleys are feeble with grain—they shout for joy and even sing.

66.1: For the one directing. A Song. A Psalm. Shout to God, all the earth!
66.2: Sing forth the glory of His name; make glorious His praise!
66.3: Say to God, “How awesome are Your works! In the abundance of Your might, Your enemies feign obedience to You.
66.4: All the earth will bow down to You and will sing to You—they will sing praises to Your name.” Selah
66.5: Come and see the works of God, being fearsome in His dealing with the sons of man.
66.6: He turned the sea to dry land; through the river they passed over on foot. There we rejoiced in Him,
66.7: ruling in His might forever; His eyes keep watch on the nations; let those rebelling not lift themselves high. Selah
66.8: bless our God, O peoples, and make heard the voice of His praise—
66.9: the One putting our soul among the living and not having given our feet to slip.
66.10: For You have tested us, O God; You have refined us like the refining of silver.
66.11: You have brought us into a net; You have placed pressure on our backs.
66.12: You have caused man to ride over our heads; we came into the fire and into the water, and You brought us forth to a watered place.
66.13: I will come into your house with burnt offerings; I will fulfill to You my vows,
66.14: for which my lips have opened, and my mouth has spoken in the distress for me.
66.15: Burnt offerings of fatlings I will offer to You, with the fragrant smoke of rams; I will offer of bulls with goats. Selah
66.16: Come, listen, all who fear God, and I will recount what He has done for my soul.
66.17: With my mouth I called out to Him, and He was praised under my tongue.
66.18: If I had seen iniquity in my heart, the Lord would not have listened.
66.19: Surely God has heard; He has attended to the voice of my prayer.
66.20: Blessed be God, who has not turned aside my prayer, or His loving devotion from me.

67.1: For the one directing. On stringed instruments. A Psalm. A Song. May God be gracious to us and bless us, and cause His face to shine upon us, Selah
67.2: for the knowing of Your way on the earth, Your salvation among all nations.
67.3: May the peoples praise You, O God; may the peoples praise You—all of them.
67.4: May the nations be glad and sing for joy, for you will judge the peoples with uprightness, and You will guide the nations on the earth. Selah
67.5: let the peoples praise You, O God; let the peoples praise You—all of them.
67.6: The earth has given her produce; God, our God, blesses us!
67.7: God blesses us, and all the ends of the earth shall fear Him.

68.1: For the one directing. Of David. A Psalm. A Song. God Arises, His enemies are scattered, and those hating Him flee before His face.
68.2: As smoke is driven away, you will drive them away; like the melting of wax from the face of the fire, the wicked will perish before the face of God.
68.3: And the righteous will be glad; they will exult before God, and will rejoice exceedingly.
68.4: Sing to God, sing praises to His name! Lift up to the One riding on the clouds—YAH is His name—and rejoice before His face!
68.5: A father of the fatherless, and a defender of widows, is God, in the habitation of His holiness.
68.6: God causes the solitary to dwell in a home; He brings out the prisoners into prosperity, but the rebellious ones have dwelt in a scorched land.
68.7: O God, in Your going forth before the face of Your people, in Your marching through the wilderness, Selah
68.8: the earth shook; also the heavens poured down before the face of God, the One of Sinai, before the face of God, the God of Israel.
68.9: You showered rain of freewill offerings, O God; when Your inheritance was weary, You restored it.
68.10: Your community dwelt in it; You provided for the poor from Your goodness, O God.
68.11: The Lord gives speech, and those bearing news were a great army:
68.12: “The kings of the armies—they flee, they flee! And she who dwells in the house divides the spoil!
68.13: Though you? lie down among the sheepfolds—the wings of the dove are covered with silver, and her pinions with yellow gold.”
68.14: When Shaddai scattered the kings in it, it was as the snow on Zalmon.
68.15: A mountain of God is Mount Bashan; a mountain of peaks is Mount Bashan!
68.16: Why do you? gaze in envy, O mountains of peaks? On the mountain where God desires to dwell, YHWH indeed will dwell forever.
68.17: The chariots of God are myriads—thousands upon thousands! The Lord is among them on Sinai, in the sanctuary.
68.18: You have gone up to the height, You have led captive captivity; You have received gifts from man, even from the rebellious ones, that YAH God may dwell there.
68.19: Blessed be the Lord, who day by day lifts us up—the God of our salvation! Selah
68.20: God is unto us a God of salvation, and from YHWH the Lord is our coming forth from death.
68.21: Surely God will wound the head of His enemies, the hairy crown of the one walking in his guilt.
68.22: The Lord said, “From Bashan I will bring them back; I will bring them back from the depths of the sea,
68.23: so that you may plunge your foot in the blood, and the tongue of your dogs have its portion from your enemies!”
68.24: They have seen Your going about, O God, the going about of my God and my King, in the sanctuary.
68.25: Those singing go in front, and behind are those playing strings, and in the midst are virgins playing timbrels.
68.26: In the assemblies bless God, YHWH, from the fountain of Israel.
68.27: There is little Benjamin leading them, with the princes of Judah in their company, the princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.
68.28: Summon, O God, your strength! Make strong, O God, this which You have done for us.
68.29: Your temple is upon Jerusalem—the kings shall bring a gift to You.
68.30: Rebuke the creature of the reed, the herd of bulls with the calves of the nations—trample underfoot the desire for silver, scatter the nations delighting in war!
68.31: Envoys will come from Egypt; Cush will make its hands run to God.
68.32: O kingdoms of the earth, sing to God; sing praises to the Lord—Selah
68.33: to the one riding on the heavens, the heavens of old! Behold, He gives forth with His voice, a voice of power.
68.34: Ascribe strength to God, for His majesty is over Israel, and His power in the clouds.
68.35: You are fearsome, O God, in Your sanctuary; the God of Israel—He is giving power and strength to the people. Blessed be God!

69.1: For the one directing. According to “The Lilies.” Of David. Save me, O God, for the waters have come up to the soul.
69.2: I sink in the mire of the deep, and there is no place to stand; I have come into the depths of the waters, and a flood washes over me.
69.3: I have grown weary with my calling out; my throat is dry; my eyes fail, waiting for my God.
69.4: Those hating me without cause outnumber the hairs of my head; many are those seeking my destruction, my enemies with deception! That which I did not steal, I must then return.
69.5: O God, You know of my foolishness, and my guilt is not hidden from You.
69.6: Do not let those waiting on You come to shame because of me, O Lord YHWH of hosts; do not let those seeking You be disgraced because of me, O God of Israel!
69.7: It is for Your sake that I have carried scorn, and shame has covered my face.
69.8: I have become a stranger to my brothers, and a foreigner to the sons of my mother.
69.9: For zeal for Your house has consumed me, and the insults of those insulting You have fallen on me.
69.10: I even wept in the fasting of my soul, and it was for a reproach to me.
69.11: I made my clothing sackcloth, and I became to them as a byword.
69.12: Those sitting at the gate meditate against me, and I am the song of those drinking wine.
69.13: And I—my prayer is to You, O YHWH; in the time of favor, O God, in the abundance of Your loving devotion, may You answer me in the truth of Your salvation!
69.14: Deliver me from the mire, that I should not sink! May I be delivered from those hating me, and out of the depths of the waters.
69.15: Do not let the flood of waters engulf me, and do not let the deep swallow me up, and do not let the pit shut its mouth over me!
69.16: Answer me, O YHWH, for good is Your loving devotion; according to the abundance of Your tender mercies, turn to me.
69.17: And do not hide Your face from Your servant, for distress is unto me; hurry to answer me!
69.18: Draw near to my soul and redeem it; on account of my enemies, may You ransom me.
69.19: You know my scorn, and my shame, and my disgrace; all my adversaries are before You.
69.20: Reproach has broken my heart, and I am sick. And I looked for a bemoaner, and there was none, and for comforters, and I found none.
69.21: And they gave gall for my food, and for my thirst they made me drink vinegar.
69.22: May their table before them become a snare, and for a recompense—for a trap!
69.23: May their eyes grow dark from seeing, and their backs be bent forever!
69.24: Pour out upon them Your indignation, and may the burning of Your anger overtake them!
69.25: May their encampment become desolate; may there be no one dwelling in their tents.
69.26: For You—the one whom You struck—they pursue, and the pain of those You pierced they recount.
69.27: May You add iniquity to their iniquity, and do not let them come into Your righteousness!
69.28: May they be blotted out from the book of the living, and do not let them be written with the righteous!
69.29: And I am afflicted and pained; may Your salvation, O God, set me on high.
69.30: I will praise the name of God with a song, and will I magnify Him with thanksgiving.
69.31: And it is better to YHWH than an ox, a bull horned and hoofed.
69.32: The humble have seen—they rejoice—you seeking God, your? heart shall live!
69.33: For YHWH listens to the needy, and he has not despised His captives.
69.34: Let the heavens and earth shout praise to Him, the seas, and every moving thing in them.
69.35: For God will save Zion, and He will rebuild the cities of Judah, that they may dwell there and possess it.
69.36: And the seed of His servants will inherit it, and those loving His name will settle in it.

70.1: For the one directing. Of David. For remembrance. O God, deliver me, O YHWH, may You hurry—to help me!
70.2: Let them be ashamed and confounded, those seeking my soul; let them be turned back and be disgraced, those taking delight in my harm!
70.3: Let them be turned back over the heel of their shame, those saying, “Aha, aha!”
70.4: Let all those seeking You rejoice and be glad in You; those loving Your salvation, let them say continually “May God be magnified!”
70.5: And I am lowly and needy; O God, hurry to me! You are my help and my deliverer, O YHWH—do not delay!

71.1: In You, O YHWH, I take refuge; let me not be put to shame forever!
71.2: In Your righteousness, deliver me and cause me to escape; incline to me Your ear, and save me!
71.3: May You be to me as a rock, a habitation, to go in continually; You have given a command to save me, for You are my rock and my fortress.
71.4: O My God, rescue me from the hand of a wicked one, from the palm of the one acting unjustly and pressing.
71.5: For You are my hope, O Lord YHWH, my trust from my youth.
71.6: By You I have been supported from the womb; from the bowels of my mother You cut me out. My praise is continually for You.
71.7: I have been as a wonder to many, and You are my strong refuge.
71.8: My mouth is filled with Your praise, with Your splendor all the day.
71.9: Do not cast me off in the time of old age; according to the consumption of my strength do not forsake me.
71.10: For my enemies speak against me, and those watching my soul take counsel together,
71.11: saying, “God has forsaken him. Pursue him and seize him, for there is none to deliver him !”
71.12: O God, do not be far from me; O my God, hurry to be my help!
71.13: Let them be put to shame and consumed, those opposing my soul; let them be covered with scorn and disgrace, those seeking my harm!
71.14: But I continually will hope, and I will add upon all Your praise.
71.15: My mouth will recount Your righteousness, all the day Your salvation, though I do not know the numbers.
71.16: I will come in the might of the Lord YHWH; I will cause Your righteousness to be remembered—Yours alone.
71.17: O God, You have taught me from my youth, and until now I declare Your wonders.
71.18: Indeed, when I am old and grayheaded, O God, do not forsake me, until I declare Your arm to a generation, Your strength to everyone to come.
71.19: And Your righteousness, O God, is to the height! You who have done great things, O God—who is like You?
71.20: Though You have made me see many troubles and distresses, You will return and revive us; even from the depths of the earth, You will return and bring me up.
71.21: You will increase my honor, and surrounding me , You will comfort me.
71.22: I will also praise You with the harp for Your faithfulness, O my God; I will make music to You with the lyre, O Holy One of Israel!
71.23: My lips will shout for joy when when I sing praises to You, even my soul, which You have redeemed.
71.24: My tongue will also utter Your righteousness all the day, for they have been put to shame, for they are humiliated—those seeking my harm.

72.1: Of Solomon. O God, give Your judgments to the king, and Your justice to the son of the king.
72.2: May he judge Your people with righteousness, and Your lowly ones with judgment.
72.3: The mountains will carry peace to the people, and the hills justice.
72.4: He will judge for the lowly ones of the people; he will save the sons of the needy, and will crush the one oppressing.
72.5: They will fear You with the sun, and before the face of the moon, from generation to generations.
72.6: He will come down like rain upon freshly cut grass, like showers dropping on the earth.
72.7: In his days the righteous will sprout forth, and an abundance of peace, until the moon is no more.
72.8: And he will rule from sea to sea, and from the River to the ends of the earth.
72.9: Before him the desert dwellers will bow down, and his enemies will lick the dust.
72.10: The kings of Tarshish and the isles will return a gift; the kings of Sheba and Seba will bring near a gift.
72.11: And all kings will bow low before him; all nations will serve him!
72.12: For he will deliver the needy one crying out, and the poor one without a helper to him.
72.13: He will have pity on the poor and needy, and he will save the souls of the needy.
72.14: From oppression and from violence he will redeem their soul, and their blood will be precious in his eyes.
72.15: Long may he live, and it shall be given to him from the gold of Sheba; and prayer shall be made for him continually; all the day he shall be blessed.
72.16: There shall be an abundance of grain in the land; on the top of the mountains it shall wave, its fruit like Lebanon; and the people of the city shall flourish like the grass of the earth.
72.17: His name shall be forever; unto the turning of the sun his name shall increase; and all nations shall be blessed in him, they shall call him blessed.
72.18: Blessed be YHWH God, the God of Israel, the One doing marvelous deeds—Him alone!
72.19: And blessed be the name of His glory forever;. And let all the earth be filled with His glory! Amen and Amen.
72.20: The prayers of David son of Jesse have come to an end.

73.1: A Psalm of Asaph. Surely God is good to Israel, to those pure of heart.
73.2: And as for me, at a little stretching out of my feet, my steps had nearly poured out.
73.3: For I was envious of those boasting when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
73.4: For there are no pangs until their death, and their body is fat.
73.5: They are not in the trouble of mankind, and with men they are not are they plagued.
73.6: Therefore pride is their necklace; a garment of violence is a covering for them.
73.7: Their eye has bulged out from fat, the imaginations of their heart overflow.
73.8: They scoff and speak with malice; from a high place they speak oppression.
73.9: They have set their mouth in the heavens, and their tongue walks through the earth.
73.10: Therefore his people turn here, and waters of fullness are wrung out for them.
73.11: And they say, “How can God know? And is there knowledge in the Most High?”
73.12: Behold, these are the wicked, and at ease always, they have increased in wealth.
73.13: Surely in vain I have cleansed my heart, and I have washed my hands in innocence.
73.14: For I have been stricken all the day, and chastened in the morning.
73.15: If I had said, “I will speak like this,” behold, I would have betrayed the generation of Your sons.
73.16: And I thought to know this—it was toil in my eyes—
73.17: until I came to the sanctuary of God, and discerned their end.
73.18: Surely You set them on slick ground; You make them fall to destruction.
73.19: How they have become a desolation in a moment! They are swept away completely by terrors.
73.20: As a dream from awakening, O Lord, in rousing Yourself, You shall despise their image.
73.21: For my heart had grown sour, and my kidneys pierce themselves.
73.22: And I was brutish and could not know; I was a beast before You.
73.23: And I am always with You; You hold my right hand.
73.24: May You lead me with Your counsel, and afterward You will receive me in glory!
73.25: Who is unto me in the heavens? And besides You I do not take delight on the earth.
73.26: My flesh and my heart may be consumed, the rock of my heart and my portion is God to the age.
73.27: For behold, those far from You will perish. You destroy everyone prostituting himself from You!
73.28: But I—the nearness of God is good for me—I have placed my refuge in the Lord YHWH, to recount all Your works.

74.1: A Maskil of Asaph. Why, O God, have You rejected us forever? Your anger smolders against the flock of Your pasture.
74.2: Remember Your congregation You acquired of old; You redeemed as the staff of Your inheritance this Mount Zion—in it You have dwelt!
74.3: Lift up Your steps to the perpetual desolations—to everything done wickedly by the enemy in the sanctuary!
74.4: Your adversaries have roared within Your meeting place; they have set up their signs for signs.
74.5: He made himself known like one bringing on high axes against a thicket of tree;
74.6: and now all its engravings they strike down with ax and hatchet.
74.7: They have sent Your sanctuary into the fire, to the ground; they have desecrated the dwelling place of Your Name.
74.8: They said in their hearts, “Let us oppress them altogether!” They have burned all the meeting places of God in the land.
74.9: Signs for us we do not see; there is no longer a prophet, and there is no one among us knowing until when.
74.10: Until when, O God, will the adversary reproach? Will the enemy revile Your name forever?
74.11: Why do You turn back Your hand, and Your right hand? May You remove it from the midst of Your bosom!
74.12: For God is my King from of old, working salvation in the midst of the earth.
74.13: You divided the sea by Your strength; You broke the heads of the dragons upon the waters.
74.14: You crushed the heads of Leviathan; You gave him as food to the people of the dry places.
74.15: You burst open spring and stream; You dried up the enduring rivers.
74.16: To You is the day, also to You is the night; You established the light and the sun.
74.17: You set all the boundaries of the earth; summer and winter, You have formed them.
74.18: Remember this, an enemy has reproached YHWH, and a foolish people have spurned Your name!
74.19: Do not give to the wild beast the soul of Your dove; do not forget the life of Your afflicted ones forever!
74.20: Look to the covenant! For the dark places of the earth are filled as habitations of violence.
74.21: Do not let the oppressed turn back, having been disgraced; let the afflicted and needy ones praise Your name.
74.22: Arise, O God, defend Your cause! Remember the scorning of You from a fool all the day.
74.23: Do not forget the voice of Your enemies, the uproar of those rising against You, going up continually.

75.1: For the one directing: To “Do not Destroy.” A Psalm of Asaph. A Song. We give thanks to You, O God; we give thanks—for Your name is near—recounting Your wonders.
75.2: “When I take the appointed place, I will judge uprightly.
75.3: At the dissolving of the earth and all its dwellers, I measure out its pillars. Selah
75.4: I said to those boasting, ‘You? shall not boast,’ and to the wicked, ‘You? shall not lift up the horn;
75.5: you? shall not lift up your? horn to the height, or speak with an insolent neck.’”
75.6: For it is not from the going forth or from the sinking, and not from the wilderness is exaltation,
75.7: But God is judging—this one He brings low, and this one He lifts up.
75.8: For a cup is in the hand of YHWH, and the wine is foaming, full of mixture, and He shall pour out from this—how its dregs shall be wrung out— and all the wicked of the earth shall drink.
75.9: And I will declare it forever; I will make music to the God of Jacob.
75.10: “And all the horns of the wicked I will cut off; the horns of the righteous will be exalted.”

76.1: For the one directing. On stringed instruments. A Psalm of Asaph. A Song. In Judah God is known, in Israel His name is great.
76.2: And His tent is in Salem, and his dwelling place in Zion.
76.3: There he has shattered the arrows of the bow—the shield and sword and weapon of war. Selah
76.4: luminous are You and majestic, from the mountains of prey.
76.5: They became plunder, the mighty of heart; they have slept their sleep, and the men of might could not find any of their hands.
76.6: At Your rebuke, O God of Jacob, both the rider and horse are lain asleep.
76.7: You are to be feared—You! And who can stand before Your face at the time of Your anger?
76.8: From the heavens You have sounded the judgment; the earth has feared and has been still,
76.9: at the rising of God to the judgment, to bring salvation to all the lowly ones of the earth. Selah
76.10: for the wrath of man shall praise You; with the remainder of wrath You gird Yourself.
76.11: Make vows to YHWH your? God, and fulfill them ; let all around Him bring tribute to the Fearsome One.
76.12: He takes away the spirit of princes; He is feared by the kings of the earth.

77.1: For the one directing. According to Jeduthun. Of Asaph. A Psalm. My voice is to God—indeed I cry out with my voice to God—and He has given ear to me.
77.2: In the day of my distress I sought the Lord; my hand in the night has been stretched out, and it does not slacken; my soul refuses to be comforted.
77.3: I remember God, and I groan; I meditate, and my spirit becomes faint. Selah
77.4: You hold the lids of my eyes; I am troubled, and I cannot speak.
77.5: I consider the days of old, the years of ages past .
77.6: I remember, my song in the night; with my heart I converse, and my spirit searches.
77.7: “Will the Lord forever reject us , and not anymore will He add to show favor?
77.8: Has His loving devotion ceased forever? Has His speech come to an end to generation and generation?
77.9: Or has God forgotten to show favor? Has He shut up in anger His compassion?” Selah
77.10: and I said, “I am grieved. Has it changed—the right hand of the Most High?”
77.11: I will remember the deeds of YAH; indeed, I will remember Your wonder of old.
77.12: And I have meditated on all Your work, and I put forth Your deeds.
77.13: O God, in holiness is Your way; what god is great like God?
77.14: You are the God doing wonders; You have made known among the peoples Your strength.
77.15: You have redeemed with Your arm a people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah
77.16: the waters saw You, O God, the waters saw You and swirled; even the depths trembled.
77.17: The clouds poured forth waters, the skies gave forth a voice, and Your arrows flashed back and forth.
77.18: The voice of Your thunder was in the whirlwind; the lightnings lit up the world; the earth trembled and shook.
77.19: Your way was in the sea, and Your paths in the great waters, and Your footprints were not known.
77.20: You led Your people like a flock, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

78.1: A Maskil of Asaph. Give ear, O my people, to my instruction; stretch out your? ear to the words of my mouth.
78.2: I will open my mouth in a parable; I will pour forth things hidden from of old,
78.3: which we have heard and known, and our fathers have recounted to us.
78.4: We will not hide them from their children, recounting to a following generation: The praises of YHWH, and His strength, and His wonders that He has done.
78.5: For He established a testimony in Jacob, and a law He has appointed in Israel, which He commanded to our fathers, to make them known to their sons,
78.6: so that a following generation would know them—the sons to be born—to arise and recount to their sons,
78.7: that they should put their hope in God, and not forget the works of God, and keep His commandments.
78.8: And they should not be like their fathers—a generation being stubborn and rebellious, a generation that did not set firm its heart, and whose spirit was not faithful to God.
78.9: The sons of Ephraim, handling and shooting the bow, turned back on the day of battle.
78.10: They did not keep the covenant of God, and in His law they refused to walk,
78.11: and they forgot His deeds, and His wonders that He had shown them.
78.12: In the eyes of their fathers He did a wonder, in the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.
78.13: He divided the sea and let them cross over, and the waters He made stand up like a heap.
78.14: And He led them with the cloud by day, and all the night with a light of fire.
78.15: He split open the rocks in the wilderness, and gave to drink, like the depths in abundance.
78.16: And He brought forth streams from the rock, and made waters come down like the rivers.
78.17: And they added still to sin against Him by rebelling against the Most High in the desert.
78.18: And they tested God in their heart, by requesting food of their soul.
78.19: And they spoke against God, saying, “Is God able to arrange a table in the wilderness?
78.20: Behold, He struck the rock, and the waters gushed out, and torrents overflowed; and also is He able to give bread or provide food for His people?”
78.21: Therefore YHWH heard and boiled over, and a fire was kindled against Jacob, and anger came up against Israel,
78.22: because they did not have faith in God, and they did not trust in His salvation.
78.23: And He commanded the clouds above, and the doors of the heavens He opened.
78.24: And He rained down on them manna to eat—and he gave to them the grain of the heavens.
78.25: The bread of the mighty ones a man ate; food He sent to them in abundance.
78.26: An east wind He caused to blow in the heavens, and He guided by His power the south wind.
78.27: And He rained upon them food like the dust, and winged bird like the sand of the seas,
78.28: and He felled them in the midst of their camp, all around their dwellings.
78.29: So they ate and were satisfied greatly, and their desires He brought to them.
78.30: They were not estranged from their desire, and still their food was in their mouth.
78.31: And the anger of God came upon them, and He killed the fattest of them and subdued the young men of Israel.
78.32: In all this, still they sinned, and they did not have faith in His wonders.
78.33: And He finished off their days in futility, and their years in terror.
78.34: When He slew them, they would seek Him, and they turned back and sought after God.
78.35: And they remembered that God was their rock, and El-Elyon their Redeemer.
78.36: And they deceived Him with their mouth, and with their tongue they lied to Him.
78.37: And their heart was not established with Him, and they were not faithful to His covenant.
78.38: And He, the compassionate One, forgave the iniquity and did not destroy them ; and He often restrained His anger, and He did not stir up all His wrath.
78.39: And He remembered that they were flesh, a breath going by that does not return.
78.40: How often they rebelled against Him in the wilderness and grieved Him in the desert!
78.41: And they turned back and tested God, and they pained the Holy One of Israel.
78.42: They have not remembered His hand, the day when He ransomed them from the adversary,
78.43: when He worked His signs in Egypt, and His wonders in the field of Zoan.
78.44: And He turned their rivers into blood, and from their streams they could not drink.
78.45: He sent among them the swarm, and it consumed them, and the frog, and it destroyed them.
78.46: And He gave to the ravager their produce, and their labor to the locust.
78.47: He destroyed with hail their vines, and their sycamore trees with frost.
78.48: And He gave up to the hail their cattle, and their flocks to the firebolts.
78.49: He sent against them the burning of His anger—fury, and rage, and distress, a discharge of messengers of evils.
78.50: He made level a path for His anger; He did not spare their soul from death, and He gave over their life to the pestilence.
78.51: He even struck all the firstborn in Egypt, the first of the vigour in the tents of Ham.
78.52: And His people He made go forth like sheep, and He guided them like a flock in the wilderness.
78.53: And He led them to safety, and they did not dread, and the sea covered their enemies.
78.54: And He brought them to the border of His holiness, this mountain His right hand had acquired.
78.55: And He drove out the nations before their face, and caused them to fall in the line of inheritance, and the tribes of Israel He settled in their tents.
78.56: And they tested and provoked El-Elyon, and His testimonies they did not keep.
78.57: And they turned back and were treacherous like their fathers; they were turned like a bow of deceit.
78.58: And they provoked Him to anger with their high places, and with their carved images they made Him jealous.
78.59: God heard and boiled over, and exceedingly He rejected Israel;
78.60: and He left behind the dwelling place of Shiloh, the tent He had pitched among men,
78.61: and He delivered His strength to captivity, and His splendor to the hand of the adversary.
78.62: And He delivered up His people to the sword, and on His inheritance He boiled over.
78.63: Fire consumed His young men, and His virgins have not been praised.
78.64: His priests fell by the sword, and the widows could not weep.
78.65: And the Lord awoke as a sleeper, as a mighty one overcome by wine.
78.66: And He beat his adversaries back; a reproach forever He gave to them.
78.67: And He rejected the tent of Joseph, and the tribe of Ephraim He would not choose.
78.68: And He chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion, which He loved.
78.69: And He built His sanctuary like the heights; like the earth He founded it forever.
78.70: And He chose David His servant, and He took him from the folds of his flock;
78.71: He brought him from tending the ewes to be shepherd of Jacob His people, and of Israel His inheritance.
78.72: And he tended them with the integrity of his heart, and by the skillfulness of his hands he guided them.

79.1: A Psalm of Asaph. O God, the nations have come into Your inheritance; they have defiled Your holy temple; they have laid Jerusalem in ruins.
79.2: They have given the corpses of Your servants as food for the birds of the heavens, the flesh of Your holy ones for the beasts of the earth.
79.3: They have poured out their blood like the waters around Jerusalem, and there is none to bury them.
79.4: We have become a reproach for our neighbors, a mocking and derision for those around us.
79.5: Until when, O YHWH? Will You be angry forever? Will Your jealousy burn like fire?
79.6: Pour out Your wrath toward the nations that have not known You, and upon the kingdoms that have not called on Your name!
79.7: For they have devoured Jacob, and his habitation they have made desolate.
79.8: Do not remember the former iniquities against us; hurry to let Your compassion come, for we have been brought very low.
79.9: Help us, O God of our salvation! And deliver us, because of the glory of of Your name, and cover over our sins for the sake of Your name.
79.10: Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?” Let it be known among the nations before our eyes, the avenging of the blood of Your servants that was shed!
79.11: Let the groaning of the prisoner come before You; by the greatness of Your power, preserve the sons of death.
79.12: And may You return to our neighbors sevenfold into their bosom their reproach with which they have reproached You, O Lord!
79.13: Then we, Your people, and the sheep of Your pasture, will give thanks to You forever; for generation and generation we will declare Your praise.

80.1: For the one directing. According to “Lilies.” A Testimony. Of Asaph. A Psalm. O Shepherd of Israel, Give ear! The One leading Joseph as a flock, dwelling above the cherubim, shine forth
80.2: before the face of Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh! Rouse Your strength, and come to bring salvation for us!
80.3: O God, restore us, and cause Your face to shine, and we shall be saved!
80.4: O YHWH, God of Hosts, until when will You smolder at the prayer of Your people?
80.5: You have fed them the bread of tears, and caused them to drink with tears a third part.
80.6: You have made us a strife to our neighbors, and our enemies mock at it.
80.7: O God of Hosts, restore us, and cause Your face to shine, and we shall be saved!
80.8: A vine from Egypt You pulled out; You drove out the nations and planted her.
80.9: You cleared before her face and let her root take root, and she filled the land.
80.10: The hills were covered with her shade, and the mighty cedars with her branches.
80.11: She sent out her branches to the Sea, and her shoots to the River.
80.12: Why have You broken down her walls, and all passing by the way pluck her fruit?
80.13: The boar from the forest tears at her, and the wild beast of the field feeds on her.
80.14: O God of Hosts, return, we pray! Look down from the heavens and see, and attend to this vine,
80.15: and the root that Your right hand has planted, and the son You have made strong for Yourself,
80.16: having been burned in the fire, having been cut down. May they perish at the rebuke of Your face!
80.17: Let Your hand be upon the man of Your right hand, upon the son of man You made strong for Yourself.
80.18: Then we will not turn back from You; revive us, and we will call on Your name.
80.19: O YHWH, God of Hosts, restore us! Cause Your face to shine, and we shall be saved!

81.1: For the one directing. According to Gittith. Of Asaph. Ring out praise to God our strength; shout for joy to the God of Jacob!
81.2: Lift up a melody and strike the timbrel, the sweet-sounding harp with the lute.
81.3: Blow the shofar on the new moon, on the full moon at the day of our Feast.
81.4: For it is a statute, O Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.
81.5: As a testimony He established it in Joseph, in His going forth over the land of Egypt. I heard a language that I did not know:
81.6: “I turned aside his shoulder from the burden; his hands pass over from the basket.
81.7: In the time of distress you called, and I drew you out; I answered you in the secret place of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah
81.8: hear, O My people, and I will testify against you—O Israel, if you would listen to Me!
81.9: There shall not be among you a strange god, and you shall not bow down to foreign god.
81.10: I am YHWH your God, who brought you out from the land of Egypt; open wide your mouth, and I will fill it.
81.11: My people would not listen to My voice, and Israel has not consented to Me.
81.12: And I sent them away in the stubbornness of their hearts, to walk by their own counsels.
81.13: If only My people would listen to Me, Israel would walk in My ways!
81.14: In a moment I would subdue their enemies, and over their adversaries I would turn back My hand.
81.15: Those hating YHWH would feign obedience before Him, and their time would be forever.
81.16: And He would feed him of the fat of wheat, and with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.”

82.1: A Psalm of Asaph. God takes His stand in the congregation of the divine; among the gods he renders judgment:
82.2: “Until when will you? judge unjustly, and lift up the faces of the wicked? Selah
82.3: Judge for the weak and the fatherless; for the oppressed and needy bring justice.
82.4: Rescue the weak and needy; deliver them from the hand of the wicked.
82.5: They do not know, ad they do not understand; in the darkness they walk about; all the foundations of the earth are shaken.
82.6: I said, ‘You? are gods, and all of you? sons of the Most High.’
82.7: Surely like a man you? will die, and like one of the princes you? will fall.”
82.8: Rise up, O God, and judge the earth! For You have an inheritance among all the nations.

83.1: A song. A Psalm of Asaph. O God, keep no silence to You; do not be silent, and do not be still, O God.
83.2: For behold, Your enemies roar, and those hating You have lifted up the head.
83.3: Against Your people, they act with cunning counsel, and they take counsel together against Your treasured ones.
83.4: They say, “Come, and we shall make them disappear as a nation, and the name of Israel will not be remembered anymore.”
83.5: For, they consulted together in heart; they cut an alliance against You—
83.6: the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagrites,
83.7: Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek, Philistia with those dwelling in Tyre.
83.8: Assyria also has joined with them; they have become an arm for the sons of Lot. Selah
83.9: may You do to them as to Midian, as to Sisera, as to Jabin, at the River Kishon.
83.10: They perished at Endor; they became dung for the earth!
83.11: Make their nobles like Oreb and like Zeeb, and like Zebah, and like Zalmunna—all their princes—
83.12: who said, “Let us possess for ourselves the pastures of God.”
83.13: O my God, set them like the whirling dust, like chaff before the face of the wind!
83.14: As the fire burns a forest, and as the flame sets the mountains on fire,
83.15: so may You pursue them with Your tempest and terrify them with Your storm.
83.16: Fill their faces with shame, and they shall seek Your name, O YHWH.
83.17: Let them be put to shame and dismayed until forever, and may they be ashamed and perish!
83.18: And they shall know that You—Your name is YHWH—You alone are Most High over all the earth!

84.1: For the one directing. According to Gittith. Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm. How lovely is Your dwelling place, O YHWH of hosts!
84.2: My soul longs, even faints, for the courts of YHWH; my heart and my flesh cry out to the living God.
84.3: Even the sparrow finds a home, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she places her young—near Your altars, YHWH of hosts, my King and My God,
84.4: blessed are those dwelling in Your house! Ever they are praising You. Selah
84.5: blessed is the man, the strength of him is in You; highways are in their heart.
84.6: Passing through the Valley of Baca, they make it a spring; even with blessings the early rain covers it.
84.7: They go from strength to strength; each appears before God in Zion.
84.8: O YHWH, God of Hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob! Selah
84.9: may You see our shield, O God, and look upon the face of Your anointed.
84.10: For better a day in Your courts than a thousand; I would choose to stand at the entrance in the house of my God, than to dwell in the tents of the wicked.
84.11: For a sun and shield is YHWH God; favor and honor YHWH will give, and He will not withhold good for those walking with integrity.
84.12: O YHWH of hosts, blessed is the man trusting in You.

85.1: For the one directing. Of the sons of Korah a Psalm. You have shown favor, O YHWH, to Your land; You have turned back the captivity of Jacob.
85.2: You have borne away the iniquity of Your people; You have covered all their sin. Selah
85.3: You have gathered up all Your fury; You have turned from the burning of Your anger.
85.4: Restore us, O God of our salvation, and Your anger toward us cause to cease!
85.5: Will You be angry with us forever? Will You draw out Your anger to generation and generation?
85.6: Will You not turn back and revive us, that Your people may rejoice in You?
85.7: Show us, O YHWH, Your loving devotion, and give to us Your salvation!
85.8: Let me hear what my God YHWH will speak, for He will speak peace to His people and to His holy ones; He will not let them return to folly.
85.9: Surely His salvation is near to those fearing Him, that glory may dwell in our land.
85.10: Loving devotion and faithfulness have met together; righteousness and peace have kissed.
85.11: Faithfulness springs forth from the earth, and righteousness looks down from the heavens.
85.12: Indeed YHWH will give that which is good, and our land will give forth its produce.
85.13: Righteousness will go before Him, to prepare the way of His steps.

86.1: A Prayer of David. Incline Your ear, O YHWH; answer me, for I am afflicted and in need.
86.2: May You guard my life, for am I faithful; may You save Your servant—the one trusting in You—You my God.
86.3: Show favor to me, O Lord, for to You I call out all the day.
86.4: Make glad the soul of Your servant, for to You, O Lord, I lift up my soul.
86.5: For You, O Lord, are good and forgiving, and abundant in in loving devotion to all those calling on You.
86.6: Give ear, O YHWH, to my prayer, and attend to the voice of my pleas.
86.7: In the day of my distress I call on you, for You will answer me!
86.8: None like You is among the gods, O Lord, and not like Your works.
86.9: All the nations whom You have made shall come and bow down before Your face, O Lord, and they will glorify Your name.
86.10: For You are great, and the One doing wonders; You are God—You alone.
86.11: Instruct me, O YHWH, in Your way, and I will walk in Your truth; unite my heart to fear Your name.
86.12: I will confess You, O Lord my God, with all my heart, and I will glorify Your name forever.
86.13: For Your loving devotion is great toward me. And You have delivered my soul from the depths of Sheol.
86.14: O God, arrogant ones have risen against me, and a gathering of ruthless ones have sought my life; and they do not set You before them.
86.15: But You, O Lord, are a compassionate and gracious God, slow to anger and abounding in in loving devotion and faithfulness.
86.16: Turn to me and show favor to me; give Your strength to Your servant, and give salvation to the son of Your maidservant.
86.17: Make with me a sign for good, and those hating me will see it and be put to shame! For You, YHWH, have helped me and comforted me!

87.1: Of the sons of Korah. A Psalm. A song. His foundation is on the holy mountains.
87.2: YHWH loves the gates of Zion above all the dwellings of Jacob.
87.3: Glorious things are ascribed to you, O city of God. Selah
87.4: “I will mention Rahab and Babylon among those who know Me—behold Philistia and Tyre, with Cush: This one was born there.
87.5: And of Zion it will be said: “ This man and that man were born in her; and He, the Most High, will establish her.”
87.6: YHWH will record in the register of the peoples: “This one was born there.” Selah
87.7: and those singing, also as players on instruments: “All my springs are in you.”

88.1: A Song. A Psalm of the sons of Korah, for the one directing. According to Mahalath Leannoth. A Maskil of Heman the Ezrahite. O YHWH, God of my salvation, by day I have cried out, and in the night before You.
88.2: May my prayer come before Your face; incline Your ear to my cry!
88.3: For my soul is filled with troubles, and my life has reached to Sheol.
88.4: I am counted with those going down into the Pit; I am like a man with no strength,
88.5: among the dead set free, like the slain lying down in the grave, whom You do not remember anymore, even they who are cut off from Your hand.
88.6: You have set me in the lowest Pit, in darkness in the depths.
88.7: Upon me Your wrath has grabbed hold, and with all Your waves You have afflicted. Selah
88.8: You have put my acquaintances far from me; You have made me loathsome to them; I am shut in, and I cannot go forth.
88.9: My eye languishes from this affliction; I have called to You, O YHWH, all the day; I have stretched out to You my hands.
88.10: Will You work wonders for the dead? And if departed spirits rise up, do they thank You? Selah
88.11: Can Your loving devotion be recounted in the Grave, or Your faithfulness in Abaddon?
88.12: Will Your wonders be known in the darkness, and Your righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?
88.13: And I, O YHWH, cry for help to You, and in the morning my prayer comes before You.
88.14: Why, O YHWH, do You reject my soul? You hide Your face from me.
88.15: I am afflicted and dying from my youth; I bear Your terrors; I am in despair.
88.16: Your wrath has passed over me; Your terrors have destroyed me.
88.17: They came around me like the waters all the day, they surrounded me altogether.
88.18: You have put far my beloved and friend; my close acquaintance is darkness.

89.1: A Maskil of Ethan the Ezrahite. I will sing of the loving devotion of YHWH forever. To generation and generation I will make known Your faithfulness with my mouth.
89.2: For I have said, “Loving devotion is built up forever; the heavens—You establish Your faithfulness in them.”
89.3: “I have cut a covenant with My chosen one, I have sworn to David My servant:
89.4: ‘Even to forever I will establish Your seed, and build up for generation and generation your throne.’” Selah
89.5: and the heavens will praise Your wonders, O YHWH, and Your faithfulness also in the assembly of the holy ones.
89.6: For who in the heavens can be compared to YHWH, likened to YHWH among the sons of the mighty?
89.7: God is to be feared greatly in the council of the holy ones, and to be revered by all those surrounding Him.
89.8: O YHWH, God of Hosts, who is like You, O mighty YAH? And Your faithfulness surrounds You!
89.9: You rule over the pride of the sea; its waves mount up, and You still them.
89.10: You crushed Rahab as one slain; with the arm of Your strength, You scattered Your enemies.
89.11: Unto You are the heavens; the earth also is unto You; the world and its fullness—You have founded them.
89.12: The north and the south, You have created them; Tabor and Hermon ring out praise in Your name.
89.13: Unto You is an arm with might; strong is Your hand; high is Your right hand.
89.14: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of Your throne; loving devotion and faithfulness go before Your face.
89.15: Blessed are the people knowing the joyful sound; O YHWH, in the light of Your face they walk.
89.16: In Your name they rejoice all the day, and in Your righteousness they are lifted high.
89.17: For You are the splendor of their strength, and by Your favor our horn is raised high.
89.18: Indeed, unto YHWH is our shield, and our king unto the Holy One of Israel.
89.19: Once You spoke in a vision to Your holy ones, and said: “I have given help to a mighty one; I have exalted one chosen from the people.
89.20: I have found David My servant; with the oil of My holiness I anointed him,
89.21: with whom My hand is established—My arm also will strengthen him.
89.22: The enemy will not exact tribute from him, and a son of wickedness shall not afflict him.
89.23: And I will crush his foes before his face, and those hating him I will strike.
89.24: And My faithfulness and My loving devotion are with him, and in My name his horn will be exalted.
89.25: And I will set his hand over the sea, and his right hand over the rivers.
89.26: He will call to Me, ‘You are My Father, my God, and the Rock of my salvation.’
89.27: I also appoint him firstborn, the highest of the kings of the earth.
89.28: Forever I will keep for him My loving devotion, and My covenant will stand fast for him.
89.29: And I will establish His seed forever, and his throne like the days of the heavens.
89.30: If his sons should abandon My instruction, and do not walk in My ordinances,
89.31: if they violate My statutes, and do not keep My commandments,
89.32: then I will attend to their transgression with the rod, and to their iniquity with stripes.
89.33: And I will not break off My loving devotion from him, and I will not be false in My faithfulness.
89.34: I will not violate My covenant, and a declaration of My lips I will not change.
89.35: Once I have sworn by My holiness—I will not lie to David—
89.36: His seed shall be forever, and his throne like the sun before Me.
89.37: Like the moon It will be established forever, even as the faithful witness in the sky.” Selah
89.38: and You have cast away and rejected; You have been enraged with Your anointed.
89.39: You have renounced the covenant of Your servant; You have defiled his diadem on the ground.
89.40: You have broken down all his walls; You have set his strongholds to ruin.
89.41: All passing by the way plunder him; he has become a reproach to his neighbors.
89.42: You have raised high the right hand of his adversaries; all of his enemies You have made glad.
89.43: Also You have turned back the sharpness of his sword, and You have not made it stand in the battle.
89.44: You have made his brightness cease, and You have cast down his throne to the ground.
89.45: You have shortened the days of his youth; You have covered over him with shame. Selah
89.46: until when, O YHWH? Will You hide Yourself forever? Will Your rage keep burning like fire?
89.47: Remember me—how short a lifetime; for what emptiness You created all the sons of men!
89.48: What man can live and not see death? Can he deliver his soul from the hand of Sheol? Selah
89.49: where is Your loving devotion is Your Of old, O Lord, which You swore of David in Your faithfulness?
89.50: Remember, O Lord, the reproach of Your servants—I bear in my bosom from all the many peoples,
89.51: with which Your enemies have taunted, O YHWH, with which they have reproached the footsteps of Your anointed one.
89.52: Blessed be YHWH forever! Amen and Amen.

90.1: A Prayer of Moses, the man of God. Lord, You have been a dwelling place for us, in generation and generation.
90.2: In the time before the mountains were brought forth, or You formed the earth and the world, and from forever even to forever, You are God.
90.3: You return man to dust and say, “Return, O sons of men!”
90.4: For a thousand years in your eyes are like a day past that passes, or a watch in the night.
90.5: You sweep them away; they are in their sleep like the grass that sprouts up in the morning.
90.6: In the morning it blossoms and flourishes; in the evening it is cut down and withers.
90.7: For we are consumed by Your anger, and by Your wrath we are terrified.
90.8: You have set our iniquities before You, our hidden things in the light of Your face.
90.9: For all our days decline in Your fury; we finish our years as a sigh.
90.10: The days of our years, in them are seventy years, or if by strength eighty years; yet their pride is toil and trouble—for it is cut off soon, and we fly away.
90.11: Who knows the power of Your anger, for as the fear of You is Your wrath?
90.12: So teach us to number our days, that we may bring in a heart of wisdom.
90.13: Turn back, O YHWH—until when?—And have compassion on Your servants!
90.14: Satisfy us in the morning with Your loving devotion, that we may sing for joy and be glad in all our days.
90.15: Let us be glad according to the days You have afflicted us—the years we have seen evil.
90.16: Let Your work be shown to Your servants, and Your splendor upon their sons.
90.17: And may the favor of YHWH our God come to be upon us, and may You support the work of our hands upon us—and the work of our hands establish!

91.1: The one sitting in the secret place of the Most High, in the shade of Shaddai he will abide.
91.2: I will say of YHWH: “My refuge and my stronghold, My God, I trust in Him!”
91.3: Surely He will snatch you from the snare of the trapper, from the plague of calamity.
91.4: With His pinions He will give cover for you, and under His wings you will take refuge; a shield and buckler is His faithfulness.
91.5: You shall not be afraid of the terror of the night, or of the arrow that flies by day,
91.6: from the plague that walks in the darkness, or from the pestilence that destroys at noon.
91.7: A thousand may fall at your side, and a multitude at your right hand—it shall not draw near to you.
91.8: But with your eyes you shall look, and you shall see the reward of the wicked.
91.9: Because You, O YHWH are my refuge; the Most High you have made your dwelling place.
91.10: Evil shall not meet with you, and plague shall not come near, by your tent.
91.11: For He will command His angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways.
91.12: Upon the hands they shall lift you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.
91.13: Upon the lion and cobra you will tread; you will trample the young lion and the serpent.
91.14: “Because in Me he has delighted, so will I rescue him; I will set him up high because he has known My Name.
91.15: He will call on Me and I will answer him; I am with him in distress; I will deliver him and honor him.
91.16: With length of days I will satisfy him, and cause him to look on My salvation.”

92.1: A Psalm. A Song for the Sabbath day. It is good to give thanks to YHWH, and to sing praise to Your name, O Most High,
92.2: to declare in the morning Your loving devotion, and Your faithfulness in the nights,
92.3: on ten strings and on the harp, with a melody of the lyre.
92.4: For You have made me glad, O YHWH, by Your work; at the works of Your hands I shout for joy .
92.5: How great are Your works, O YHWH, how very deep Your thoughts!
92.6: A stupid man does not know, and a fool cannot understand this:
92.7: The wicked sprout like grass, and all the workers of iniquity blossom, to be destroyed forever and ever!
92.8: And You are on high forever, O YHWH!
92.9: For behold, Your enemies, O YHWH—for behold, Your enemies will perish; all the workers of iniquity will be scattered!
92.10: But You have lifted high my horn like that of a wild ox; I have been anointed with fresh oil.
92.11: And my eye has looked on my enemies; of those rising up against me, those doing evil, my ears hear.
92.12: The righteous one will sprout like a palm tree; like a cedar in Lebanon he will grow.
92.13: Those planted in the house of YHWH, in the courts of our God, they will sprout.
92.14: Still bearing fruit in old age, they will remain fat and flourishing—
92.15: to declare that YHWH my Rock is upright, and there is no injustice in Him.

93.1: YHWH reigns! In majesty He is robed. YHWH has girded Himself with strength. Surely the world is established; no more can it be shaken.
93.2: Your throne is established from that time; You are King from everlasting.
93.3: The floodwaters have lifted up, O YHWH, the streams have lifted up their voice; the streams have lifted up their breakers.
93.4: More than the voices of many waters, more majestic than the breakers of the sea, majestic on high is YHWH!
93.5: Your testimonies are fully confirmed; Your house is lovely in holiness, O YHWH, for the length of days.

94.1: God of vengeance, O YHWH, O God of vengeance, shine forth!
94.2: Rise up, O Judge of the earth! Return a recompense on the proud.
94.3: Until when will the wicked, O YHWH, until when will the wicked exult?
94.4: They pour out words of arrogance; all the workers of iniquity boast in themselves.
94.5: Your people, O YHWH, they crush, and Your heritage they afflict.
94.6: The widow and the foreigner they kill, and the fatherless they murder.
94.7: And they say, “Yah does not see, and the God of Jacob does not discern.”
94.8: Discern, O senseless among the people! O fools, when will you? be wise?
94.9: The One planting the ear, can He not hear? Or the One forming the eye, can He not see?
94.10: The One disciplining the nations, does He not correct—the One teaching a man knowledge?
94.11: YHWH knows the thoughts of man, that they are vapor.
94.12: Blessed is the man whom You instruct, O YAH, and teach him from Your law,
94.13: that You may give rest to him from the days of evil, until a pit is dug for the wicked.
94.14: For YHWH will not forsake His people, and he will not forsake His inheritance.
94.15: But to righteousness the judgment will return, and all the upright of heart will follow it.
94.16: Who will rise up for me against the evildoers? Who will stand up for me against the workers of iniquity?
94.17: Unless YHWH had been the help for me, My soul would soon have dwelt in silence.
94.18: When I said, “My foot is slipping,” Your loving devotion, O YHWH, supports me.
94.19: In the abundance of my anxieties within me, Your comforts delighted my soul.
94.20: Can the throne of iniquity join with You—one shaping mischief by statute?
94.21: They band together against the soul of the righteous, and condemn innocent blood.
94.22: And YHWH has become for me as a place up high, and my God as the rock of my refuge.
94.23: And He has turned back upon them their own iniquity, and in their wickedness He will finish them off—YHWH our God, will finish them off!

95.1: O come, let us sing out to YHWH; let us shout to the Rock of our salvation!
95.2: Let us come before His face with thanksgiving, let us shout joyfully with psalms to Him!
95.3: For a great God is YHWH, and the great King above all gods—
95.4: in whose hand are the depths of the earth, and the peaks of the mountains are unto Him,
95.5: and to whom is the sea, for He made it, and His hands formed the dry land.
95.6: O come, let us worship and bow down; let us kneel before the face of YHWH our Maker!
95.7: For He is our God, and we are the people of His pasture, and the sheep of His hand. Today, if you? hear His voice:
95.8: You? shall not harden your? hearts, as at Meribah, as in the day at Massah in the wilderness,
95.9: when your? fathers tested Me—they tried Me, even though they had seen My work.
95.10: Forty years I was repulsed by that generation, and I said, “They are a people going astray in heart, and they have not known My ways.”
95.11: So I swore in My anger, “They shall not come into My rest!”

96.1: Sing to YHWH a new song; sing to YHWH, all the earth!
96.2: Sing to YHWH, bless His name; give the news from day to day of His salvation!
96.3: Recount among the nations His glory, among all the peoples His wonders.
96.4: For great is YHWH, and to be praised greatly; He is to be feared above all gods.
96.5: For all the gods of the peoples are idols, and YHWH made the heavens.
96.6: Splendor and majesty are before His face; strength and beauty are in His sanctuary.
96.7: Give to YHWH, O families of the peoples, give to YHWH honor and strength!
96.8: Give to YHWH the glory due His name; lift up an offering and come into His courts!
96.9: Bow down before YHWH in the splendor of His holiness; tremble before Him, all the earth!
96.10: Say among the nations, “YHWH reigns!” Surely the world is firmly set—it cannot be shaken; He will judge the peoples with uprightness.
96.11: Let the heavens rejoice and let the earth be glad; let the sea and its fullness roar!
96.12: Let the field be joyful, and all that is in it! Then all the trees of the forest will shout for joy,
96.13: before YHWH, for He is coming—for He is coming to judge the earth! He will judge the world in righteousness and the peoples in His faithfulness.

97.1: YHWH reigns, let the earth rejoice, let the many shores be glad!
97.2: Cloud and darkness surround Him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of His throne.
97.3: A fire goes out before His face, and burns up His adversaries round about.
97.4: His lightnings give light to the world; the earth sees, and trembles.
97.5: The mountains are melted like wax from before the face of YHWH, from before the face of the Lord of all the earth.
97.6: The heavens declare His righteousness, and all the peoples see His glory.
97.7: Let all those serving a carved image be put to shame, those boasting in idols! Bow down to Him, all gods!
97.8: Zion hears and is glad; the daughters of Judah rejoice, because of Your judgments, O YHWH.
97.9: For You, O YHWH-Elyon, are over all the earth; greatly are You exalted over all gods.
97.10: O lovers of YHWH, hate evil; He preserves the souls of His holy ones; from the hand of the wicked He delivers them.
97.11: Light shines on the righteous, and gladness for the upright of heart.
97.12: Be glad, O righteous ones, in YHWH, and give thanks at the remembrance of His holiness!

98.1: A Psalm. Sing to YHWH a new song, for He has done wonders! His right hand has gained victory for Him, even the arm of His holiness.
98.2: YHWH has made known His victory; before the eyes of the nations, He has revealed His righteousness.
98.3: He has remembered His kindness and His faithfulness to the house of Israel; all the ends of the earth have seen the victory of our God.
98.4: Shout joyfully to YHWH, all the earth; break forth and cry aloud and sing praises!
98.5: Sing praises to YHWH with the harp, with the harp and the voice of melody!
98.6: With the trumpets and the voice of the shofar, shout joyfully before the face of YHWH the King!
98.7: Let the sea roar, and its fullness, the world, and those dwelling in it!
98.8: Let the rivers clap the hands; let the hills cry aloud together,
