98.9: before the face of YHWH—for He is coming to judge the earth! He will judge the world with righteousness, and the peoples with uprightness!

99.1: YHWH reigns, let the peoples tremble! He dwells above the cherubim, let the earth be shaken!
99.2: YHWH is great in Zion, and high is He over all the peoples.
99.3: Let them praise Your great and awesome name! Holy is He!
99.4: The mighty King loves justice, You have established uprightness; justice and righteousness You have done in Jacob.
99.5: Exalt YHWH, our God, and bow down at His footstool! Holy is He!
99.6: Moses and Aaron were among His priests, and Samuel among those calling on His name—they called to YHWH, and He answered them.
99.7: Within the pillar of cloud He spoke to them; they kept His testimonies, and the statute He gave to them.
99.8: O YHWH our God, You answered them; a forgiving God You became for them, and one taking vengeance over their misdeeds.
99.9: Exalt YHWH, our God, and bow down at the mountain of His holiness—for holy is YHWH our God!

100.1: A Psalm of thanksgiving. Shout to YHWH, all the earth.
100.2: Serve YHWH with gladness! Come before His face with a shout of joy!
100.3: Know that YHWH, He is God; He has made us and not we ourselves; we are His people and the sheep of His pasture.
100.4: Enter His gates with thanksgiving, His courts with praise; give thanks to Him; bless His name!
100.5: For YHWH is good; forever is His loving devotion, and unto generation and generation His faithfulness.

101.1: A Psalm. Of David. I will sing of loving devotion and justice; to You, O YHWH, I will sing praises.
101.2: I will act wisely in the way of integrity. When will You come to me? I walk about in the integrity of my heart, in the midst of my house.
101.3: I do not set before my eyes an object of worthlessness; I have hated the work of those turning aside—it shall not cling to me!
101.4: A perverse heart shall depart from me; evil I will not know.
101.5: The one slandering his neighbor in secret, I will put an end to him; the high of eyes and proud of heart—him I will not endure.
101.6: My eyes are on the faithful ones of the land, to dwell with me; the one walking in the way of integrity, he shall minister to me.
101.7: There shall not dwell within my house one working deceit; the one speaking lies shall not be fixed before my eyes.
101.8: At morning I cut off all the wicked of the land, to cut off from the city of YHWH all workers of iniquity!

102.1: A Prayer of one afflicted, when he grows weak and pours out his complaint before YHWH. O YHWH, may You hear my prayer and may my cry come to You!
102.2: Do not hide Your face from me, in the day of distress for me! Incline to me Your ear; on the day that I call may You hurry and answer me!
102.3: For my days come to an end like smoke, and my bones like a burning wick have been charred.
102.4: My heart has been struck like the grass and has withered, so that I forget to eat my bread.
102.5: From the voice of my groaning, my bone has clung to my flesh.
102.6: I am like a vulture of the desert; I have become like an owl of the wasteland.
102.7: I stay awake, and I am like a bird alone on the housetop.
102.8: All the day my enemies the taunt me; those shouting boastfully swear against me.
102.9: For I have eaten ashes like bread, and my drink I have mingled with weeping,
102.10: from the face of Your anger and Your wrath, for You have lifted me up and cast me away.
102.11: My days are like a shadow stretching out; and I, like the grass, wither away.
102.12: But You, O YHWH, dwell forever, and Your memorial to generation and generation.
102.13: You will rise up and have compassion on Zion—for the time to show her favor, indeed, the appointed time, has come.
102.14: How Your servants take delight in her stones—even her dust they favor!
102.15: And the nations will fear the name of YHWH, and all the kings of the earth Your glory.
102.16: For YHWH has built up Zion; He has appeared in His glory.
102.17: He has turned toward the prayer of the destitute, and He will not despise their prayer.
102.18: This will be written for the generation to come, and a people yet to be created will praise YAH.
102.19: For He looked down from the height of His sanctuary, YHWH looked out from the heavens to the earth,
102.20: to hear the groaning of the prisoner, to release the sons of death;
102.21: to declare in Zion the name of YHWH, and His praise in Jerusalem,
102.22: in the gathering of the peoples together—and the kingdoms—to serve YHWH.
102.23: He has broken my strength on the way; He has shortened my days.
102.24: I said, “O my God, do not take me up in the midst of my days; Your years are generation to generations.
102.25: Before time You founded the earth, and the heavens are the work of Your hands.
102.26: They will perish, but You will stand; and all of them will wear out like a garment—like clothing, You will pass them on, and they will pass away.
102.27: And You are the same, and Your years will not end.
102.28: The sons of Your servants will settle down, and their seed will be established before Your face.”

103.1: Of David. Bless, O my soul, YHWH, and all within me—His holy name!
103.2: O my soul, bless YHWH, and do not forget all His benefits—
103.3: the One forgiving all your iniquities, the One healing all your diseases,
103.4: the One redeeming your life from destruction, the One crowning you with loving devotion and compassion,
103.5: the One filling your beauty with the good; your youth is renewed like the eagle.
103.6: YHWH is doing righteousness and justice for all being oppressed.
103.7: He made known His ways to Moses, His deeds to the sons of Israel.
103.8: Compassionate and gracious is YHWH, slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion.
103.9: He will not contend forever, and He will not keep it forever.
103.10: He has not done to us according to our sins, and He has not dealt with us according to our iniquities.
103.11: For as the heavens are high above the earth, so His loving devotion is mighty for those fearing Him.
103.12: As far as the east is from the west, so has He put our transgressions far from us.
103.13: As a father has compassion for his children, so YHWH has compassion for those fearing Him.
103.14: For He knows our form; He remembers that we are dust.
103.15: A man—his days are like grass; like the flower of the field, so he blossoms;
103.16: For the wind passes over it, and it is no more; and its place does not recognize it anymore.
103.17: But the loving devotion of YHWH is from forever and even to forever upon those fearing Him, and His righteousness to the sons of the sons—
103.18: to those keeping His covenant, and to those remembering His precepts, to do them.
103.19: YHWH has established His throne in the heavens, and His kingdom rules over all.
103.20: Bless YHWH, His angels, mighty of strength, doing His word, to listen to the voice of His word.
103.21: Bless YHWH, all His hosts, His ministering ones doing His pleasure.
103.22: Bless YHWH, all His works, in all places of His dominion. O my soul, bless YHWH!

104.1: O my soul, bless YHWH! O YHWH my God, You are very great; with honor and majesty You are clothed.
104.2: Covering Himself with light as a garment, stretching out the heavens like a curtain,
104.3: the One laying the beams in the waters of His upper chambers, the One making the clouds His chariot, the One walking on the wings of the wind,
104.4: making the winds His messengers, His servants flaming fire.
104.5: He set the earth upon its foundations; it should not be shaken forever and ever.
104.6: The deep, as with a garment You covered it; upon the mountains the waters were standing.
104.7: From Your rebuke they fled; from the voice of Your thunder they hurried away.
104.8: Upon the mountains, the valleys sank down, to the place that You established for them.
104.9: You have set a boundary that they may not cross over; they may never return to cover the earth.
104.10: He sends the springs into the valleys; they flow between the hills.
104.11: They give drink to every beast of the field; the wild donkeys break their thirst.
104.12: Nearby them the birds of the heavens are dwelling; from between the branches they give forth a voice.
104.13: Watering the hills from His upper chambers; with the fruit of Your works the earth is satisfied—
104.14: causing the grass to sprout for the cattle, and vegetation for the service of man, to bring forth food from the earth,
104.15: and wine makes glad the heart of man, to make his face shine from oil, and bread supports the heart of man.
104.16: The trees of YHWH are satisfied, the cedars of Lebanon that He planted,
104.17: where in the birds make their nests; the stork—the cypresses are her house.
104.18: The high mountains are for the wild goats; the crags a refuge for the rock badgers.
104.19: He made the moon for appointed times; the sun knows its entrance.
104.20: You make darkness, and it becomes night; in it all the living things of the forest creep about.
104.21: The young lions roar for the prey and seek their food from God.
104.22: The sun rises, they are gathered; and in their dens they stretch out.
104.23: Man goes out to his work, and to his labor until the evening.
104.24: How many are Your works, O YHWH! All of them You have made in wisdom; the earth is full of Your creatures.
104.25: This is the sea, great and wide—in it are teeming things, and without number, living things small with the great.
104.26: There the ships go about, and Leviathan, which You formed to play in it.
104.27: All of them look to You, to give their food, in its season.
104.28: You give to them, and they gather in; You open Your hand, and they are satisfied with good.
104.29: You hide Your face—they are troubled; You gather their spirit—they die, and to their dust they return.
104.30: You send forth Your Spirit—they are created, and You renew the face of the earth.
104.31: May the glory of YHWH be forever; may YHWH rejoice in His works—
104.32: the One looking on the earth, and it trembles; He touches the mountains, and they smoke.
104.33: I will sing to YHWH during my life; I will sing praise to my God while I live !
104.34: May my melody be pleasing to Him; I rejoice in YHWH.
104.35: May the sinful ones be consumed from the earth, and the wicked shall not be anymore! O my soul, bless YHWH! Hallelu YAH!

105.1: Give thanks to YHWH; call on His name; make known among the nations His deeds!
105.2: Sing to Him, make music to Him; meditate on all His wonders!
105.3: Glory in the name of His holiness; let the heart of those seeking YHWH rejoice!
105.4: Search after YHWH and His strength; seek His face continually.
105.5: Remember His wonders that He has done—His wonders and the judgments of His mouth—
105.6: O seed of Abraham His servant, O sons of Jacob, His chosen ones!
105.7: He is YHWH our God; His judgments are over all the earth.
105.8: He remembers His covenant forever—the word He commanded for a thousand generations—
105.9: that which He cut with Abraham, and His oath to Isaac.
105.10: And He established it to Jacob as a decree, to Israel as a covenant forever,
105.11: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan, the portion of your? inheritance.”
105.12: In their being few in number, few indeed, and strangers in it,
105.13: and they went about from nation to nation, from a kingdom to another people.
105.14: He has permitted no man to oppress them, and He rebuked kings for them:
105.15: “You? shall not touch My anointed ones, and to My prophets You? shall not do evil.”
105.16: And He called down famine on the land; the whole staff of bread He has broken.
105.17: He sent before them a man, to be sold as a slave—Joseph.
105.18: They afflicted his feet with the fetters; into iron came his neck,
105.19: until the time of the coming of His word—the word of YHWH tried him.
105.20: The king sent and released him, and the ruler of the peoples drew him out.
105.21: He made him lord of his house, and ruler of all his substance,
105.22: to instruct his princes by his soul, and his elders to make wise.
105.23: And Israel came to Egypt, and Jacob resided in the land of Ham.
105.24: And His people He made exceedingly fruitful, and made them mightier than their enemies.
105.25: He turned their heart to hate His people, to act deceitfully with His servants.
105.26: He sent Moses His servant, Aaron whom He had chosen.
105.27: They set among them the words of His signs, and wonders in the land of Ham.
105.28: He sent darkness and it became dark, and they defied His words.
105.29: He turned their waters to blood, and He put to death their fish.
105.30: Their land swarmed with frogs, in the inner chambers of their kings.
105.31: He spoke, and the swarm came, gnats in all their country.
105.32: He gave hail for rain, flames of fire in their land.
105.33: And He struck their vines and their fig trees, and He shattered the trees of their country.
105.34: He spoke, and the locust came, and the young locust without number,
105.35: and it ate up every herb in their land, and it devoured the fruit of their ground.
105.36: And He struck all the firstborn in their land, the firstfruits of all their vigor.
105.37: And He brought them out with silver and gold, and there were none among His tribes stumbling.
105.38: Egypt was glad at their going out, for the dread of them had fallen upon them.
105.39: He spread a cloud for a screen, and fire to light up the night.
105.40: They asked, and He brought quail, and with the bread of the heavens He satisfied them.
105.41: He opened the rock, and waters gushed from it; they went forth in the desert like a river.
105.42: For He remembered the word of His holiness to Abraham His servant.
105.43: And He brought out His people with rejoicing, and His chosen with a shout of joy.
105.44: And He gave to them the lands of the nations, and they possessed the labor of the peoples,
105.45: that they may keep His statutes and obey His laws. Hallelu YAH!

106.1: Hallelujah! Give thanks to YHWH for He is good, for His loving devotion is forever!
106.2: Who can describe the mighty acts of YHWH? Who can sound all His praise?
106.3: Blessed are those guarding justice, doing righteousness at every time.
106.4: Remember me, O YHWH, in favor to Your people; attend to me with Your salvation,
106.5: to look on the prosperity of Your chosen ones, and to be glad in the gladness of Your nation, and to glory with Your inheritance.
106.6: We have sinned, with our fathers; we have been perverse; we have acted wickedly.
106.7: Our fathers in Egypt did not consider Your wonders; they did not remember the abundance of Your kindness; and they rebelled by the sea—at the Sea of Reeds.
106.8: And He saved them for the sake of His name, to make known His might.
106.9: And He rebuked the Sea of Reeds, and it dried up, and He made them walk through the depths as through a desert.
106.10: And He saved them from the hand of the one hating, and He redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
106.11: For the waters covered their enemies; not one of them remained.
106.12: And they had faith in His words; they sang His praise.
106.13: They were quick to forget His works; they have not wait for His counsel;
106.14: and they craved with desire in the wilderness, and they tested God in the desert.
106.15: And He gave to them their request, and He sent leanness upon their soul.
106.16: And they were jealous of Moses in the camp, of Aaron, the holy one of YHWH.
106.17: The earth opened up and swallowed Dathan, and it covered over the company of Abiram.
106.18: And a fire was kindled in their company; the flame consumed the wicked.
106.19: They made a calf in Horeb, and they bowed down to a molten image.
106.20: And they exchanged their Glory for the image of an ox eating grass.
106.21: They forgot God their Savior, the One having done great things in Egypt,
106.22: wonders in the land of Ham, fearful things by the Sea of Reeds.
106.23: And he said he would destroy them, had not Moses his chosen one stood in the breach before His face, to turn away His fury from destroying them.
106.24: Then they despised the land of the desire; they were not faithful to His word,
106.25: and they grumbled in their tents; they did not listen to the voice of YHWH.
106.26: And He lifted up His hand against them to make them fall in the desert,
106.27: and to make their seed fall among the nations, and to scatter them in the lands.
106.28: They even joined themselves to Baal-Peor, and they ate sacrifices to the dead.
106.29: And they provoked Him to anger with their deeds, and a plague broke out among them.
106.30: And Phinehas stood up and intervened, and the plague was restrained.
106.31: And it was reckoned to him as righteousness, to generation and generation, even to forever.
106.32: And they caused wrath at the waters of Meribah, and it was evil to Moses on account of them.
106.33: For they rebelled against his spirit, and he spoke rashly with his lips.
106.34: They did not destroy the peoples whom YHWH had given to them the order.
106.35: And they mingled with the nations, and they learned their works.
106.36: And they served their idols, and they were to them as a snare.
106.37: And they sacrificed their sons and their daughters to the demons;
106.38: and they shed innocent blood, the blood of their sons and their daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan, and the land was polluted with the blood.
106.39: And they were defiled by their deeds and prostituted themselves by their practices.
106.40: And the anger of YHWH was kindled against His people, and He came to abhor His inheritance.
106.41: And He gave them into the hand of the nations, and those hating them ruled over them.
106.42: And their enemies oppressed them, and they were humbled under their hand.
106.43: Many times He delivered them, and they were rebellious in their counsel, and were brought low in their iniquity.
106.44: And He looked on the distress to them in His hearing their cry.
106.45: And He remembered unto them His covenant, and He relented by the abundance of His loving devotion.
106.46: And He appointed them for mercies before the face of all their captors.
106.47: Save us, O YHWH our God, and gather us from the nations, to give thanks to the name of Your holiness, to glory in Your praise!
106.48: Blessed be YHWH the God of Israel from forever and even to forever. And let all the people say, “Amen!” Praise YAH!

107.1: Give thanks to YHWH, for He is good, for His loving devotion is forever!
107.2: Let the redeemed of YHWH say, whom He has redeemed from the hand of the enemy,
107.3: and from the lands He has gathered them—from the east and from the west, from the north and from the sea.
107.4: They wandered in the wilderness, by the way in a desert; a city for of dwelling they did not find.
107.5: Hungry and thirsty, their soul in them grew faint.
107.6: And they cried out to YHWH in the distress for them; from their straits He delivered them.
107.7: And He made them tread on a straight path, to walk toward a city of dwelling.
107.8: Let them give thanks to YHWH for His loving devotion, and His wonders to the sons of man!
107.9: For He satisfies the rushing soul, and the hungry soul He fills with goodness.
107.10: They are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, prisoners in affliction and irons,
107.11: because they rebelled against the words of God, and they despised the counsel of the Most High,
107.12: and He humbled with labor their heart; they fell down, and no one was helping.
107.13: And they cried out to YHWH in the distress for them, and from their straits He saved them.
107.14: He brought them forth from darkness and the shadow of death, and their bonds He broke away.
107.15: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His loving devotion, and His wonders to the sons of man!
107.16: For He has broken down doors of bronze, and He has cut through bars of iron!
107.17: Fools from the path of their transgression, and by their iniquities they were afflicted.
107.18: All food their soul abhorred, and they drew near to the gates of death.
107.19: And they cried out to YHWH in the distress for them, and from their straits He saved them.
107.20: He sent His word and healed them, and He rescued them from the Pit.
107.21: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His loving devotion, and His wonders to the sons of man!
107.22: And let them sacrifice sacrifices of thanksgiving, and recount His works with rejoicing!
107.23: Those going down to the sea in ships, doing business on the great waters,
107.24: they have seen the works of YHWH, and His wonders in the deep.
107.25: And He spoke and stationed the wind, a tempest, and it lifted high the waves;
107.26: they went up to the heavens, they went down to the depths; their soul melted in evil.
107.27: They reel and stagger like the drunkard, and all their wisdom is swallowed up.
107.28: And they cried out to YHWH in the distress for them, and from their straits He brought them out.
107.29: He stilled the tempest to a whisper, and the waves of the sea were hushed.
107.30: And they rejoiced because they grew quiet, and He guided them to the haven of their desire.
107.31: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His loving devotion and His wonders to the sons of man!
107.32: And let them exalt Him in the assembly of the people, and in the seat of the elders praise Him!
107.33: He turns rivers into a wilderness, and outpourings of waters into thirsty ground,
107.34: a land of fruit into salty ground, from the wickedness of those dwelling in it.
107.35: He turns the desert into pools of water, and a land of dryness into springs of waters.
107.36: And the hungry He makes to dwell there, that they may establish a city for a dwelling place.
107.37: And there they sow fields and plant vineyards, that they may yield the fruit of produce.
107.38: And He blesses them and they multiply exceedingly, and their cattle He does not let diminish.
107.39: When they are diminished and brought low from oppression, evil, and sorrow,
107.40: He pours out contempt on nobles, and causes them to wander in a wasteland without a path.
107.41: And He sets on high for the needy from affliction, and He places families as a flock.
107.42: Let the upright see and rejoice, and may all iniquity shut its mouth.
107.43: And him who is wise, let him attend to these things, and they will consider the loving devotion of YHWH.

108.1: A Song. A Psalm of David. My heart being firm, O God, I will sing and make music, even with my glory!
108.2: Awake, O harp and lyre! I will awaken the dawn!
108.3: I will praise You among the peoples, O YHWH, and I will sing praises to You among the nations.
108.4: For great over the heavens is Your loving devotion, and up to the clouds is Your faithfulness.
108.5: Be exalted above the heavens, O God, and over all the earth Your glory!
108.6: That Your beloved may be delivered, save with Your right hand, and answer me!
108.7: God has spoken from His holy place: “I will exult; I will divide Shechem, and measure out the Valley of Succoth.
108.8: Unto Me is Gilead, unto Me is Manasseh; and Ephraim is the strength of My head, Judah is My scepter.
108.9: Moab is the basin of My washing; upon Edom I will cast My sandal; over Philistia I will shout in triumph.”
108.10: Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me as far as Edom?
108.11: Have You not, O God, cast us off? And will You no longer go forth, O God, with our armies?
108.12: May You give to us help from adversity, and the salvation of man is emptiness.
108.13: With God we will act in strength, and He will trample our adversaries.

109.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. O God of my praise, do not be silent!
109.2: For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of the deceitful, they have opened against me—they speak against me with a tongue of lies;
109.3: and with words of hatred they surround me, and they fight me without cause.
109.4: In exchange for my love they accuse me, and I am of prayer;
109.5: and they set against me evil for good, and hatred for my love.
109.6: Appoint a wicked man over him, and let an adversary stand at his right hand.
109.7: In his being judged, may he go forth as wicked, and let his prayer come to be as sin.
109.8: May his days be few; may another take his position!
109.9: May his sons be fatherless, and his wife a widow.
109.10: And let his sons wander continually and beg, and let them seek sustenance from their desolate places!
109.11: Let the creditor lay a snare for all that is unto him, and let strangers plunder his labour.
109.12: None shall there be for him extending kindness, and none shall there be showing favor to his fatherless.
109.13: Let his posterity be for cutting off; in the next generation let their name be blotted out!
109.14: May the iniquity of his fathers be remembered to YHWH, and the sin of his mother not be blotted out.
109.15: Let them be before YHWH continually, that He may cut off from the earth their memory!
109.16: Because that he has not remembered to do kindness, and he pursued the poor and needy man, and the broken of heart to kill him .
109.17: And he loved cursing, and let it come to him; and he did not delight in blessing, and let it be far from him!
109.18: And he clothed himself with cursing as his garment, and it came in like the water within him, and like the oil into his bones!
109.19: May it be for him like the garment wrapped about him, and continually as a belt he girds on.
109.20: May this be the reward of my accusers from YHWH, and of those speaking evil against my soul!
109.21: But You, O YHWH my Lord, may you act with me for the sake of Your name; because of the goodness of Your loving devotion, deliver me!
109.22: For I am poor and needy, and my heart is pierced within me.
109.23: Like a shadow in its lengthening, I am gone; I am shaken off like the locust.
109.24: My knees wobble from fasting, and my flesh has failed of fatness.
109.25: And I have become an object of scorn for them; they see me; they shake their head.
109.26: Help me, O YHWH my God; save me according to Your loving devotion,
109.27: that they may know that this is Your hand; You, O YHWH, have done it!
109.28: They may curse, but You will bless; may they rise up, and be put to shame, and may Your servant rejoice!
109.29: May my accusers be clothed with disgrace, and may they be covered with their shame as the robe.
109.30: I will confess YHWH greatly with my mouth, and in the midst of the multitudes I will praise Him.
109.31: For He shall stand at the right hand of the poor, to save him from those judging his soul.

110.1: A Psalm of David. An utterance of YHWH to my Lord: “Sit at My right hand, until I make your enemies a footstool for Your feet.”
110.2: YHWH sends forth the rod of your strength from Zion: “Rule in the midst of Your enemies!”
110.3: Your people are freewill offerings in the day of your might, in the splendors of holiness; from the womb of the morning, the dew of your youth is unto you.
110.4: YHWH has sworn, and He will not change His mind: “You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek.”
110.5: My Lord is at Your right hand; he will shatter kings in the day of his anger;
110.6: He will judge among the nations, he will fill it with corpses; he will wound the head of many lands;
110.7: from the brook by the way He will drink. By this He will lift up the head.

111.1: Hallelu YAH! I will give thanks to YHWH with all of my heart, in the council of the upright and in the assembly.
111.2: Great are the works of YHWH, being searched out by all who delight in them.
111.3: Splendor and majesty are His work, and His righteousness stands forever.
111.4: A memorial He has made by His wonders; gracious and compassionate is YHWH.
111.5: Food He has given to those fearing Him; He remembers His covenant forever.
111.6: The power of His works He has declared to His people, by giving to them the inheritance of the nations.
111.7: The works of His hands are truth and justice, and all His precepts are trustworthy.
111.8: They are upheld for ever everlasting; they are made in truth and uprightness.
111.9: Redemption He has sent to His people; He has appointed His covenant forever; Holy and awesome is His name!
111.10: The beginning of wisdom is the fear of YHWH; good understanding is unto all those doing it. May His praise be enduring forever!

112.1: Hallelu YAH! Blessed is the man fearing YHWH; in His commandments he has delighted greatly.
112.2: His seed is mighty in the land; the generation of the upright will be blessed.
112.3: Wealth and riches are in his house, and his righteousness stands forever.
112.4: Light has risen in the darkness for the upright— for the gracious, and compassionate, and righteous.
112.5: The good man is gracious and lends freely; he contains his affairs with justice.
112.6: Surely he will not be shaken forever; the righteous will be as a memorial forever.
112.7: Of an evil report he is not afraid; his heart is firm, trusting in YHWH.
112.8: His heart is upheld; he will not be afraid, until that he looks on his enemies.
112.9: He has scattered—he has given to the needy; his righteousness stands forever; his horn is raised high in honor.
112.10: A wicked one will see and be angry; he will gnash his teeth and melt away; the desire of the wicked ones will perish.

113.1: Hallelu YAH! Praise, O servants of YHWH, praise the name of YHWH!
113.2: The name of YHWH be blessed, from now and even to forever!
113.3: From the rising of the sun to its coming in, praised is the name of YHWH!
113.4: High over all nations is YHWH, and over the heavens His glory,
113.5: who is like YHWH our God, being on high to dwell,
113.6: stooping down to look on the heavens and on the earth,
113.7: raising up the poor from the dust. He lifts high the needy one from the ash heap,
113.8: to make him sit with princes, with the princes of His people,
113.9: settling in a home the barren woman, joyful like a mother of sons. Praise YAH!

114.1: In the going forth of Israel from Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people speaking strangely,
114.2: Judah became His sanctuary, Israel His dominion.
114.3: The sea saw Him and fled; the Jordan turned to the back.
114.4: The mountains skipped like rams, the hills like sons of the flock.
114.5: What was it to you, O sea, that you fled, O Jordan, that you turned back,
114.6: O mountains, that you? skipped like rams, O hills, like sons of the flock?
114.7: Tremble before the face of the Lord, O earth, before the face of the God of Jacob—
114.8: turning the rock into a pool of waters, the flint into a spring of waters!

115.1: Not to us, O YHWH, not to us, but to Your name, give glory, because of Your loving devotion, because of Your faithfulness!
115.2: Why should the nations say, “Where now is their God?”
115.3: And our God is in the heavens; all that he has pleased, He has done.
115.4: Their idols are silver and gold, the work of the hands of man.
115.5: A mouth is unto them, and they do not speak; eyes are unto them, and they do not see;
115.6: ears are unto them, and they do not hear; noses are unto them, and they do not smell;
115.7: they have hands and they do not feel; they have feet and they do not walk; and they do not utter by their throat.
115.8: Those making them shall be like them, everyone who is trusting in them.
115.9: O Israel, trust in YHWH! And He is their help and their shield.
115.10: O House of Aaron, trust in YHWH! And He is their help and their shield.
115.11: O fearers of YHWH, trust in YHWH! He is their help and their shield.
115.12: YHWH has remembered us; He will bless; He will bless the house of Israel, He will bless the house of Aaron.
115.13: He will bless those fearing YHWH, the small with the great.
115.14: YHWH adds to you?—to you? and to your? sons!
115.15: May you? be blessed by YHWH, maker of the heavens and the earth!
115.16: The heavens of the heavens are unto YHWH, and the earth He has given to the sons of man.
115.17: The dead do not praise YAH, and not any of those going down into silence.
115.18: And we will bless YAH, from now and even to forever. Hallelu YAH!

116.1: I have loved because YHWH has heard the voice of my supplications,
116.2: Because He has inclined His ear to me, even in the day I called.
116.3: The cords of death surrounded me, and the straits of Sheol found me; distress and sorrow did I find.
116.4: And in the name of YHWH I called out: “I pray, O YHWH, deliver my soul!”
116.5: Gracious is YHWH, and righteous; and our God is compassionate.
116.6: YHWH preserves the simple ones; I was brought low, and to me He gave salvation.
116.7: Return my soul, to your rest, for YHWH has dealt kindly with you.
116.8: For you were rescued from death, O my soul, my eye from tears, my feet from stumbling.
116.9: I will walk before the face of YHWH in the land of the living.
116.10: I believed, therefore I said, “I afflicted am greatly.”
116.11: I said in my hastening, “Every man is a liar.”
116.12: What shall I return to YHWH; all His benefits are upon me?
116.13: I will take up the cup of salvation, and upon the name of YHWH I will call.
116.14: I will fulfill My vows to YHWH now in front of all His people.
116.15: Precious in the eyes of YHWH is the death of His holy ones.
116.16: I ask, O YHWH—surely I am Your servant; I am Your servant, son of Your maidservant—You have loosed my bonds!
116.17: To You I will sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving, and in your name, O YHWH, I will call.
116.18: I will fulfill My vows to YHWH now in front of all His people,
116.19: in the courts of the house of YHWH, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Hallelu YAH!

117.1: Praise YHWH, all you nations; adore Him, all you peoples!
117.2: For mighty is His loving devotion toward us, and the faithfulness of YHWH is forever. Hallelu YAH!

118.1: Give thanks to YHWH, for He is good, for His loving devotion is forever!
118.2: Now let Israel say that His loving devotion is forever!
118.3: Now let the house of Aaron say that His loving devotion is forever!
118.4: Now let those fearing YHWH say that His loving devotion is forever!
118.5: In distress I called on YAH; YAH answered me in a broad place.
118.6: YHWH is with me; I will not fear—what can man do to me?
118.7: YHWH is with me as the One helping me; and I shall look on those hating me.
118.8: It is better to take refuge in YHWH, than to trust in man.
118.9: It is better to take refuge in YHWH, than to trust in princes.
118.10: All nations surrounded me; in the name of YHWH I surely cut them off.
118.11: They surrounded me, yes, they surrounded me, and in the name of YHWH I surely cut them off!
118.12: They have surrounded me like bees, they have been extinguished as fire among thorns; and in the name of YHWH I surely cut them off!
118.13: You have severely thrust me that I might fall, and YHWH helped me.
118.14: My strength and song is YAH; and He has become to me salvation.
118.15: The voice of rejoicing and salvation is in the tents of the righteous: “The right hand of YHWH acts with strength.
118.16: The right hand of YHWH is exalted; the right hand of YHWH acts with strength.”
118.17: I will not die, but I will live, that I might recount the works of YAH.
118.18: Yah, chastened me severely, and to death He has not given me up.
118.19: Open to me the gates of righteousness; I will enter into them; I will give thanks to YAH.
118.20: This is the gate of YHWH; the righteous shall enter into it.
118.21: I thank You, for You have answered me, and You have become to me for salvation.
118.22: The stone the builders rejected has become as the head of the corner.
118.23: From YHWH this is; it is marvelous in our eyes.
118.24: This is the day YHWH has made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.
118.25: I ask, O YHWH, save us now. I ask, O YHWH, send prosperity now!
118.26: Blessed is the one coming in the name of YHWH; we bless you? from the house of YHWH.
118.27: YHWH is God, and He has given light to us! Bind the festal sacrifice with cords to the horns of the altar!
118.28: You are my God, and I will give You thanks; my God, I will exalt You!
118.29: Give thanks to YHWH, for He is good; for His loving devotion is forever!

119.1: Blessed are those complete in the way, those walking in the law of YHWH.
119.2: Blessed are those keeping His testimonies; with all the heart they seek Him.
119.3: Also they do not do injustice; in His ways they walk.
119.4: You have commanded Your precepts, to be kept diligently.
119.5: Oh, that my ways were set firm to keep Your statutes!
119.6: Then I would not be ashamed, in my looking to all Your commandments.
119.7: I will praise You with uprightness of heart, in my learning the judgments of Your righteousness.
119.8: Your statutes I will keep; may You not even to exceedingly forsake me!
119.9: How can a young man keep pure his way? By guarding it according to Your word.
119.10: With all my heart I have sought You; do not let me stray from Your commandments.
119.11: In my heart I have hidden Your word, that I should not sin against You.
119.12: Blessed are You, O YHWH; teach me Your statutes.
119.13: With my lips I have recounted all the judgments of Your mouth.
119.14: In the way of Your testimonies I rejoice, more than in all wealth.
119.15: May I meditate on Your precepts and look upon Your paths.
119.16: In Your statutes I will delight myself; I will not forget Your word.
119.17: Deal bountifully with Your servant, that I may live and keep Your word.
119.18: Open my eyes, that I may see the wonders from Your law.
119.19: I am a sojourner on the earth; do not hide from me Your commandments!
119.20: My soul is crushed with longing for Your judgments at all times.
119.21: You rebuke the arrogant ones, the accursed ones, those going astray from Your commandments.
119.22: Remove from me scorn and contempt, for I have kept Your testimonies.
119.23: Though princes sit against me and slander, Your servant meditates on Your statutes.
119.24: Your testimonies also are my delight—the men of my counsel.
119.25: My soul clings to the dust; revive me according to Your word!
119.26: My ways I recounted, and You answered me; teach me Your statutes.
119.27: Make me understand the way of Your precepts, that I may meditate on Your wonders.
119.28: My soul drips from sorrow; establish me according to Your word!
119.29: Turn away from me the path of deceit, and with Your law show favor to me.
119.30: The way of truth I have chosen; Your ordinances I have set.
119.31: I cling to Your testimonies, O YHWH; let me not be put to shame!
119.32: The way of Your commandments I will run, for You will enlarge my heart.
119.33: Teach me, O YHWH, the way of Your statutes, and I will keep it to the end.
119.34: Give me understanding, and I will guard Your law, and I will keep it with all my heart.
119.35: Cause me to tread in the path of Your commandments, for in it I delight.
119.36: Incline my heart toward Your testimonies, and not toward dishonest gain.
119.37: Make my eyes pass from seeing the vain thing ; with Your word revive me.
119.38: Make Your word stand for Your servant, which is for the fearing of You.
119.39: Remove the reproach that I dread, for Your judgments are good.
119.40: Behold, I long for Your precepts; Revive me in Your righteousness!
119.41: And may Your loving devotion come to me, O YHWH, Your salvation according to Your word.
119.42: Then I will answer with a word for the one reproaching me, for I trust in Your word.
119.43: And do not take away from my mouth the word of truth even to exceedingly! For in Your judgments I have hoped.
119.44: And I will keep Your law continually, forever and ever,
119.45: and I will walk in a broad place, for Your precepts I have sought.
119.46: And I will speak of Your testimonies before kings, and I will not be ashamed.
119.47: And I will delight myself in Your commandments, which I have loved.
119.48: And I will lift up my hands to Your commandments, which I have loved, and I will meditate on Your statutes.
119.49: Remember the word to Your servant, upon that You have caused me to hope.
119.50: This is my comfort in my affliction, for Your word has given me life.
119.51: The arrogant ones mock me even to exceedingly; from Your law I do not stretch away.
119.52: I remember Your judgments from old, O YHWH, and I am comforted.
119.53: Indignation has seized me, from the wicked forsaking Your law.
119.54: Your statutes have been songs to me, in the house of my sojourning.
119.55: I remember Your name in the night, O YHWH, and I keep Your law.
119.56: This has come to be for me, for I have kept Your precepts.
119.57: YHWH is my portion; I have said I would keep Your words.
119.58: I appeased Your face with all my heart; be gracious to me according to Your word.
119.59: I have considered my paths, and turned back my feet to Your testimonies.
119.60: I hurried and did not delay to keep Your commandments.
119.61: The cords of the wicked have surrounded me, Your law I have not forgotten.
119.62: In the middle of the night, I rise to give thanks to You, upon the judgments of Your righteousness.
119.63: I am a companion to all who fear You, and to those keeping Your precepts.
119.64: O YHWH, with Your loving devotion the earth is full; teach me Your statutes.
119.65: You have done well with Your servant, O YHWH, according to Your word.
119.66: So may You teach me good judgment and knowledge, for in Your commandments I have believed.
119.67: Before I was afflicted, I was going astray; and now I have kept Your word.
119.68: You are good, and the One doing good; may You teach me Your statutes.
119.69: The arrogant ones have smeared a lie over me; I, with all my heart, will guard Your precepts.
119.70: Their heart is unfeeling like fat; I have delighted in Your law.
119.71: It is good for me that I was afflicted, so that I might learn Your statutes.
119.72: The law from Your mouth is better to me than thousands of gold and silver.
119.73: Your hands have made me and established me; give me understanding to learn Your commandments.
119.74: Those fearing You see me and rejoice, because in Your word I have hoped.
119.75: I know, O YHWH, that Your judgments are righteous, and in faithfulness You have afflicted me.
119.76: Your loving devotion, may it be, please, to comfort me, according to Your word to Your servant.
119.77: May Your compassions come to me, that I may live, for Your law is my delight.
119.78: May the arrogant ones be put to shame, for with a lie they dealt perversely with me; I will meditate on Your precepts.
119.79: Let those who fear You turn to me, and those who know Your testimonies.
119.80: May my heart be perfect in Your statutes, so that I may not be ashamed.
119.81: My soul faints for Your salvation; for Your word I wait.
119.82: My eyes fail for your word, saying, “When will You comfort me?”
119.83: For, I have become like a wineskin in smoke; Your statutes I have not forgotten.
119.84: How many are the days of Your servant? When will You execute judgment on my pursuers?
119.85: The arrogant ones have dug pits for me that are not according to Your law.
119.86: All Your commandments are firm, falsely they pursue me—Help me!
119.87: Almost they consumed me on the earth; and I have not forsaken Your precepts.
119.88: According to Your loving devotion, revive me, that I may keep the testimony of Your mouth.
119.89: Forever, O YHWH, is Your word, standing fixed in the heavens.
119.90: For generation and generation is Your faithfulness; You established the earth, and it stands.
119.91: According to Your ordinances, they stand this day, for all things are Your servants.
119.92: Had Your law not been my delight, then I would have perished in my affliction.
119.93: Forever, I will not forget Your precepts, for by them You have revived me.
119.94: Unto You I am—save me! For I have sought Your precepts.
119.95: The wicked wait for me to destroy me; Your testimonies I will consider.
119.96: To all perfection I have seen an end; broad is Your commandment exceedingly.
119.97: Oh, how I love Your law! All the day, it is my meditation.
119.98: By Your commandment You make me wiser than my enemies; for, it is with me forever.
119.99: I have insight above all my teachers, for Your testimonies are a meditation to me.
119.100: I understand above elders, for Your precepts I have kept.
119.101: From every path of evil I have restrained my feet, that I may keep Your word.
119.102: From Your ordinances I have not turned aside, for You have instructed me.
119.103: How smooth are Your words to my palate, more than honey to my mouth!
119.104: From Your precepts I gain understanding; upon thus, I hate every path of deception!
119.105: Your word is a lamp to my feet, and a light to my path.
119.106: I have sworn and confirmed: I will keep the judgments of Your righteousness.
119.107: I am afflicted even exceedingly, O YHWH; revive me according to Your word!
119.108: May You accept the freewill offerings of my mouth, please, O YHWH, and may You teach me Your judgments.
119.109: My soul is in my hand continually, and I do not forget Your law.
119.110: The wicked have set a snare for me, and from Your precepts I do not go astray.
119.111: I have inherited Your testimonies forever; for they are the joy of my heart.
119.112: I have inclined my heart to perform Your statutes forever, to the end!
119.113: The double-minded I hate, and Your law I love.
119.114: You are my hiding place and my shield; in Your word I hope.
119.115: Turn away from me, O evildoers! And I will keep the commandments of my God.
119.116: Uphold me according to Your word, and l will live; and do not make me ashamed of my hope!
119.117: Support me, and I will be saved; and I will gaze on Your statutes continually.
119.118: You have trodden down all those going astray from Your statutes, for their treachery is falsehood.
119.119: Like dross You put an end to all the wicked of the earth; therefore I love Your testimonies.
119.120: My flesh bristles from dread of You, and of your judgments I have been afraid.
119.121: I have done justice and righteousness; do not leave me to my oppressors!
119.122: Ensure Your servant for good; do not let the arrogant ones oppress me.
119.123: My eyes fail for Your salvation, and for the word of Your righteousness.
119.124: Deal with Your servant according to Your loving devotion, and teach me Your statutes.
119.125: I am Your servant—give me understanding, that I may know Your testimonies.
119.126: It is time, O YHWH, to act; they have broken Your law!
119.127: Upon thus, I love Your commandments more than gold, and more than fine gold.
119.128: Upon thus, all the precepts I have declared wholly right; every path of deception I hate.
119.129: Wonderful are Your testimonies; upon thus, my soul has kept watch over them.
119.130: The opening of Your words gives light, giving understanding to the simple ones.
119.131: I have opened my mouth and I pant—for I have longed for Your commandments!
119.132: Turn to me and be gracious to me, according to justice to those loving Your name.
119.133: Establish my steps by Your word, and do not let any iniquity rule over me.
119.134: Redeem me from the oppression of man, that I may keep Your precepts.
119.135: Make Your face shine on Your servant, and teach me Your statutes.
119.136: Streams of waters run down from my eyes, because they have not kept Your law.
119.137: You are righteous, O YHWH, and Your judgments are upright.
119.138: You have appointed your righteous testimonies, and exceedingly faithful.
119.139: My zeal has cut me off, because my adversaries have forgotten Your words.
119.140: Your word is exceedingly pure, and Your servant has loved it.
119.141: I am little and despised; I do not forget Your precepts.
119.142: Your righteousness is righteous forever, and Your law is truth.
119.143: Trouble and distress have found me; Your commandments are my delight.
119.144: The righteousness of Your testimonies is forever; give me understanding, that I may live!
119.145: I cry out with all my heart; answer me, O YHWH! Your statutes I will guard.
119.146: I call out to You, save me, that I may keep Your testimonies!
119.147: I went ahead at the dawn, and I cried out, and for Your word I have hoped.
119.148: My eyes have gone before the watches to meditate on Your word.
119.149: Hear my voice, O YHWH, according to Your loving devotion; according to Your justice, preserve my life!
119.150: They draw near, those pursuing wickedness! From Your law they are far.
119.151: You are near, O YHWH, and all Your commandments are truth.
119.152: I have known of Your testimonies of old, that forever You have founded them.
119.153: See my affliction and rescue me! For I have not forgotten Your law.
119.154: Plead my plea and redeem me; according to Your word, revive me!
119.155: Far from the wicked is salvation, because they have not not sought Your statutes.
119.156: Your mercies are great, O YHWH; according to Your ordinances, revive me!
119.157: Many are my pursuers and my adversaries; from Your testimonies I have not turned aside.
119.158: I have seen those acting treacherously, and I felt loathing, because they have not kept Your word.
119.159: See, how I love Your precepts, O YHWH; according to Your loving devotion, revive me!
119.160: The head of Your word is truth, and every judgment of Your righteousness is forever.
119.161: Princes may pursue me without cause, and of Your word my heart was afraid.
119.162: I am rejoicing over Your word like one finding great plunder.
119.163: Deception I hate and abhor; Your law I do love.
119.164: Seven times in the day I praise You, because of the judgments of Your righteousness.
119.165: Great prosperity is unto those loving Your law, and nothing to them is a stumbling block.
119.166: I wait for Your salvation, O YHWH, and Your commandments I have done.
119.167: My soul has kept Your testimonies, and I love them exceedingly.
119.168: I have kept Your precepts and Your testimonies, because all my ways are before You.
119.169: Let my cry come before You, O YHWH; according to Your word, give me understanding.
119.170: Let my supplication come before You; according to Your word, deliver me.
119.171: Let my lips pour forth praise, for You teach me Your statutes.
119.172: Let my tongue sing out Your word, for all Your commandments are righteous.
119.173: Let Your hand be to help me, for Your precepts I have chosen.
119.174: I long for Your salvation, O YHWH, and Your law is my delight.
119.175: Let my soul live, and it shall praise You; and may Your judgments help me.
119.176: I have gone astray, getting lost like a sheep; may You seek Your servant, for Your commandments I have not forgotten.

120.1: A Song of the Ascents. To YHWH, in the distress for me, I cried out, and He answered me.
120.2: O YHWH, deliver my soul from a lip of falsehood, from a tongue of deceit!
120.3: What will He give to you, or what will He add to you, O tongue of deceit?
120.4: The sharpened arrows of a mighty one, with the burning coals of broom trees!
120.5: Woe to me for I have sojourned in Mesech; I have dwelt among the tents of Kedar!
120.6: Too much has dwelt my soul with the one hating peace!
120.7: I am peace, and when I speak, they are for war.

121.1: A Song for the Ascents. I lift up my eyes to the hills, from where my help does come.
121.2: My help is from YHWH, the Maker of the heavens and the earth.
121.3: He will not give your foot to the slipping; the One guarding you will not slumber.
121.4: Behold, He does not slumber, and He does not sleep—the One guarding Israel.
121.5: YHWH is the One guarding you; YHWH is your shade on your right hand.
121.6: By day the sun will not strike you, nor the moon in the night.
121.7: YHWH will guard you from all evil; He will guard your soul.
121.8: YHWH will guard your going out and your coming in, from now even to forever.

122.1: A Song of the Ascents. Of David. I was glad at the saying to me, “Let us go to the house of YHWH.”
122.2: Our feet have been standing within your gates, O Jerusalem!
122.3: Jerusalem is built up as a city joined to itself together;
122.4: there the tribes go up, the tribes of YAH, as a testimony for Israel, to give thanks to the name of YHWH.
122.5: For there sit the thrones of judgment, the thrones of the house of David.
122.6: Ask for the peace of Jerusalem: “May they prosper, those loving you!
122.7: May there be peace within your wall, prosperity within your citadels!”
122.8: For the sake of my brothers and my companions let me now say: “Peace be within you!”
122.9: For the sake of the house of YHWH our God I will seek good for you.

123.1: A Song of the Ascents. Unto You I lift up my eyes, to the One sitting in the heavens.
123.2: Behold, as the eyes of servants are to the hand of their masters, as the eyes of a maidservant are to the hand of her mistress, so are our eyes to YHWH our God, until He shows us mercy.
123.3: Have mercy on us, O YHWH, Have mercy on us! For we are filled exceedingly with contempt.
123.4: Exceedingly our soul has been filled with the scorn of those at ease, with the contempt of the prideful ones.

124.1: A Song of the Ascents. Of David. If YHWH had not been for us—now let Israel say—
124.2: if YHWH had not been for us, in the rising up of men against us,
124.3: then they would have swallowed us alive, in the burning of their anger against us;
124.4: then the waters would have engulfed us, the stream would have passed over our soul;
124.5: then would have passed over our soul the raging waters.
124.6: Blessed be YHWH, who has not given us as prey to their teeth!
124.7: Our soul like a bird has escaped from the snare of the fowlers; the snare is broken, and we have slipped away.
124.8: Our help is in the name of YHWH, the Maker of the heavens and the earth.

125.1: A Song of the Ascents. Those trusting in YHWH are like Mount Zion—it cannot be shaken; it abides forever.
125.2: Jerusalem, the mountains surround her, and YHWH surrounds His people, from now even to forever.
125.3: For the rod of the wicked shall not rest upon the lot of the righteous, so that the righteous do not stretch out their hands to injustice.
125.4: Do good, O YHWH, to the good, and to the upright in their hearts!
125.5: And those turning to their crooked ways, YHWH will lead them away with the workers of iniquity! Peace be upon Israel!

126.1: A Song of the Ascents. In YHWH turning back the captivity of Zion, we were like those dreaming.
126.2: Then our mouth was filled with laughter, and our tongue with a shout of joy. Then they said among the nations, “YHWH, has done great things for these.”
126.3: YHWH has done great things for us; and we came to be glad.
126.4: Bring back, O YHWH, our captives, like streams in the Negev!
126.5: Those sowing in tears will reap with a shout of joy.
126.6: Going forth, he who goes and weeps, bearing the basket of seed, coming in, he comes with a shout, bearing his sheaves.

127.1: A Song of the Ascents. Of Solomon. If YHWH does not build the house, in vain do its builders labor on it; if YHWH does not guard the city, in vain the one guarding keeps watch.
127.2: It is vain for you?, being early in rising, and delaying to sit, eating the bread of sorrows; for so He gives sleep to His beloved.
127.3: Behold, an inheritance of YHWH are sons, a reward is the fruit of the womb.
127.4: Like arrows in the hand of a mighty one, so are the sons of one’s youth.
127.5: Blessed is the man who has filled his quiver with them! He will not be put to shame, but he shall speak with the enemies at the gate.

128.1: A Song of the Ascents. Blessed is everyone who fears YHWH, those walking in His ways!
128.2: The labor of your hands, surely you shall eat; blessings and good are unto you.
128.3: Your wife is fruitful like a vine in the sides of your house, your sons like shoots of olive trees round about your table.
128.4: Behold, how thus is blessed the man fearing YHWH.
128.5: May YHWH bless you from Zion, that you may look on the good of Jerusalem all the days of your life,
128.6: and may you see the sons of your sons! Peace be upon Israel!

129.1: A Song of the Ascents. Often they have distressed me from my youth—now let Israel say—
129.2: Often they have distressed me from my youth, yet they have not prevailed against me.
129.3: Upon my back, the plowmen were plowing; they have made long their furrows.
129.4: YHWH the Righteous One, may He cut off the cords of the wicked!
129.5: May they be put to shame and turned back, all those hating Zion!
129.6: May they be like grass on the rooftops that has withered before it can grow,
129.7: unable to fill the hand of one reaping, or the bosom of one binding sheaves.
129.8: And neither let those passing by say, “The blessing of YHWH be upon you?; we bless you? in the name of YHWH!”

130.1: A Song of the Ascents. Out of the depths I have called out to You, O YHWH!
130.2: O Lord, listen to my voice! Let Your ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications!
130.3: If You were to mark iniquities, O YAH, O Lord, who could stand?
130.4: But with You is forgiveness, so that You may be feared.
130.5: I wait for YHWH; my soul waits, and in His word I have hoped.
130.6: My soul is for the Lord, more than those watching for the morning, watching for the morning.
130.7: Look with hope, O Israel, unto YHWH! For with YHWH is loving devotion, and redemption is abundant with Him.
130.8: And He will redeem Israel from all his iniquities.

131.1: A Song of the Ascents. Of David. O YHWH, my heart is not lifted high, and my eyes are not raised up, and neither have I gone about in great things, and in marvelous things beyond me.
131.2: If not, I have stilled and quieted my soul like a weaned child with his mother; my soul is like a weaned child within me.
131.3: Look with hope, O Israel, unto YHWH, from now even to forever!

132.1: A Song of the Ascents. O YHWH, Remember for David, all his afflictions,
132.2: how he swore to YHWH and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
132.3: “If I enter into the tent of my house, or if go up on the couch of my bed,
132.4: I will not give sleep to my eyes, and slumber to my eyelids,
132.5: until I find a place for YHWH, a dwelling place for the Mighty One of Jacob.”
132.6: Behold, we have heard of it in Ephrathah, we have found it in the fields of Jaar.
132.7: Let us go into His dwelling place; let us worship at the stool of His feet!
132.8: Arise, O YHWH, to Your resting place—You and the ark of Your strength!
132.9: May Your priests be clothed with righteousness, and Your holy ones shout for joy!
132.10: For the sake of David Your servant, do not turn away the face of Your anointed one.
132.11: YHWH has sworn in truth of David; He will not turn back from it: “I will set one from the fruit of your belly on the throne of yours.
132.12: If your sons will keep My covenant and My testimonies that I teach them, their sons also, forever and ever, will sit on the throne of yours.”
132.13: For YHWH has set His choice on Zion; He has desired it as a seat for Himself:
132.14: “This is My resting place forever and ever; here I will sit, for I have desired it.
132.15: I will abundantly bless her provision, and her needy ones I will satisfy with bread;
132.16: and her priests I will clothe with salvation, and her godly ones will shout in joy.
132.17: There I will make to sprout a horn for David; I will arrange a lamp for My anointed one.
132.18: I will clothe his enemies with shame, and upon him his diadem will shine.”

133.1: A Song of the Ascents. Of David. Behold, how good and how pleasant is the dwelling of brothers even together!
133.2: It is good like oil upon the head, running down upon the beard, the beard of Aaron, which is running down upon the mouth of his garments;
133.3: or like the dew of Hermon, which is coming down upon the mountains of Zion. For there YHWH commanded the blessing—life even to forever!

134.1: A Song of the Ascents. Come, bless YHWH, all servants of YHWH, those standing in the house of YHWH by night!
134.2: Lift up your? hands to the holy place, and bless YHWH!
134.3: May YHWH bless you from Zion, Maker of heavens and earth!

135.1: Hallelu YAH! Praise the name of YHWH! Give praise, O servants of YHWH
135.2: who are standing in the house of YHWH, in the courts of the house of our God!
135.3: Praise YAH, for YHWH is good; sing praises to His name, for it is pleasant!
135.4: For YAH has chosen Jacob for Himself, Israel for His possession.
135.5: For I know that YHWH is great, and our Lord is greater than all gods!
135.6: All that YHWH delights in, He does, in the heavens and on the earth, in the seas and all the depths,
135.7: bringing up the clouds from the ends of the earth. He has made lightning for the rain, bringing forth the wind from His storehouses—
135.8: who struck the firstborn of Egypt, from man even to beast.
135.9: He sent forth signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt, unto Pharaoh and all of his servants—
135.10: who struck many nations and killed mighty kings,
135.11: even Sihon king of the Amorite, and Og king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan.
135.12: And He gave their land as an inheritance, an inheritance to Israel His people.
135.13: O YHWH, Your name is forever; YHWH, Your memorial is to generation and generation!
135.14: For YHWH will vindicate His people, and He will have compassion on His servants.
135.15: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of the hands of man.
135.16: Mouths are unto them, and they do not speak; eyes are unto them, and they do not see.
135.17: Ears are unto them and they do not hear; indeed, there is no breath in their mouths.
135.18: Their makers shall come to be like them—everyone who is trusting in them!
135.19: O House of Israel, bless YHWH; O house of Aaron, bless YHWH;
135.20: O house of Levi, bless YHWH; all you fearing YHWH, bless YHWH!
135.21: Blessed be YHWH from Zion, the One dwelling in Jerusalem! Praise YAH!

136.1: Give thanks to YHWH, for He is good, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.2: Give thanks to the God of gods, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.3: Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.4: to the One doing great wonders by Himself alone, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.5: to the One making the heavens with understanding, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.6: to the One spreading out the earth upon the waters, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.7: to the One making great the lights—for His loving devotion is forever—
136.8: the sun for ruling over the day, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.9: the moon and stars for ruling over the night, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.10: to the one striking Egypt, against their firstborn, for His loving devotion is forever.
136.11: And He brought forth Israel from their midst, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.12: with a strong hand and an outstretched arm, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.13: to the One dividing the Red sea into parts, for His loving devotion is forever.
136.14: And He made Israel pass through the midst of it, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.15: and He shook off Pharaoh and his army into the Red Sea, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.16: to the One leading His people through the wilderness, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.17: to the One striking great kings, for His loving devotion is forever.
136.18: And He killed mighty kings, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.19: Sihon king of the Amorite, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.20: and Og king of Bashan, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.21: and He gave their land as an inheritance—for His loving devotion is forever;
136.22: an inheritance to Israel His servant, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.23: who in our lowliness remembered us, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.24: and rescued us from our enemies, for His loving devotion is forever;
136.25: the One giving food to all flesh, for His loving devotion is forever.
136.26: Give thanks to the God of the heavens, for His loving devotion is forever!

137.1: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down and we wept in our remembering Zion.
137.2: Upon the willows in her midst, we hung our harps.
137.3: For there our captors required of us the words of a song, and our tormentors, joy: “Sing to us a song of Zion.”
137.4: How can we sing a song of YHWH in a foreign land?
137.5: If I should forget you, O Jerusalem, may my right hand forget.
137.6: May my tongue cling to my palate, if I do not remember you, if I do not exalt Jerusalem above my chief joy!
137.7: Remember, O YHWH, about the sons of Edom, on the day of Jerusalem—those saying, “Lay her bare! Lay her bare to her foundation!”
137.8: O Daughter of Babylon, O destroyed one, blessed is he who repays to you, dealing with you as you have dealt with us!
137.9: Blessed is he who seizes and dashes your infants against the rock!

138.1: Of David. I give You praise with all of my heart; before the gods I sing Your praises.
138.2: I bow down toward the temple of Your holiness, and I give thanks to Your name for Your loving devotion, and for Your faithfulness; for You have magnified above all Your name and Your word.
138.3: On the day I called out, then You answered me; You embolden me in my soul with strength.
138.4: O YHWH, all the kings of the earth will give You thanks when they hear the words of Your mouth.
138.5: And they will sing of the ways of YHWH: For great is the glory of YHWH!
138.6: For YHWH is on high, and the lowly one He sees, and the haughty He knows from afar.
138.7: When I walk in the midst of distress, You revive me against the anger of my enemies; You stretch out Your hand, and Your right hand saves me.
138.8: May YHWH complete this on my behalf! O YHWH, Your loving devotion is forever; do not forsake the works of Your hands!

139.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. O YHWH, You have searched me and know me .
139.2: You know my sitting down and my rising up; You discern my thought from afar.
139.3: You have fanned My wandering and my lying down, and You are aware of all my ways.
139.4: For there is not a word on my tongue—behold, O YHWH, You have known it all.
139.5: Behind and before, You hem me in, and You have laid Your hand upon me.
139.6: Such knowledge is wonderful for me; it is high, I am not able to atttain it.
139.7: Where can I go from Your Spirit? Or where can I flee from Your face?
139.8: If I ascend to the heavens, there You are ; and if I make my bed in Sheol, behold, You!
139.9: If I lift up the wings of the morning, if I settle in the furthest part of the sea,
139.10: even there Your hand will guide me, and Your right hand will hold me fast.
139.11: And I said, “Surely the darkness will cover me, and the light become night around me”—
139.12: even the darkness is not dark to You, and the night shines like the day—as the darkness, so is the light.
139.13: For You have possessed my kidneys; You covered me in the womb of my mother.
139.14: I give You praise upon that fearfully I have been distinguished; wonderful are Your works, and my soul knows it very well.
139.15: My bone was not hidden from You when I was made in secret, woven together in the depths of earth.
139.16: Your eyes saw my unformed body, and in Your book all of them were written—the days fashioned for me, and not one was yet among them!
139.17: And how precious are Your thoughts to me, O God! How vast is their sum!
139.18: If I could count them, they would number more than the sand! I awaken, and I am still with You.
139.19: If only You would slay, O God, the wicked; and the men of blood, may they turn aside from me—
139.20: who speak against You for wickedness; Your enemies are lifted up to vanity.
139.21: Do I not hate those hating you, O YHWH, and I loathe those standing against You?
139.22: I have hated them with perfect hatred; they have become as enemies to me!
139.23: Search me, O God, and know my heart; test me, and know my thoughts.
139.24: And see if a way of wickedness is in me, and lead me in the way everlasting.

140.1: For the one directing. A Psalm of David. Rescue me, O YHWH, from the evil man; from the man of violence guard me—
140.2: those who devise evil in the heart, and all the day they stir up wars.
140.3: They sharpen their tongues like a snake; the venom of a viper is under their lips. Selah
140.4: keep me, O YHWH, from the hands of the wicked, from the man of violence guard me—those who have devised to push down my steps.
140.5: The prideful ones have hidden a snare for me, and ropes they have spread as a net by the side of the path; lures they have set for me. Selah
140.6: I said, “O YHWH, You are my God;” give ear to, O YHWH, the voice of my supplications!
140.7: YHWH my Lord, the strength of my salvation, You have covered my head on the day of armor.
140.8: Do not grant, O YHWH, the desires of the wicked; do not promote his wicked device; lest they be exalted! Selah
140.9: the poison of those surrounding me, the trouble of their lips, let it cover them!
140.10: Let burning coals fall upon them; let them be cast into the fire, into deep pits, never to rise up again!
140.11: A man of tongues, do not let him be established in the earth; a man of violence, may evil hunt him to overflowing!
140.12: I know that YHWH upholds justice for the poor, and judgment for the needy.
140.13: Indeed, the righteous will give praise to Your name; the upright will dwell with Your face.

141.1: A Psalm of David. O YHWH, I call to You—hurry to me; give ear to my voice in my calling out to You!
141.2: Let my prayer be established like incense before Your face, and the lifting of my hands like the offering of the evening.
141.3: Set, O YHWH, a guard for my mouth; keep watch over the door of my lips.
141.4: Do not make my heart bend to an evil thing, to do works in wickedness, with men working iniquity; never shall I eat of their delicacies!
141.5: Let a righteous one strike me, and let him rebuke me in loving devotion; it is oil for my head—let not my head refuse it ! For still my prayer is against their evil deeds.
141.6: Let their judges be released into the hands of the rock, and let them hear my words, for they are pleasant!
141.7: Like the tilling and ripping up of the land, our bones are scattered at the mouth of Sheol.
141.8: Yet unto You, O YHWH, O Lord, are my eyes; in You I take refuge—do not lay bare my soul!
141.9: Keep me from the snares they have laid for me, and from the lures of the workers of iniquity!
141.10: Let the wicked fall into their nets together, until I pass over.

142.1: A Maskil of David, in his being in the cave. A prayer. With my voice to YHWH I cry out; with my voice to YHWH I plead for grace.
142.2: I pour out before Him my complaint; I declare my distress before Him.
142.3: With the weakening of my spirit within me, You indeed know my path; on the road that I walk, they have hidden a snare for me.
142.4: Look to the right and see, and no one attends to me; the place of refuge has perished for me; no one is inquiring for my soul.
142.5: I cried out to You, O YHWH; I said, “You are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living!”
142.6: Attend to my cry, for exceedingly I am brought low! Deliver me from my pursuers, for they are stronger than I.
142.7: Bring forth my soul from prison, for giving praise by me to Your name! Let the righteous ones surround me, for You deal bountifully with me.

143.1: A Psalm of David. O YHWH, hear my prayer; give ear to my supplications! In Your faithfulness, answer me in Your righteousness!
143.2: And do not enter into judgment with Your servant, for not anyone alive is righteous before Your face.
143.3: For the enemy has pursued my soul; he has crushed my life to the ground; he has made me dwell in darkness, like the dead of old.
143.4: So my spirit through me has grown weak, and within me my heart is desolate.
143.5: I remember the days of antiquity; I have meditated on all Your deeds; I muse on the work of Your hands.
143.6: I spread out my hands to You; my soul is like a land exhausted for You. Selah
143.7: Hurry to answer me, O YHWH; my spirit fails! Do not hide Your face from me, or I will be likened with those going down to the Pit.
143.8: Cause me to hear Your loving devotion in the morning, for in You do I trust; make me to know the way in which I should walk, for to You I lift up my soul.
143.9: Deliver me from my enemies, O YHWH; to You I have fled for refuge.
143.10: Teach me to do Your will, for You are my God; May Your good Spirit, lead me into the land of uprightness.
143.11: For the sake of Your name, O YHWH, revive me; in Your righteousness, bring forth my soul from distress.
143.12: And in Your loving devotion, may You cut off my enemies and destroy all afflicting my soul! For I am Your servant.

144.1: Of David. Blessed be YHWH, my Rock, the One training my hands for war, my fingers for battle—
144.2: my steadfast One and my fortress, my tower and my deliverer—for me; my shield in whom I take refuge, the One subduing the peoples under me.
144.3: O YHWH, what is man that You take knowledge of him, the son of man that You think of him?
144.4: Man is like a breath; his days are like a shadow passing by.
144.5: O YHWH, bend Your heavens and come down! Touch the mountains, that they may smoke!
144.6: Flash lightning and scatter them; send forth Your arrows and rout them!
144.7: Stretch out Your hand from on high; release me and deliver me from the great waters, from the hand of sons of a foreigner,
144.8: whose mouth has spoken emptiness, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
144.9: O God, let me sing a new song to You; on a harp of ten strings I will make music to You—
144.10: the One giving salvation to kings, the One releasing David His servant from the sword of evil.
144.11: May You release me and snatch me from the hand of the sons of a foreigner, whose mouth speaks emptiness, and their right hand is a right hand of deception.
144.12: That our sons may be as plants grown up in their youth, our daughters like corner pillars, carved for the pattern of the palace.
144.13: Our granaries full, bringing out from kind to kind, our flocks producing thousands, being myriads in our fields,
144.14: our oxen carrying. Let there be no breach, and no going forth, and no an outcry in our wide streets.
144.15: Blessed are the people for whom is thus; blessed are the people for whom YHWH is God!

145.1: A Psalm of David. I will lift You high, my God, the King, and I will bless Your name forever and ever!
145.2: With every day, I will bless You, and I will praise Your name, forever and ever!
145.3: Great is YHWH and to be praised greatly, and of His greatness there is no searching out.
145.4: Generation to generation will commend Your works, and Your mighty acts they will declare.
145.5: The splendor of the glory of Your majesty, and words of Your wonders I will put forth.
145.6: And the strength of Your fearful acts they will speak, and Your greatness I will recount.
145.7: The memory of the abundance of Your goodness they will pour forth, and Your righteousness they will sing out.
145.8: Gracious and compassionate is YHWH, slow to anger and great in loving devotion.
145.9: Good is YHWH to all, and His mercies are upon all His works.
145.10: O YHWH, all Your works will give You thanks, and Your holy ones will bless You.
145.11: The glory of Your kingdom they will speak, and Your might they will declare,
145.12: to make known to the sons of man His mighty deeds , and the glory of the majesty of His kingdom.
145.13: Your kingdom is a kingdom of all ages, and Your dominion is in every generation and generation. Yahweh is faithful in all His words and kind in all His actions.
145.14: YHWH is upholding all those falling, and raising up all those bent down low.
145.15: The eyes of all wait for You, and You give to them their food in its time.
145.16: You open Your hand, and satisfy the desire of every living thing.
145.17: Righteous is YHWH in all His ways, and kind in all His works.
145.18: Near is YHWH to all those calling on Him, to all who call on Him in truth.
145.19: He does the desire of those who fear Him, and their cry for help He hears, and He saves them.
145.20: YHWH is guarding all those loving Him, and all the wicked He will destroy.
145.21: My mouth will declare the praise of YHWH, and all flesh blesses the name of His holiness, forever and ever!

146.1: Hallelu YAH! Praise, O my soul, YHWH!
146.2: I will praise YHWH during my life; I will sing praises to my God while I exist.
146.3: Do not put your? trust in princes—in a son of man, for in him is no deliverance.
146.4: His spirit goes forth, he returns to his earth; on that day his plans perish.
146.5: Blessed is he whose help is the God of Jacob; his hope is upon YHWH his God,
146.6: maker of the heavens and the earth, the sea and all that is in them, the One guarding truth forever,
146.7: doing justice for the oppressed, giving bread to the hungry. YHWH is the One freeing those who are bound;
146.8: YHWH opens the eyes of the blind; YHWH raises up those bent down; YHWH loves the righteous ones;
146.9: YHWH guards the sojourners; the fatherless and widow He sustains, and the way of the wicked He overturns.
146.10: YHWH reigns forever—Your God, O Zion, from generation and generation! Praise YAH!

147.1: Hallelu YAH! For it is good to praise our God—how pleasant and beautiful is praise!
147.2: The One building up Jerusalem is YHWH, and He gathers together the exiles of Israel,
147.3: giving healing to the broken of heart, and binding up their wounds,
147.4: Appointing the number of the stars—for all of them He calls by names.
147.5: Great is our Lord, and abundant in power; of His understanding there is no numbering!
147.6: YHWH restores the humble; He casts down the wicked to the ground.
147.7: Sing to YHWH with thanksgiving; make music to our God on the harp,
147.8: to the One covering the heavens with clouds, the One preparing rain for the earth, the One making grass sprout on the hills,
147.9: giving to the beast its food, and to the young of the ravens that call out.
147.10: He does not delight in the strength of the horse, and not in the legs of the man does He take pleasure.
147.11: YHWH is pleased with those who fear him, those hoping in His loving devotion.
147.12: Exalt, O Jerusalem, YHWH; praise your God, O Zion!
147.13: For He has strengthened the bars of your gates; He has blessed your sons within you.
147.14: The One making peace for your border, He satisfies you with the fat of wheat.
147.15: The One sending forth His utterance to the earth, how swiftly His word runs!
147.16: The One giving snow like wool, He scatters the frost like ashes,
147.17: casting forth His hail like morsels; before the face of His cold, who can stand?
147.18: He sends out His word and melts them; His wind he makes blow, and the waters flow.
147.19: He declares His words to Jacob, His statutes and His judgments to Israel.
147.20: He has not done so for any nation; and His judgments they have not known. Praise YAH!

148.1: Hallelu YAH! Praise YHWH from the heavens; praise Him in the heights!
148.2: Praise Him, all His angels; praise Him, all His hosts!
148.3: Praise Him, O sun and moon; praise Him, all stars of light!
148.4: Praise Him, O heavens of heavens, and waters that are above the heavens!
148.5: Let them praise the name of YHWH, for He commanded, and they were created.
148.6: And He established them for ever to forever; He made a decree, and it shall not pass away.
148.7: Praise YHWH from the earth, sea creatures and all the depths;
148.8: fire and hail, snow and clouds, wind of storm—doing His word;
148.9: mountains and all hills, trees of fruit and all cedars;
148.10: the beast and all cattle, the creeping thing and bird of wing;
148.11: kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all judges of the earth;
148.12: young men and also maidens, old with young:
148.13: Let them praise the name of YHWH, for His name alone is exalted; His splendor above the earth and the heavens!
148.14: For He has raised up the horn of His people, the praise from all His saints, from the sons of Israel, a people near to Him. Praise YAH!

149.1: Hallelu YAH! Sing to YHWH a new song—His praise in the assembly of the holy ones!
149.2: Let Israel rejoice in his Maker; let the sons of Zion rejoice in their King!
149.3: Let them praise His name with dancing; with tambourine and harp let them make music to Him!
149.4: For YHWH delights in His people; He adorns the humble with salvation.
149.5: Let the holy ones exult in His glory; let them sing for joy upon their beds.
149.6: Let the high praises of God be in their throat, and a two-edged sword in their hand,
149.7: to execute vengeance on the nations, and punishments against the peoples,
149.8: to bind their kings with chains, and their nobles with shackles of iron,
149.9: to execute against them the judgment written—it is an honor for all His holy ones. Praise YAH!

150.1: Hallelu YAH! Praise God in His sanctuary; praise Him in His mighty expanse!
150.2: Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him according to the abundance of His greatness!
150.3: Praise Him with the blast of the shofar; praise Him with the harp and lyre!
150.4: Praise Him with tambourine and dancing; praise Him with strings and flute!
150.5: Praise Him with cymbals sounding; praise Him with cymbals shouting!
150.6: Let everything that has breath praise YAH! Praise YAH!
